── Public Member Functions ──
OmneEngineSpace::OmneTimerCB — Public Member Functions:
virtual int  call ( tsNCharcb *pTimerName, void *pContext, int *aiCode)=0
virtual  ~OmneTimerCB ()

── Detailed Description ──
Class OmneEngineSpace::OmneTimerCB: Timer routine class used by the RApi .
An OmneTimerCB is a callback routine fired for RApi timer events.
See also: REngine::addTimer | REngine::removeTimer | REngine::startTimer | REngine::stopTimer

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ ~OmneTimerCB  [inline, virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: virtual OmneEngineSpace::OmneTimerCB::~OmneTimerCB() [inline, virtual]

── Member Function Documentation ──

▶ call  [pure virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: int OmneEngineSpace::OmneTimerCB::call( tsNCharcb * pTimerName , void * pContext , int * aiCode ) [pure virtual]

▶ call  [pure virtual]  (text)
The timer name is the one returned from REngine::addTimer(). The pContext is the one passed to REngine::addTimer(). The return value and aiCode is ignored.

▶ call  [pure virtual]  (see_also)
See also (call): REngine::addTimer

RApi::AccountInfo Class Reference
#include <[RApiPlus.h](RApiPlus_8h_source.html)>
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

── Public Member Functions ──
RApi::AccountInfo — Public Member Functions:
AccountInfo ()
int  clearHandles (int *aiCode)
int  dump (int *aiCode)
virtual  ~AccountInfo ()

── Public Attributes ──
RApi::AccountInfo — Public Attributes:
int  iCreationSsboe
int  iCreationUsecs
AutoLiquidateInfo *  pAutoLiquidateInfo
RmsInfo *  pRmsInfo
tsNCharcb  sAccountId
tsNCharcb  sAccountName
tsNCharcb  sFcmId
tsNCharcb  sIbId

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::AccountInfo: This class represents an account.
The AccountInfo class is used to pass account information to/from RApi . The strings in the class represent a unique account on which a logged in user can trade.

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ AccountInfo  (prototype)
Prototype: AccountInfo::AccountInfo()

▶ AccountInfo  (text)
This constructor initializes all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to NULL and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to zero. Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Pointers to arrays are initialized to NULL, and their lengths are set to zero.

▶ ~AccountInfo  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: AccountInfo::~AccountInfo() [virtual]

▶ ~AccountInfo  [virtual]  (text)
The destructor does not deallocate any memory pointed to by member tsNCharcbs.

── Member Function Documentation ──

▶ clearHandles  (prototype)
Prototype: int AccountInfo::clearHandles( int * aiCode)

▶ clearHandles  (text)
This routine initializes all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to NULL and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to zero. Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Boolean members are set to false. Pointer values are set to NULL. (Internally used by the constructor.)

▶ dump  (prototype)
Prototype: int AccountInfo::dump( int * aiCode)

▶ dump  (text)
This routine prints the value of each public member to stdout. If a tsNCharcb has an iDataLen value of zero and pData of NULL, the string "<empty>" will be printed.

── Member Data Documentation ──

▶ iCreationSsboe  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::AccountInfo::iCreationSsboe

▶ iCreationSsboe  (text)
Creation time of this account, expressed in whole seconds since the beginning of the epoch (unix time).

▶ iCreationUsecs  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::AccountInfo::iCreationUsecs

▶ iCreationUsecs  (text)
Microsecond portion of the creation time of this account.

▶ pAutoLiquidateInfo  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::AccountInfo::pAutoLiquidateInfo

▶ pAutoLiquidateInfo  (code)
```
Auto-Liquidate current values associated with this Account.  This 
 value will only be non-null in RCallbacks::AccountList().  For other
 callbacks, such as order or pnl related callbacks, this value will
 be null.  When specifying an AccountInfo instance to the REngine as a
 parameter, this value does not need to be set.
```

▶ pRmsInfo  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::AccountInfo::pRmsInfo

▶ pRmsInfo  (text)
Risk management settings associated with this Account. This pointer will only be non-null in RCallbacks::AccountList() . Typically, for order-related callbacks, this pointer will be null. When specifying an AccountInfo instance to the REngine as a parameter, this value does not need to be set.

▶ sAccountId  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::AccountInfo::sAccountId

▶ sAccountId  (text)
The account id.

▶ sAccountName  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::AccountInfo::sAccountName

▶ sAccountName  (text)
The account name. This value may be empty unless the AccountInfo instance is from RCallbacks::AccountList() . When specifying an AccountInfo to the REngine for order or pnl related methods, this property can be empty.

▶ sFcmId  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::AccountInfo::sFcmId

▶ sFcmId  (text)
The FCM (futures commission merchant) associated with this account.

▶ sIbId  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::AccountInfo::sIbId

▶ sIbId  (text)
The IB (introducing broker) associated with this account.

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::AccountListInfo: An instance of this class represents a list of accounts.
An AccountListInfo object is used to pass an array of AccountInfo via RCallbacks::AccountList() .
See also: REngine::getAccounts()

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ AccountListInfo  (prototype)
Prototype: AccountListInfo::AccountListInfo()

▶ AccountListInfo  (text)
This constructor initializes all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to NULL and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to zero. Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Boolean members are set to false. Pointers (and pointers to arrays) are set to NULL.

▶ ~AccountListInfo  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: AccountListInfo::~AccountListInfo() [virtual]

▶ ~AccountListInfo  [virtual]  (text)
This destructor will not deallocate memory associated with internal pointers such as the pData of any tsNCharcbs or array elements.

── Member Function Documentation ──

▶ dump  (prototype)
Prototype: int AccountListInfo::dump( int * aiCode)

▶ dump  (text)
This routine prints the values of the AccountListInfo object to stdout. If a tsNCharcb has an iDataLen value of zero, then the string "<empty>" will be printed.

── Member Data Documentation ──

▶ asAccountInfoArray  (prototype)
Prototype: AccountListInfo::asAccountInfoArray

▶ asAccountInfoArray  (text)
This is an array of AccountInfo objects. The array length is specified by AccountListInfo::iArrayLen .

▶ iArrayLen  (prototype)
Prototype: AccountListInfo::iArrayLen

▶ iArrayLen  (text)
This variable specifies the array length.

▶ iRpCode  (prototype)
Prototype: AccountListInfo::iRpCode

▶ iRpCode  (text)
The response code, if available, associated with the request REngine::getAccounts() . A non-zero response code can be interpreted as an error.

▶ sRpCode  (prototype)
Prototype: AccountListInfo::sRpCode

▶ sRpCode  (text)
This is a string equivalent of the response code. It may not be populated if the value of iRpCode is zero (API_OK).

RApi::AccountUpdateInfo Class Reference
#include <[RApiPlus.h](RApiPlus_8h_source.html)>

── Public Member Functions ──

── Public Attributes ──
RApi::AccountUpdateInfo — Public Attributes:
AccountInfo  oAccount
tsNCharcb  sAction
tsNCharcb  sUser

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::AccountUpdateInfo: An instance of this class represents a change to an account.
The AccountUpdateInfo class conveys that some update has happened to the account specified by AccountUpdateInfo::oAccount . The nature of the update is indicated by what action ( AccountUpdateInfo::sAction ) has occurred.
See also: RCallbacks::AccountUpdate()

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ AccountUpdateInfo  (prototype)
Prototype: AccountUpdateInfo::AccountUpdateInfo()

▶ AccountUpdateInfo  (text)
This constructor initializes all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to NULL and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to zero. Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Boolean members are set to false. Pointers (and pointers to arrays) are set to NULL.

▶ ~AccountUpdateInfo  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: AccountUpdateInfo::~AccountUpdateInfo() [virtual]

▶ ~AccountUpdateInfo  [virtual]  (text)
This destructor will not deallocate memory associated with internal pointers such as the pData of any tsNCharcbs or array elements.

── Member Function Documentation ──

▶ dump  (prototype)
Prototype: int AccountUpdateInfo::dump( int * aiCode)

▶ dump  (text)
This routine prints the values of the AccountUpdateInfo object to stdout. If a tsNCharcb has an iDataLen value of zero or a pData of NULL, then the string "<empty>" will be printed.

── Member Data Documentation ──

▶ oAccount  (prototype)
Prototype: AccountUpdateInfo::oAccount

▶ oAccount  (text)
Account on which the action occurred.

▶ sAction  (prototype)
Prototype: AccountUpdateInfo::sAction

▶ sAction  (text)
Action representing the nature of the change. See below for the list of possible actions. Notification of adding and modifying accounts is only supported when the currently logged in user is an FCM or IB administrator.

▶ sAction  (see_also)
See also (sAction): RApi::sADD_ACCOUNT | RApi::sMODIFY_ACCOUNT | RApi::sASSIGN_ACCOUNT_TO_USER | RApi::sREMOVE_ACCOUNT_FROM_USER

▶ sUser  (prototype)
Prototype: AccountUpdateInfo::sUser

▶ sUser  (text)
User to which this update pertains, if specified/applicable. Normally this will be when accounts are assigned to or removed from a user.

▶ sUser  (see_also)
See also (sUser): RApi::sASSIGN_ACCOUNT_TO_USER | RApi::sREMOVE_ACCOUNT_FROM_USER

RApi::AdmCallbacks Class Reference abstract
#include <[RApiPlus.h](RApiPlus_8h_source.html)>
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

── Public Member Functions ──
RApi::AdmCallbacks — Public Member Functions:
AdmCallbacks ()
virtual int  Alert ( AlertInfo *pInfo, void *pContext, int *aiCode)=0
virtual int  Environment ( EnvironmentInfo *pInfo, void *pContext, int *aiCode)
virtual int  EnvironmentList ( EnvironmentListInfo *pInfo, void *pContext, int *aiCode)
virtual  ~AdmCallbacks ()

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::AdmCallbacks: This abstract class contains all the admin-related callbacks for RApi .
A subclass of AdmCallbacks can be defined, instantiated and passed via REngineParams::pCallbacks when logging into the infrastructure. While the REngine instance is alive, the AdmCallbacks instance referenced by REngineParams::pCallbacks must not be destroyed.

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ AdmCallbacks  (prototype)
Prototype: AdmCallbacks::AdmCallbacks()

▶ ~AdmCallbacks  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: AdmCallbacks::~AdmCallbacks() [virtual]

── Member Function Documentation ──

▶ Alert  [pure virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: int AdmCallbacks::Alert( AlertInfo * pInfo , void * pContext , int * aiCode ) [pure virtual]

▶ Alert  [pure virtual]  (text)
This method is invoked when the engine detects an alert condition.

▶ Alert  [pure virtual]  (params)
Parameters of Alert:
  • pInfo: the AlertInfo object that contains information about the alert.
  • pContext: the global context pointer provided by either REngineParams::pContext or set via REngine::setContext . If this value has not been set, its value will be NULL.
  • aiCode: the error code set by the method if it failed.
Returns: OK if the method was successful, NOT_OK otherwise. In the latter case aiCode is populated with the error code.

▶ Alert  [pure virtual]  (see_also)
See also (Alert): AlertInfo

▶ Environment  [inline, virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: int AdmCallbacks::Environment( EnvironmentInfo * pInfo , void * pContext , int * aiCode ) [inline, virtual]

▶ Environment  [inline, virtual]  (text)
Returns the EnvironmentVariables in the Environment returned by a request via REngine::getEnvironment() .

▶ Environment  [inline, virtual]  (params)
Parameters of Environment:
  • pInfo: EnvironmentInfo object listing the EnvironmentVariables in this environment
  • pContext: the global context pointer provided by either REngineParams::pContext or set via REngine::setContext . If this value has not been set, its value will be NULL.
  • aiCode: the error code set by the method if it failed.
Returns: OK if the method was successful, NOT_OK otherwise. In the latter case aiCode is populated with the error code.

▶ Environment  [inline, virtual]  (see_also)
See also (Environment): EnvironmentInfo | REngine::getEnvironment()

▶ EnvironmentList  [inline, virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: int AdmCallbacks::EnvironmentList( EnvironmentListInfo * pInfo , void * pContext , int * aiCode ) [inline, virtual]

▶ EnvironmentList  [inline, virtual]  (text)
Returns the list of environments returned by a request via REngine::listEnvironments() .

▶ EnvironmentList  [inline, virtual]  (params)
Parameters of EnvironmentList:
  • pInfo: EnvironmentListInfo object listing the environment keys
  • pContext: the global context pointer provided by either REngineParams::pContext or set via REngine::setContext . If this value has not been set, its value will be NULL.
  • aiCode: the error code set by the method if it failed.
Returns: OK if the method was successful, NOT_OK otherwise. In the latter case aiCode is populated with the error code.

▶ EnvironmentList  [inline, virtual]  (see_also)
See also (EnvironmentList): EnvironmentListInfo | REngine::listEnvironments()

RApi::AggregatorInfo Class Reference
#include <[RApiPlus.h](RApiPlus_8h_source.html)>
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

── Public Member Functions ──
RApi::AggregatorInfo — Public Member Functions:
AggregatorInfo ()
int  dump (int *aiCode)
virtual  ~AggregatorInfo ()

── Public Attributes ──
RApi::AggregatorInfo — Public Attributes:
int  iRpCode

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::AggregatorInfo: Returns information about whether the market data connection is to an aggregated ticker plant.
See also: REngine::isThereAnAggregator() | RCallbacks::Aggregator()

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ AggregatorInfo  (prototype)
Prototype: AggregatorInfo::AggregatorInfo()

▶ AggregatorInfo  (text)
This constructor initializes all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to NULL and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to zero. Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Boolean members are set to false. Pointers (and pointers to arrays) are set to NULL.

▶ ~AggregatorInfo  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: AggregatorInfo::~AggregatorInfo() [virtual]

▶ ~AggregatorInfo  [virtual]  (text)
This destructor will not deallocate memory associated with internal pointers such as the pData of any tsNCharcbs or array elements.

── Member Function Documentation ──

▶ dump  (prototype)
Prototype: int AggregatorInfo::dump( int * aiCode)

▶ dump  (text)
This routine prints instance information to stdout. If a tsNCharcb has an iDataLen value of zero, then the string "<empty>" will be printed.

── Member Data Documentation ──

▶ iRpCode  (prototype)
Prototype: int AggregatorInfo::iRpCode

▶ iRpCode  (text)
Response code resulting from a call to REngine::isThereAnAggregator() . A response code of zero (API_OK) indicates that there is an aggregator. A response code of 14 (API_UNKNOWN_REQUEST) indicates that there is no aggregator. A response code of 6 (API_BAD_INPUT) indicates that the market data connect point does not actually connect to a ticker plant. Any other response code reflects an unexpected error condition.

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::AgreementInfo: This class represents an agreement.
The AgreementInfo class is used to convey agreement meta-data. One can request agreement information by calling REngine::listAgreements() .
Please read the FAQ on agreements for more information : FAQ - Agreements
See also: REngine::listAgreements | RCallbacks::AgreementList | AgreementListInfo | AgreementInfo

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ AgreementInfo  (prototype)
Prototype: AgreementInfo::AgreementInfo()

▶ AgreementInfo  (text)
This constructor initializes all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to NULL and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to zero. Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Boolean members are set to false.

▶ ~AgreementInfo  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: AgreementInfo::~AgreementInfo() [virtual]

▶ ~AgreementInfo  [virtual]  (text)
This destructor will not deallocate memory associated with internal pointers such as the pData of any tsNCharcbs.

── Member Function Documentation ──

▶ dump  (prototype)
Prototype: int AgreementInfo::dump( int * aiCode)

▶ dump  (text)
This routine will print the current values of the object to stdout.

── Member Data Documentation ──

▶ bMandatory  (prototype)
Prototype: AgreementInfo::bMandatory

▶ bMandatory  (text)
Indicates whether acceptance of this agreement is mandatory for access to the trading platform.

▶ iEndUserAcceptanceSsboe  (prototype)
Prototype: AgreementInfo::iEndUserAcceptanceSsboe

▶ iEndUserAcceptanceSsboe  (text)
Time, in seconds since the beginning of the epoch, of when the agreement was accepted by the end user.

▶ iInForceSsboe  (prototype)
Prototype: AgreementInfo::iInForceSsboe

▶ iInForceSsboe  (text)
Time, in seconds since the beginning of the epoch, by when the agreement must be accepted.

▶ iPublicationSsboe  (prototype)
Prototype: AgreementInfo::iPublicationSsboe

▶ iPublicationSsboe  (text)
Time, in seconds since the beginning of the epoch, of the publication date of the agreement.

▶ sEndUserAcceptanceStatus  (prototype)
Prototype: AgreementInfo::sEndUserAcceptanceStatus

▶ sEndUserAcceptanceStatus  (text)
Status of the end user's acceptance.

▶ sMarketDataUsageCapacity  (prototype)
Prototype: AgreementInfo::sMarketDataUsageCapacity

▶ sMarketDataUsageCapacity  (text)
If available, the market data usage capacity indicated by the end user when the agreement was submitted or accepted.

▶ sStatus  (prototype)
Prototype: AgreementInfo::sStatus

▶ sStatus  (text)
Status of the agreement itself. Currently this property will have values of either "active" or "inactive". Agreements with inactive statuses are retained for historical purposes. Please note that this agreement status is separate and distinct from the status of the end user's acceptance ( AgreementInfo::sEndUserAcceptanceStatus ).

▶ sTitle  (prototype)
Prototype: AgreementInfo::sTitle

▶ sTitle  (text)
Title of the agreement document.

RApi::AgreementListInfo Class Reference
#include <[RApiPlus.h](RApiPlus_8h_source.html)>
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

── Public Member Functions ──
RApi::AgreementListInfo — Public Member Functions:
AgreementListInfo ()
int  dump (int *aiCode)
virtual  ~AgreementListInfo ()

── Public Attributes ──
RApi::AgreementListInfo — Public Attributes:
AgreementInfo *  asAgreementInfoArray
bool  bAccepted
int  iArrayLen
int  iRpCode
void *  pContext
tsNCharcb  sRpCode

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::AgreementListInfo: An instance of this class represents a collection of agreements.
An AgreementListInfo object is passed via RCallbacks::AgreementList() , and conveys the results of a call to REngine::listAgreements() . It returns the initial parameters, a response code indicating whether or not an error has occurred, and, if applicable, an array of AgreementInfo instances.
Please read the FAQ on agreements for more information : FAQ - Agreements
See also: REngine::listAgreements | RCallbacks::AgreementList | AgreementListInfo | AgreementInfo

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ AgreementListInfo  (prototype)
Prototype: AgreementListInfo::AgreementListInfo()

▶ AgreementListInfo  (text)
This constructor initializes all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to NULL and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to zero. Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Boolean members are set to false. Pointers (and pointers to arrays) are set to NULL.

▶ ~AgreementListInfo  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: AgreementListInfo::~AgreementListInfo() [virtual]

▶ ~AgreementListInfo  [virtual]  (text)
This destructor will not deallocate memory associated with internal pointers such as the pData of any tsNCharcbs or array elements.

── Member Function Documentation ──

▶ dump  (prototype)
Prototype: int AgreementListInfo::dump( int * aiCode)

▶ dump  (text)
This routine prints the values of the AgreementListInfo object to stdout. If a tsNCharcb has an iDataLen value of zero, then the string "<empty>" will be printed.

── Member Data Documentation ──

▶ asAgreementInfoArray  (prototype)
Prototype: AgreementListInfo::asAgreementInfoArray

▶ asAgreementInfoArray  (text)
Array of AgreementInfo objects. The array length is specified by AgreementListInfo::iArrayLen .

▶ bAccepted  (prototype)
Prototype: AgreementListInfo::bAccepted

▶ bAccepted  (text)
Returns the value of bAccepted initially passed into REngine::listAgreements() . A value of true indicates that each agreement in the array has been accepted.

▶ iArrayLen  (prototype)
Prototype: AgreementListInfo::iArrayLen

▶ iArrayLen  (text)
Specifies the array length.

▶ iRpCode  (prototype)
Prototype: AgreementListInfo::iRpCode

▶ iRpCode  (text)
Response code, if available, associated with the request REngine::listAgreements() . A non-zero response code can be interpreted as an error.

▶ pContext  (prototype)
Prototype: AgreementListInfo::pContext

▶ pContext  (text)
Returns the value of pContext passed into REngine::listAgreements() .

▶ sRpCode  (prototype)
Prototype: AgreementListInfo::sRpCode

▶ sRpCode  (text)
String equivalent of the response code AgreementListInfo::iRpCode . It may not be populated if the value of iRpCode is zero (API_OK).

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::AlertInfo: This class represents a system alert.
The AlertInfo class is used to pass alert information via
RCallbacks::Alert() . Alerts typically convey information about the system infrastructure, rather than data content. It is possible that one or more of members of AlertInfo may not be populated.

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ AlertInfo  (prototype)
Prototype: AlertInfo::AlertInfo()

▶ AlertInfo  (text)
This constructor initializes all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to MNM_EMPTY_DATA_ITEM and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to MNM_EMPTY_DATA_ITEM_LEN. Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Pointers to arrays are initialized to NULL, and their lengths are set to zero.

▶ ~AlertInfo  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: AlertInfo::~AlertInfo() [virtual]

▶ ~AlertInfo  [virtual]  (text)
The destructor does not deallocate any memory pointed to by member tsNCharcbs.

── Member Function Documentation ──

▶ dump  (prototype)
Prototype: int AlertInfo::dump( int * aiCode)

▶ dump  (text)
This routine prints the value of each public member to stdout. If a tsNCharcb has an iDataLen value of MNM_EMPTY_DATA_ITEM_LEN and pData of MNM_EMPTY_DATA_ITEM, the string "<empty>" will be printed.

── Member Data Documentation ──

▶ iAlertType  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::AlertInfo::iAlertType

▶ iAlertType  (text)
A numeric type of the alert. The possible values are defined in RApi.h and begin with the ALERT_ prefix. Possible values are listed below.

▶ iAlertType  (see_also)
See also (iAlertType): ALERT_CONNECTION_OPENED | ALERT_CONNECTION_CLOSED | ALERT_CONNECTION_BROKEN | ALERT_LOGIN_COMPLETE | ALERT_LOGIN_FAILED | ALERT_SERVICE_ERROR | ALERT_FORCED_LOGOUT | ALERT_QUIET_HEARTBEAT | ALERT_TRADING_DISABLED | ALERT_TRADING_ENABLED | ALERT_SHUTDOWN_SIGNAL

▶ iConnectionId  (prototype)

▶ iConnectionId  (text)

▶ iConnectionId  (see_also)
See also (iConnectionId): MARKET_DATA_CONNECTION_ID | TRADING_SYSTEM_CONNECTION_ID | PNL_CONNECTION_ID | INTRADAY_HISTORY_CONNECTION_ID

▶ iRpCode  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::AlertInfo::iRpCode

▶ iRpCode  (text)
The response code, if available, associated with the alert. A non-zero response code can be interpreted as an error.

▶ sExchange  (prototype)

▶ sExchange  (text)
The exchange abbreviation, if available, pertaining to this Alert.

▶ sMessage  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::AlertInfo::sMessage

▶ sMessage  (text)
A description of the alert condition.

▶ sRpCode  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::AlertInfo::sRpCode

▶ sRpCode  (text)
The is a string equivalent of the response code. It may not be populated if the value of iRpCode is zero (API_OK).

▶ sTicker  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::AlertInfo::sTicker

▶ sTicker  (text)
The ticker symbol, if available, pertaining to this Alert.

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::AskInfo: This class represents an ask quote.
The AskInfo class is used to pass quote information received based on REngine::subscribe() requests via the RCallbacks::AskQuote() and RCallbacks::BestAskQuote callbacks.

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ AskInfo  (prototype)
Prototype: AskInfo::AskInfo()

▶ AskInfo  (text)
This constructor initializes all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to NULL and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to zero. Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Boolean members are set to false.

▶ ~AskInfo  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: AskInfo::~AskInfo() [virtual]

▶ ~AskInfo  [virtual]  (text)
This destructor will not deallocate memory associated with internal pointers such as the pData of any tsNCharcbs.

── Member Function Documentation ──

▶ dump  (prototype)
Prototype: int AskInfo::dump( int * aiCode)

▶ dump  (text)
This routine will print the current values of the object to stdout.

── Member Data Documentation ──

▶ bLeanPriceFlag  (prototype)
Prototype: AskInfo::bLeanPriceFlag

▶ bLeanPriceFlag  (text)
Indicates whether dLeanPrice is defined.

▶ bPriceFlag  (prototype)
Prototype: AskInfo::bPriceFlag

▶ bPriceFlag  (text)
This member variable specifies if AskInfo::dPrice is undefined. If the value is true, then the price is defined. If the value is false, the price is undefined. It is possible that a price is undefined if an order book becomes corrupt or invalid.

▶ bSizeFlag  (prototype)
Prototype: AskInfo::bSizeFlag

▶ bSizeFlag  (text)
This member variable specifies if AskInfo::llSize is undefined. A value of true indicates that the value is defined. A value of false indicates that the value is undefined.

▶ dLeanPrice  (prototype)
Prototype: AskInfo::dLeanPrice

▶ dLeanPrice  (text)
If available, specifies the lean price of the inside of the book. The lean price applies to best bid/ask updates, and indicates where the price is 'leaning' within the spread.

▶ dPrice  (prototype)
Prototype: AskInfo::dPrice

▶ dPrice  (text)
This member variable specifies the price for the quote.

▶ iConnId  (prototype)
Prototype: AskInfo::iConnId

▶ iConnId  (text)
Identifies the connection on which this data was received. It is possible when using the R | Diamond Cutter API, that certain data can be received twice, once from the regular infrastructure, and once from the R | Diamond Cutter infrastructure. This indicator provides a mechanism to distinguish between the two.

▶ iConnId  (see_also)
See also (iConnId): MARKET_DATA_CONNECTION_ID | DIAMOND_CUTTER_MARKET_DATA_CONNECTION_ID

▶ iNumOrders  (prototype)
Prototype: AskInfo::iNumOrders

▶ iNumOrders  (text)
This member variable specifies the number of orders associated with the price/quantity of this quote.

▶ iSsboe  (prototype)
Prototype: AskInfo::iSsboe

▶ iSsboe  (text)
Timestamp in seconds since the beginning of epoch (Jan. 1, 1970). If the callback type is MD_UPDATE_CB, this timestamp indicates the receipt time of this message from the market data provider.
If the image type is MD_IMAGE_CB, the timestamp indicates that the data is current "as of" that time.

▶ iType  (prototype)
Prototype: AskInfo::iType

▶ iType  (text)
This member variable indicates if the quote is from a cache or the real-time data stream.
Possible values for this variable are

▶ iType  (see_also)
See also (iType): MD_IMAGE_CB | MD_UPDATE_CB

▶ iUpdateType  (prototype)
Prototype: AskInfo::iUpdateType

▶ iUpdateType  (text)
This member variable indicates if this update is part of a set of updates to the order book. It should be examined if the callback is RCallbacks::AskQuote() but not for RCallbacks::BestAskQuote() .
Possible values for this variable are :

▶ iUpdateType  (see_also)
See also (iUpdateType): UPDATE_TYPE_UNDEFINED | UPDATE_TYPE_SOLO | UPDATE_TYPE_BEGIN | UPDATE_TYPE_MIDDLE | UPDATE_TYPE_END | UPDATE_TYPE_AGGREGATED

▶ iUsecs  (prototype)
Prototype: AskInfo::iUsecs

▶ iUsecs  (text)
Microsecond component of the iSsboe timestamp

▶ llImpliedSize  (prototype)
Prototype: AskInfo::llImpliedSize

▶ llImpliedSize  (text)
If available, this member variable specifies the implied quantity associated with the price for the quote. Subtracting this amount from AskInfo::llSize yields the outright quantity for the price level. A value of zero indicates that the implied size is not available/defined or that it is actually zero.

▶ llSize  (prototype)
Prototype: AskInfo::llSize

▶ llSize  (text)
This member variable specifies the quantity associated with the price for the quote.

▶ sExchange  (prototype)
Prototype: AskInfo::sExchange

▶ sExchange  (text)
This member variable contains the exchange corresponding to the quote information.

▶ sTicker  (prototype)
Prototype: AskInfo::sTicker

▶ sTicker  (text)
This member variable contains the symbol corresponding to the quote information.

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::AuxRefDataInfo: This class represents auxiliary reference data for an instrument.
The AuxRefDataInfo class is used to pass auxiliary reference data resulting from calls to REngine::getAuxRefData() or REngine::subscribe() where the flags include RApi::MD_REF_DATA . The data is passed via the callback RCallbacks::AuxRefData() . Please note that this auxiliary reference data is seperate and distinct from RefDataInfo and PriceIncrInfo .

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ AuxRefDataInfo  (prototype)
Prototype: AuxRefDataInfo::AuxRefDataInfo()

▶ AuxRefDataInfo  (text)
This constructor initializes all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to NULL and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to zero. Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Boolean members are set to false.

▶ ~AuxRefDataInfo  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: AuxRefDataInfo::~AuxRefDataInfo() [virtual]

▶ ~AuxRefDataInfo  [virtual]  (text)
This destructor will not deallocate memory associated with internal pointers such as the pData of any tsNCharcbs.

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::BarInfo: An instance of this class represents bar/candlestick data.
The BarInfo class holds bar/candlestick data, consisting mainly of open/high/low/close trade prices over a given set of market data trades. There are different types of bars (different ways of aggregating the set of trades), including by time period, price range, volume traded, and number of trades.
One can subscribe to bars from the real-time stream using REngine::subscribeBar() , or replay bars from a historical database, using REngine::replayBars() .
See also: REngine::replayBars() | REngine::subscribeBar()

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

Prototype: BarInfo::BarInfo()

▶ dVwap  (prototype)
Prototype: BarInfo::dVwap

▶ dVwap  (text)
VWAP, if available. If it is available, it is either provided by the exchange or calculated by the infrastructure. This VWAP value will be rounded to the nearest price increment. The time period over which the VWAP is calculated is from the beginning of the trading session.

▶ dVwapLong  (prototype)
Prototype: BarInfo::dVwapLong

▶ dVwapLong  (text)
'long form of VWAP, if available. If it is available, it is either provided by the exchange or calculated by the infrastructure. This VWAP value will not be rounded to the nearest tick. The time period over which the VWAP is calculated is from the beginning of the trading session.

The callback must be associated With the same instance of REngine on which REngine::subscribeBar() or REngine::replayBars() was called. In other words, the context pointer is only supported within an instance of REngine .

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::BarParams: This class encapsulates information needed to request data bars.

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ BarParams  (prototype)
Prototype: BarParams::BarParams()

▶ BarParams  (text)
This constructor initializes all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to NULL and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to zero. Integers and doubles are initialized to zero. Boolean values are set to false. Pointer values are set to NULL. Pointers to arrays are set to NULL and their lengths are set to zero.

▶ ~BarParams  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: BarParams::~BarParams() [virtual]

▶ ~BarParams  [virtual]  (text)
This destructor will not deallocate memory associated with internal pointers such as the pData of any tsNCharcbs.

── Member Data Documentation ──

▶ bCustomSession  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::BarParams::bCustomSession

▶ bCustomSession  (text)
Data bars typically use market mode transitions to determine when a trading session begins and ends. These session events can cause bars to be opened or closed. To configure a custom session time, this flag must be set to true. Custom sessions can only be applied to tick, volume and range bars.
Custom sessions may be ignored for tick bars where the tick size is one.

▶ bCustomSession  (see_also)
See also (bCustomSession): BarParams::bCustomSession | BarParams::iOpenSsm | BarParams::iCloseSsm | BAR_TYPE_RANGE | BAR_TYPE_TICK | BAR_TYPE_VOLUME

▶ bProfile  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::BarParams::bProfile

▶ bProfile  (text)
This flag indicates whether or not to include volume/data profile information for each bar. Currently, only replayed minute bars and multi-tick tick bars support volume/data profile information. This flag is ignored where there is no support. Retrieving volume profile data is generally slower than not retrieving the data.
Volume profile information is the volume traded at each price level within the bar's price range. The volumes are separated into three categories - volumes where there was no aggressor, volumes where the buyer was the aggressor, and finally volumes where the seller was the aggressor.
The number of trades is also included for each of these categories.

▶ bProfile  (see_also)
See also (bProfile): BarInfo::iProfileArrayLen | BarInfo::adProfilePrices | BarInfo::allProfileNoAggressorVolumes | BarInfo::allProfileBuyerAsAggressorVolumes | BarInfo::allProfileSellerAsAggressorVolumes | BarInfo::aiProfileNoAggressorNumTrades | BarInfo::aiProfileBuyerAsAggressorNumTrades | BarInfo::aiProfileSellerAsAggressorNumTrades

▶ dSpecifiedRange  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::BarParams::dSpecifiedRange

▶ dSpecifiedRange  (text)
Configured range of the bar. This value only applies when iType is BAR_TYPE_RANGE. If the bar is truncated due to a session close, the range spanned by the high and low prices may not equal this specified range.

▶ dSpecifiedRange  (see_also)
See also (dSpecifiedRange): BAR_TYPE_RANGE

▶ iCloseSsm  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::BarParams::iCloseSsm

▶ iCloseSsm  (text)
The custom session close time, expressed as seconds-since-midnight, GMT.

▶ iCloseSsm  (see_also)
See also (iCloseSsm): BarParams::bCustomSession | BarParams::iOpenSsm | BarParams::iCloseSsm

▶ iOpenSsm  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::BarParams::iOpenSsm

▶ iOpenSsm  (text)
The custom session open time, expressed as seconds-since-midnight, GMT.

▶ iOpenSsm  (see_also)
See also (iOpenSsm): BarParams::bCustomSession | BarParams::iOpenSsm | BarParams::iCloseSsm

▶ iSpecifiedMinutes  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::BarParams::iSpecifiedMinutes

▶ iSpecifiedMinutes  (text)
Configured period (in minutes) for the bar. This value only applies when iType is BAR_TYPE_MINUTE. If the bar is truncated due to a session close, the actual elapsed time of the trades comprising the bar may not equal the specified period.

▶ iSpecifiedMinutes  (see_also)
See also (iSpecifiedMinutes): BAR_TYPE_MINUTE

▶ iSpecifiedSeconds  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::BarParams::iSpecifiedSeconds

▶ iSpecifiedSeconds  (text)
Configured period (in seconds) for the bar. This value only applies when iType is BAR_TYPE_SECOND. If the bar is truncated due to a session close, the actual elapsed time of the trades comprising the bar may not equal the specified period.

▶ iSpecifiedSeconds  (see_also)
See also (iSpecifiedSeconds): BAR_TYPE_SECOND

▶ iSpecifiedTicks  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::BarParams::iSpecifiedTicks

▶ iSpecifiedTicks  (text)
Configured number of ticks for the bar. This value only applies when iType is BAR_TYPE_TICK. If the bar is truncated due to a session close, the number of ticks may not equal the number of trades.

▶ iSpecifiedTicks  (see_also)
See also (iSpecifiedTicks): BAR_TYPE_TICK

▶ iType  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::BarParams::iType

▶ iType  (code)
```
Type of bar.  Daily and weekly bars do not support custom sessions or
   specified periods (i.e. N days for daily bars or N weeks for weekly 
   bars is not supported).

   Weekly bars are not published in the real time stream, so one cannot 
   subscribe to such bars.

   \see BAR_TYPE_DAILY
   \see BAR_TYPE_MINUTE
   \see BAR_TYPE_RANGE
   \see BAR_TYPE_SECOND
   \see BAR_TYPE_TICK
   \see BAR_TYPE_VOLUME
   \see BAR_TYPE_WEEKLY
```

▶ llSpecifiedVolume  (prototype)

▶ llSpecifiedVolume  (see_also)
See also (llSpecifiedVolume): BAR_TYPE_VOLUME

RApi::BarReplayInfo Class Reference

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::BarReplayInfo: This class demarcates the end of a stream of historical bar data.
The BarReplayInfo class is used to convey the conclusion of the data stream and the response code associated with a call to REngine::replayBars() . When the data stream concludes, RCallbacks::BarReplay() is invoked, passing a BarReplayInfo instance containing the parameter values initially passed into the corresponding call to REngine::replayBars() .
If the returned bars does not include data for the entire requested time period, and/or if the number of returned bars is a round number (such as 10000), then it is possible that the request was truncated. One can request the missing bars by shifting the time period of the original request to cover the truncated data. This truncation can occur when large amounts of data are requested.
See also: REngine::replayBars() | RCallbacks::Bar() | RCallbacks::BarReplay()

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ BarReplayInfo  (prototype)
Prototype: BarReplayInfo::BarReplayInfo()

▶ BarReplayInfo  (text)
This constructor initializes all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to NULL and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to zero. Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Boolean members are set to false. Pointer values are set to NULL.

▶ ~BarReplayInfo  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: BarReplayInfo::~BarReplayInfo() [virtual]

▶ ~BarReplayInfo  [virtual]  (text)
This destructor will not deallocate memory associated with internal pointers such as the pData of any tsNCharcbs.

── Member Function Documentation ──

▶ dump  (text)
This routine will print the current values of the object to stdout. The precision of the output may not reflect the actual precision of the value. For example, doubles may only be printed to six decimal places, even if the value of the double has a greater significant precision.

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::BidInfo: This class represents an bid quote.
The BidInfo class is used to pass quote information received based on REngine::subscribe() requests via the RCallbacks::BidQuote() and RCallbacks::BestBidQuote callbacks.

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

── Member Data Documentation ──

▶ bLeanPriceFlag  (prototype)
Prototype: BidInfo::bLeanPriceFlag

▶ bLeanPriceFlag  (text)
Indicates whether dLeanPrice is defined.

▶ bPriceFlag  (prototype)
Prototype: BidInfo::bPriceFlag

▶ bPriceFlag  (text)
This member variable specifies if BidInfo::dPrice is undefined. If the value is true, then the price is defined. If the value is false, the price is undefined. It is possible that a price is undefined if an order book becomes corrupt or invalid.

▶ bSizeFlag  (prototype)
Prototype: BidInfo::bSizeFlag

▶ bSizeFlag  (text)
This member variable specifies if BidInfo::llSize is undefined. A value of true indicates that the value is defined. A value of false indicates that the value is undefined.

▶ dLeanPrice  (prototype)
Prototype: BidInfo::dLeanPrice

▶ dLeanPrice  (text)
If available, specifies the lean price of the inside of the book. The lean price applies to best bid/ask updates, and indicates where the price is 'leaning' within the spread.

▶ dPrice  (prototype)
Prototype: BidInfo::dPrice

▶ dPrice  (text)
This member variable specifies the price for the quote.

▶ iConnId  (prototype)
Prototype: BidInfo::iConnId

▶ iConnId  (text)
Identifies the connection on which this data was received. It is possible when using the R | Diamond Cutter API, that certain data can be received twice, once from the regular infrastructure, and once from the R | Diamond Cutter infrastructure. This indicator provides a mechanism to distinguish between the two.

▶ iConnId  (see_also)
See also (iConnId): MARKET_DATA_CONNECTION_ID | DIAMOND_CUTTER_MARKET_DATA_CONNECTION_ID

▶ iNumOrders  (prototype)

This member variable specifies the number of orders associated with the price/quantity of this quote.

▶ iSsboe  (prototype)
Prototype: BidInfo::iSsboe

▶ iSsboe  (text)
Timestamp in seconds since the beginning of epoch (Jan. 1, 1970). If the callback type is MD_UPDATE_CB, this timestamp indicates the receipt time of this message from the market data provider.
If the image type is MD_IMAGE_CB, the timestamp indicates that the data is current "as of" that time.

▶ llImpliedSize  (text)
If available, this member variable specifies the implied quantity associated with the price for the quote. Subtracting this amount from BidInfo::llSize yields the outright quantity for the price level. A value of zero indicates that the implied size is not available/defined or that it is actually zero.

tsNCharcb *  pExchangeArray
tsNCharcb *  pExpirationTimeArray
tsNCharcb *  pPeriodCodeArray
tsNCharcb *  pProductCodeArray

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::BinaryContractListInfo: An instance of this class is the response to a call to REngine::listBinaryContracts() .
An BinaryContractListInfo object is used to pass :
• a list of exchanges,
• a list of product codes within an exchange,
• a list of binary contract types within that product code,
• a list of period codes within that binary contract type,
• a list of expiration times within that period code, or
• a list of RefDataInfo objects representing binary contracts with the particular features of exchange, product code, binary contract type, period code and expiration time.
What is returned depends upon what was initially passed into REngine::listBinaryContracts() .
See also: REngine::listBinaryContracts() | RCallbacks::BinaryContractList()

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ BinaryContractListInfo  (prototype)
Prototype: BinaryContractListInfo::BinaryContractListInfo()

▶ BinaryContractListInfo  (text)
This constructor initializes all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to NULL and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to zero. Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Boolean members are set to false. Pointers (and pointers to arrays) are set to NULL.

▶ ~BinaryContractListInfo  [virtual]  (prototype)

▶ ~BinaryContractListInfo  [virtual]  (text)

▶ asRefDataInfoArray  (prototype)
Prototype: BinaryContractListInfo::asRefDataInfoArray

▶ asRefDataInfoArray  (text)
This array contains all of the instruments matching the filter criteria of exchange, product code, binary contract type, period code and expiration time passed into REngine::listBinaryContracts() .
This array may be empty in the event of an error.
The array length is specified by BinaryContractListInfo::iArrayLen .

▶ iArrayLen  (text)

▶ iRpCode  (prototype)

▶ iRpCode  (text)
The response code, if available, associated with the request REngine::listBinaryContracts() . A non-zero response code can be interpreted as an error.

▶ pBinaryContractTypeArray  (prototype)
Prototype: BinaryContractListInfo::pBinaryContractTypeArray

▶ pBinaryContractTypeArray  (text)
If no binary contract type was specified when calling REngine::listBinaryContracts() , the array will contain all binary contract types within the specified exchange and product which have binary contracts. If a binary contract type was specified when calling REngine::listBinaryContracts() , that binary contract type will be returned here.
This array may be empty in the event of an error.
The array length is specified by BinaryContractListInfo::iBinaryContractTypeArrayLen .

▶ pContext  (prototype)
Prototype: BinaryContractListInfo::pContext

▶ pContext  (text)
The context initially sent with REngine::listBinaryContracts() .

▶ pExchangeArray  (prototype)
Prototype: BinaryContractListInfo::pExchangeArray

▶ pExchangeArray  (text)
If no exchange was specified when calling REngine::listBinaryContracts() , the array will contain all exchanges which have binary contracts. If an exchange was specified when calling REngine::listBinaryContracts() , that exchange will be returned here.
This array may be empty in the event of an error.
The array length is specified by BinaryContractListInfo::iExchangeArrayLen .

▶ pExpirationTimeArray  (prototype)
Prototype: BinaryContractListInfo::pExpirationTimeArray

▶ pExpirationTimeArray  (text)
If no expiration time was specified when calling REngine::listBinaryContracts() , the array will contain all expiration times within the specified exchange, product, binary contract type and period code which have binary contracts. If an expiration time was specified when calling REngine::listBinaryContracts() , that expiration time will be returned here.
This array may be empty in the event of an error.
The array length is specified by BinaryContractListInfo::iExpirationTimeArrayLen .

▶ pPeriodCodeArray  (prototype)
Prototype: BinaryContractListInfo::pPeriodCodeArray

▶ pPeriodCodeArray  (text)
If no period code was specified when calling REngine::listBinaryContracts() , the array will contain all period codes within the specified exchange, product and binary contract type which have binary contracts. If a period code was specified when calling REngine::listBinaryContracts() , that period code will be returned here.
This array may be empty in the event of an error.
The array length is specified by BinaryContractListInfo::iPeriodCodeArrayLen .

▶ pProductCodeArray  (prototype)
Prototype: BinaryContractListInfo::pProductCodeArray

▶ pProductCodeArray  (text)
If no product code was specified when calling REngine::listBinaryContracts() , the array will contain all product codes within the specified exchange which have binary contracts. If a product code was specified when calling REngine::listBinaryContracts() , that product code will be returned here.
This array may be empty in the event of an error.
The array length is specified by BinaryContractListInfo::iProductCodeArrayLen .

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::BracketInfo: This class conveys bracket-specific information aboout a bracket order.
See also: REngine::replayBrackets() | REngine::subscribeBracket() | RCallbacks::BracketReplay() | RCallbacks::BracketUpdate()

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ BracketInfo  (prototype)
Prototype: BracketInfo::BracketInfo()

▶ BracketInfo  (text)
This constructor initializes all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to NULL and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to zero. Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Boolean members are set to false. Pointer values are set to NULL.

▶ ~BracketInfo  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: BracketInfo::~BracketInfo() [virtual]

▶ ~BracketInfo  [virtual]  (text)
This destructor will not deallocate memory associated with internal pointers such as the pData of any tsNCharcbs.

── Member Function Documentation ──

▶ clearHandles  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: int BracketInfo::clearHandles( int * aiCode) [virtual]

▶ clearHandles  [virtual]  (text)
This routine will clear all internal pointers and values. It will not deallocate any memory associated with them before clearing the values.

▶ dump  (prototype)
Prototype: int BracketInfo::dump( int * aiCode)

▶ dump  (text)
This routine will print the current values of the object to stdout. The precision of the output may not reflect the actual precision of the value. For example, doubles may only be printed to six decimal places, even if the value of the double has a greater significant precision. Empty tsNCharcbs will be displayed with a value of "<empty>".

── Member Data Documentation ──

▶ asStopArray  (prototype)
Prototype: BracketInfo::asStopArray

▶ asStopArray  (text)
Array of BracketTier objects representing the set of stop loss orders.
When the callback is RCallbacks::BracketReplay() all of the tiers will be represented in the array. When the callback is RCallbacks::BracketUpdate() , only a single tier will be represented in the array. Also, updates may indicate that the tier has been removed when the BracketTier::llQty is zero.

▶ asStopArray  (see_also)
See also (asStopArray): BracketTier

▶ asTargetArray  (prototype)
Prototype: BracketInfo::asTargetArray

▶ asTargetArray  (text)
Array of BracketTier objects representing the set of target/profit orders.

▶ asTargetArray  (see_also)
See also (asTargetArray): BracketTier

▶ iTrailByPriceId  (prototype)
Prototype: BracketParams::iTrailByPriceId

▶ iTrailByPriceId  (text)
Indicates the price type used by the trailing stop. This setting is used in conjunction with BracketParams::bTrailingStop .
For example, specifying trade price will cause the trailing stop to follow the most recent trade price.

▶ iTrailByPriceId  (see_also)
See also (iTrailByPriceId): BracketParams::bTrailingStop | RApi::BEST_ASK_PRICE | RApi::BEST_BID_PRICE | RApi::TRADE_PRICE

▶ sBracketType  (prototype)
Prototype: BracketParams::sBracketType

▶ sBracketType  (text)
Bracket type of the order, specifying whether targets and/or stops are to be released, and from where the tick offset of each are calculated. Required.

▶ sBracketType  (see_also)
See also (sBracketType): sBRACKET_TYPE_TARGET_AND_STOP | sBRACKET_TYPE_TARGET_ONLY | sBRACKET_TYPE_STOP_ONLY | sBRACKET_TYPE_TARGET_AND_STOP_STATIC | sBRACKET_TYPE_TARGET_ONLY_STATIC | sBRACKET_TYPE_STOP_ONLY_STATIC

▶ sOrderOperationType  (prototype)
Prototype: BracketParams::sOrderOperationType

▶ sOrderOperationType  (text)
Order operation type of the order, specifying cancellation behavior of the exit orders. This is required when placing target and stop orders.

▶ sOrderOperationType  (see_also)
See also (sOrderOperationType): sORDER_OPERATION_TYPE_AFOCCA | sORDER_OPERATION_TYPE_FOCCA | sORDER_OPERATION_TYPE_FCA | sORDER_OPERATION_TYPE_CCA

▶ sStopsToMarketOnReject  (prototype)
Prototype: BracketParams::sStopsToMarketOnReject

▶ sStopsToMarketOnReject  (text)
A value of "yes" indicates that stop orders should be converted to market orders if rejected. Otherwise set to null or empty.

▶ sTargetsToMarketWhenTouched  (prototype)
Prototype: BracketParams::sTargetsToMarketWhenTouched

▶ sTargetsToMarketWhenTouched  (text)
A value of "yes" indicates that target orders should be converted to market orders when the limit price of the order is touched. Otherwise set to null or empty.

RApi::BracketReplayInfo Class Reference
#include <[RApiPlus.h](RApiPlus_8h_source.html)>
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

── Public Member Functions ──
RApi::BracketReplayInfo — Public Member Functions:
BracketReplayInfo ()
int  dump (int *aiCode)
virtual  ~BracketReplayInfo ()

── Public Attributes ──
RApi::BracketReplayInfo — Public Attributes:
BracketInfo *  asBracketArray
int  iArrayLen
int  iRpCode
AccountInfo  oAccount

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::BracketReplayInfo: This class conveys the results from a call to REngine::replayBrackets() . If the request was handled without error, the iRpCode will be zero.
Each bracket is represented by a BracketInfo instance. This bracket information will not provide all order details, but the bracket-specific order details.
See also: REngine::replayBrackets | RCallbacks::BracketReplay

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ BracketReplayInfo  (prototype)
Prototype: BracketReplayInfo::BracketReplayInfo()

▶ BracketReplayInfo  (text)
This constructor initializes all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to NULL and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to zero. Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Boolean members are set to false. Pointer values are set to NULL.

▶ ~BracketReplayInfo  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: BracketReplayInfo::~BracketReplayInfo() [virtual]

▶ ~BracketReplayInfo  [virtual]  (text)
This destructor will not deallocate memory associated with internal pointers such as the pData of any tsNCharcbs.

── Member Function Documentation ──

▶ dump  (prototype)
Prototype: int BracketReplayInfo::dump( int * aiCode)

▶ dump  (text)
This routine will print the current values of the object to stdout. The precision of the output may not reflect the actual precision of the value. For example, doubles may only be printed to six decimal places, even if the value of the double has a greater significant precision.

── Member Data Documentation ──

▶ asBracketArray  (prototype)
Prototype: BracketReplayInfo::asBracketArray

▶ asBracketArray  (text)
Array of BracketInfo objects representing the set of bracket orders.

▶ asBracketArray  (see_also)
See also (asBracketArray): BracketInfo

▶ iArrayLen  (prototype)
Prototype: BracketReplayInfo::iArrayLen

▶ iArrayLen  (text)
Number of elements in the array BracketReplayInfo::asBracketArray .

▶ iRpCode  (prototype)
Prototype: BracketReplayInfo::iRpCode

▶ iRpCode  (text)
Response code associated with the replay brackets request.
A non-zero value can be interpreted as an error.

▶ oAccount  (prototype)
Prototype: BracketReplayInfo::oAccount

▶ oAccount  (text)
Account specified when calling REngine::replayBrackets() .

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::BracketTierModifyInfo: This class conveys the results of a call to REngine::modifyBracketTier() . Except for iRpCode, this class just returns the parameters passed in to REngine::modifyBracketTier()
See also: REngine::modifyBracketTier | RCallbacks::BracketTierModify

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ BracketTierModifyInfo  (prototype)
Prototype: BracketTierModifyInfo::BracketTierModifyInfo()

▶ BracketTierModifyInfo  (text)
This constructor initializes all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to NULL and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to zero. Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Boolean members are set to false. Pointer values are set to NULL.

▶ ~BracketTierModifyInfo  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: BracketTierModifyInfo::~BracketTierModifyInfo() [virtual]

▶ ~BracketTierModifyInfo  [virtual]  (text)
This destructor will not deallocate memory associated with internal pointers such as the pData of any tsNCharcbs.

── Member Function Documentation ──

▶ dump  (prototype)
Prototype: int BracketTierModifyInfo::dump( int * aiCode)

▶ dump  (text)
This routine will print the current values of the object to stdout. The precision of the output may not reflect the actual precision of the value. For example, doubles may only be printed to six decimal places, even if the value of the double has a greater significant precision.

Prototype: BracketTierModifyInfo::bTarget

int  iSsboe
int  iType
int  iUsecs
tsNCharcb  sClosePriceDate
tsNCharcb  sExchange
tsNCharcb  sTicker

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::CloseMidPriceInfo: This class represents close mid price information.
The CloseMidPriceInfo class is used to convey the close mid price for a trading session.
To express interest in mid price data, one can specify the MD_MID_PRICE subscription flag when subscribing. The MidPriceInfo class is used to pass mid price info via RCallbacks::MidPrice() and the CloseMidPriceInfo class is used to convey close mid prices via RCallbacks::CloseMidPrice() .
See also: REngine::subscribe | RApi::MD_MID_PRICE | MidPriceInfo | RCallbacks::MidPrice | CloseMidPriceInfo | RCallbacks::CloseMidPrice

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ CloseMidPriceInfo  (prototype)
Prototype: CloseMidPriceInfo::CloseMidPriceInfo()

▶ CloseMidPriceInfo  (text)
This constructor initializes all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to NULL and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to zero. Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Boolean members are set to false.

▶ ~CloseMidPriceInfo  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: CloseMidPriceInfo::~CloseMidPriceInfo() [virtual]

▶ ~CloseMidPriceInfo  [virtual]  (text)
This destructor will not deallocate memory associated with internal pointers such as the pData of any tsNCharcbs.

── Member Function Documentation ──

▶ dump  (prototype)
Prototype: int CloseMidPriceInfo::dump( int * aiCode)

▶ dump  (text)
This routine will print the current values of the object to stdout.

── Member Data Documentation ──

▶ bClosePrice  (prototype)
Prototype: CloseMidPriceInfo::bClosePrice

▶ bClosePrice  (text)
Indicates whether MidPriceInfo::dClosePrice is populated and valid. If this flag is false, it means that there is no close mid price.

▶ dClosePrice  (prototype)
Prototype: CloseMidPriceInfo::dClosePrice

▶ dClosePrice  (text)
Specifies the close mid price of the current trading session.

▶ iSsboe  (prototype)
Prototype: CloseMidPriceInfo::iSsboe

▶ iSsboe  (text)
Timestamp in seconds since the beginning of epoch (Jan. 1, 1970). If the callback type is MD_UPDATE_CB, this timestamp indicates the receipt time of this message from the market data provider.
If the image type is MD_IMAGE_CB, the timestamp indicates that the data is current "as of" that time.

▶ iType  (prototype)
Prototype: CloseMidPriceInfo::iType

▶ iType  (text)
Indicates if the trade is from a cache, the real-time data stream or from the historical database.
Possible values for this variable are

▶ iType  (see_also)
See also (iType): MD_IMAGE_CB | MD_UPDATE_CB | MD_HISTORY_CB

▶ iUsecs  (prototype)
Prototype: CloseMidPriceInfo::iUsecs

▶ iUsecs  (text)
Microsecond component of the iSsboe timestamp

▶ sClosePriceDate  (prototype)
Prototype: CloseMidPriceInfo::sClosePriceDate

▶ sClosePriceDate  (text)
Specifies the date of the trading session for which the close mid price applies.

▶ sExchange  (prototype)
Prototype: CloseMidPriceInfo::sExchange

▶ sExchange  (text)
Exchange of this instrument.

▶ sTicker  (prototype)
Prototype: CloseMidPriceInfo::sTicker

▶ sTicker  (text)
Ticker symbol of this instrument.

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::ClosePriceInfo: This class represents close information.
The ClosePriceInfo class is used to pass close price information received as a result of calls to REngine::subscribe() via RCallbacks::ClosePrice() .

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ ClosePriceInfo  (prototype)
Prototype: ClosePriceInfo::ClosePriceInfo()

▶ ClosePriceInfo  (text)
This constructor initializes all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to MNM_EMPTY_DATA_ITEM and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to MNM_EMPTY_DATA_ITEM_LEN. Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Boolean members are set to false.

▶ ~ClosePriceInfo  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: ClosePriceInfo::~ClosePriceInfo() [virtual]

▶ ~ClosePriceInfo  [virtual]  (text)
This destructor will not deallocate memory associated with internal pointers such as the pData of any tsNCharcbs.

── Member Function Documentation ──

▶ dump  (prototype)
Prototype: int ClosePriceInfo::dump( int * aiCode)

▶ dump  (text)
This routine will print the current values of the object to stdout.

── Member Data Documentation ──

▶ bPriceFlag  (prototype)
Prototype: ClosePriceInfo::bPriceFlag

▶ bPriceFlag  (text)
This member variable specifies if ClosePriceInfo::dPrice is defined or not. If the value is true, then the price is defined. If the value is false, the price is undefined. It is possible that a price is undefined if the exchange does not publish this information.

▶ dPrice  (prototype)
Prototype: ClosePriceInfo::dPrice

▶ dPrice  (text)
This member variable specifies the close price.

▶ iSsboe  (prototype)
Prototype: ClosePriceInfo::iSsboe

▶ iSsboe  (text)
Timestamp in seconds since the beginning of epoch (Jan. 1, 1970). If the callback type is MD_UPDATE_CB, this timestamp indicates the receipt time of this message from the market data provider.
If the image type is MD_IMAGE_CB, the timestamp indicates that the data is current "as of" that time.

▶ iType  (prototype)
Prototype: ClosePriceInfo::iType

▶ iType  (text)
This member variable indicates if the close is from a cache or the real-time data stream.
Possible values for this variable are

▶ iType  (see_also)
See also (iType): MD_IMAGE_CB | MD_UPDATE_CB

▶ iUsecs  (prototype)
Prototype: ClosePriceInfo::iUsecs

▶ iUsecs  (text)
Microsecond component of the iSsboe timestamp

▶ sExchange  (prototype)
Prototype: ClosePriceInfo::sExchange

▶ sExchange  (text)
This member variable contains the exchange corresponding to the close.

▶ sTicker  (prototype)
Prototype: ClosePriceInfo::sTicker

▶ sTicker  (text)
This member variable contains the symbol corresponding to the close.

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::ClosingIndicatorInfo: This class represents closing indicators.
The ClosingIndicatorInfo class is used to pass closing indicator price information received as a result of calls to REngine::subscribe() requests via the RCallbacks::ClosingIndicator() callback. Not all exchanges or instruments make use of closing indicators.

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

#include <[RApiPlus.h](RApiPlus_8h_source.html)>
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

── Public Member Functions ──

── Public Attributes ──
RApi::DboBookRebuildInfo — Public Attributes:
bool  bPrice
double  dPrice
int  iRpCode
void *  pContext
tsNCharcb  sExchange
tsNCharcb  sRpCode
tsNCharcb  sTicker

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::DboBookRebuildInfo: This class represents status information related to requesting an dbo order book image.
DboBookRebuildInfo conveys the conclusion and/or response code associated with a call to REngine::rebuildDboBook() . The individual orders comprising the order book image will be conveyed via RCallbacks::Dbo() .
See also: REngine::rebuildDboBook() | RCallbacks::Dbo() | RCallbacks::DboBookRebuild()

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ DboBookRebuildInfo  (prototype)
Prototype: DboBookRebuildInfo::DboBookRebuildInfo()

▶ DboBookRebuildInfo  (text)
This constructor initializes all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to NULL and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to zero. Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Boolean members are set to false.

▶ ~DboBookRebuildInfo  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: DboBookRebuildInfo::~DboBookRebuildInfo() [virtual]

▶ ~DboBookRebuildInfo  [virtual]  (text)
This destructor will not deallocate memory associated with internal pointers such as the pData of any tsNCharcbs.

── Member Function Documentation ──

▶ dump  (prototype)
Prototype: int DboBookRebuildInfo::dump( int * aiCode)

▶ dump  (text)
This routine will print the current values of the object to stdout.

── Member Data Documentation ──

▶ bPrice  (prototype)
Prototype: DboBookRebuildInfo::bPrice

▶ bPrice  (text)
Indicates if a price was specified when requesting the dbo order book image.

▶ dPrice  (prototype)
Prototype: DboBookRebuildInfo::dPrice

▶ dPrice  (text)
Price specified when requesting the dbo order book image, if DboBookRebuildInfo::bPrice is true.

▶ iRpCode  (prototype)
Prototype: DboBookRebuildInfo::iRpCode

▶ iRpCode  (text)
Response code associated with the rebuild dbo book request.
Most non-zero values can be interpreted as errors, although some can be expected, such as receiving API_NO_DATA (7) if there is no dbo data for a given price level.

▶ pContext  (prototype)
Prototype: DboBookRebuildInfo::pContext

▶ pContext  (text)
If a context pointer was passed into REngine::rebuildDboBook() , it will be returned here. The callback must be associated With the same instance of REngine on which REngine::rebuildDboBook() was called. In other words, the context pointer is only supported within an instance of REngine . If a context pointer was not passed when calling REngine::rebuildDboBook() , this will be null.

▶ sExchange  (prototype)
Prototype: DboBookRebuildInfo::sExchange

▶ sExchange  (text)
Exchange for which the dbo order book image was requested.

▶ sRpCode  (prototype)
Prototype: DboBookRebuildInfo::sRpCode

▶ sRpCode  (text)
The string equivalent of the response code DboBookRebuildInfo::iRpCode . If DboBookRebuildInfo::iRpCode equals API_OK, this string may be empty.

▶ sTicker  (prototype)
Prototype: DboBookRebuildInfo::sTicker

▶ sTicker  (text)
Ticker for which the dbo order book image was requested.

tsNCharcb  sSide
tsNCharcb  sTicker

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::DboInfo: This class represents depth-by-order (DBO) market data.
What is DBO data and how might I use it?
See also: REngine::subscribeDbo | REngine::rebuildDboBook | RCallbacks::Dbo

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ DboInfo  (prototype)
Prototype: DboInfo::DboInfo()

▶ DboInfo  (text)
This constructor initializes all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to NULL and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to zero. Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Boolean members are set to false. Pointer values are set to NULL.

▶ ~DboInfo  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: DboInfo::~DboInfo() [virtual]

▶ ~DboInfo  [virtual]  (text)
This destructor will not deallocate memory associated with internal pointers such as the pData of any tsNCharcbs.

── Member Function Documentation ──

▶ dump  (prototype)
Prototype: int DboInfo::dump( int * aiCode)

▶ dump  (text)
This routine will print the current values of the object to stdout. The precision of the output may not reflect the actual precision of the value. For example, doubles may only be printed to six decimal places, even if the value of the double has a greater significant precision. Empty tsNCharcbs will be displayed with a value of "<empty>".

── Member Data Documentation ──

▶ bPreviousPrice  (prototype)
Prototype: DboInfo::bPreviousPrice

▶ bPreviousPrice  (text)
Indicates whether a previous price was on this message.

▶ bPrice  (prototype)
Prototype: DboInfo::bPrice

▶ bPrice  (text)
Indicates whether DboInfo::dPrice contains a value. Some exchanges may not publish a price when removing an order from the order book.

▶ bSize  (prototype)
Prototype: DboInfo::bSize

▶ bSize  (text)
Indicates whether DboInfo::llSize contains a valid value. For example, some exchanges may not publish a size when removing an order from the order book.

▶ dPreviousPrice  (prototype)
Prototype: DboInfo::dPreviousPrice

▶ dPreviousPrice  (text)
If present, the previous price of this order.

▶ dPrice  (prototype)
Prototype: DboInfo::dPrice

▶ dPrice  (text)
Price associated with this order. Check that DboInfo::bPrice is true before using this value.

▶ iJopNsecs  (prototype)
Prototype: DboInfo::iJopNsecs

▶ iJopNsecs  (text)
If available, the nanosecond component of the jump-off-point timestamp as presented by the exchange/market data source.
If the callback type (iType) is MD_IMAGE_CB, this value may be zero.

▶ iJopSsboe  (prototype)
Prototype: DboInfo::iJopSsboe

▶ iJopSsboe  (text)
If available, the jump-off-point timestamp in seconds since the beginning of epoch (Jan. 1, 1970). This usually represents the transmit timestamp as presented by the exchange/market data source.
If the callback type (iType) is MD_IMAGE_CB, the timestamp indicates that the data is current "as of" that time.

▶ iSourceNsecs  (prototype)
Prototype: DboInfo::iSourceNsecs

▶ iSourceNsecs  (text)
If available, the nanosecond component of the source timestamp as presented by the exchange/market data source.
If the callback type (iType) is MD_IMAGE_CB, this value may be zero.

▶ iSourceSsboe  (prototype)
Prototype: DboInfo::iSourceSsboe

▶ iSourceSsboe  (text)
If available, the timestamp in seconds since the beginning of epoch (Jan. 1, 1970) as presented by the exchange/market data source. Generally this timestamp correlates to the event time, rather than a transmission (JOP) or receipt timestamp.
If the callback type (iType) is MD_IMAGE_CB, the timestamp indicates that the data is current "as of" that time.

▶ iSourceUsecs  (prototype)
Prototype: DboInfo::iSourceUsecs

▶ iSourceUsecs  (text)
If available, the microsecond component of the source timestamp as presented by the exchange/market data source.
If the callback type (iType) is MD_IMAGE_CB, this value may be zero.

▶ iSsboe  (prototype)
Prototype: DboInfo::iSsboe

▶ iSsboe  (text)
Timestamp in seconds since the beginning of epoch (Jan. 1, 1970). If the callback type is MD_UPDATE_CB, this timestamp indicates the receipt time of this message from the market data provider.
If the callback type (iType) is MD_IMAGE_CB, the timestamp indicates that the data is current "as of" that time.

▶ iType  (prototype)
Prototype: DboInfo::iType

▶ iType  (text)
Indicates if the data from a cache (MD_IMAGE_CB), the real-time data stream (MD_UPDATE_CB) or from a historical database (MD_HISTORY_CB).
For this class, expected values are MD_IMAGE_CB if the DboInfo data is in response to REngine::rebuildDboBook() , or MD_UPDATE_CB if the DboInfo from a subscription ( REngine::subscribeDbo() ).

▶ iType  (see_also)
See also (iType): REngine::rebuildDboBook | REngine::subscribeDbo | RCallbacks::Dbo | MD_IMAGE_CB | MD_UPDATE_CB

▶ iUsecs  (prototype)
Prototype: DboInfo::iUsecs

▶ iUsecs  (text)
Microsecond component of the iSsboe timestamp
If the callback type (iType) is MD_IMAGE_CB, this value may be zero.

▶ llSize  (prototype)
Prototype: DboInfo::llSize

▶ llSize  (text)
Size associated with this order. Check that DboInfo::bSize is true before using this value.

▶ pContext  (prototype)
Prototype: void* RApi::DboInfo::pContext

▶ sDboUpdateType  (prototype)
Prototype: DboInfo::sDboUpdateType

▶ sDboUpdateType  (text)
Type of depth-by-order update. The update types indicate whether the order specified by this instance is a new order ("N"), a modified/changed order ("C"), or a deleted order ("D").

▶ sExchange  (prototype)
Prototype: DboInfo::sExchange

▶ sExchange  (text)
Exchange of the instrument

▶ sExchOrdId  (prototype)
Prototype: DboInfo::sExchOrdId

▶ sExchOrdId  (text)
Exchange order id of this order.

▶ sPriority  (prototype)
Prototype: DboInfo::sPriority

▶ sPriority  (text)
Priority of this order in the exchange's matching engine queue. This value may have different usage conventions at different exchanges. At the time of this writing, on Globex/CME, a lower priority means being closer to the front of the queue.

▶ sSide  (prototype)
Prototype: DboInfo::sSide

▶ sSide  (text)
Side of the order

▶ sTicker  (prototype)
Prototype: DboInfo::sTicker

▶ sTicker  (text)
Ticker symbol of the instrument

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::EasyToBorrowInfo: Instrument's easy-to-borrow information.
The EasyToBorrowInfo class is used to convey information about shares of an instrument, and if they can be borrowed. Normally this information is used when short selling equities.
See also: REngine::getEasyToBorrowList() | REngine::subscribeEasyToBorrow() | REngine::unsubscribeEasyToBorrow() | RCallbacks::EasyToBorrow() | RCallbacks::EasyToBorrowList() | EasyToBorrowListInfo

▶ iType  (text)
Indicates if this easy-to-borrow information is from a cache or the real-time data stream. If one has both subscribed to easy-to-borrow updates ( REngine::subscribeEasyToBorrow() ) and requested the list ( REngine::getEasyToBorrowList() ), and one receives an MD_UPDATE_CB before the MD_IMAGE_CB for a given instrument, one should regard the MD_UPDATE_CB as current, and discard the MD_IMAGE_DB.
Possible values for this variable are

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::EndQuoteInfo: This class represents the end to a set of updates to an order book.
The EndQuoteInfo class conveys that a set of quote updates to the instrument's order book is over, and the order book can be evaluated. This class is similar to the AskInfo or BidInfo classes where iUpdateType is UPDATE_TYPE_END, except that this class does not include price level information.

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ EndQuoteInfo  (prototype)
Prototype: EndQuoteInfo::EndQuoteInfo()

▶ EndQuoteInfo  (text)
This constructor initializes all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to MNM_EMPTY_DATA_ITEM and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to MNM_EMPTY_DATA_ITEM_LEN. Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Boolean members are set to false.

▶ ~EndQuoteInfo  [virtual]  (prototype)

── Member Data Documentation ──

▶ iConnId  (prototype)
Prototype: EndQuoteInfo::iConnId

▶ iConnId  (text)
Identifies the connection on which this data was received. It is possible when using the R | Diamond Cutter API, that certain data can be received twice, once from the regular infrastructure, and once from the R | Diamond Cutter infrastructure. This indicator provides a mechanism to distinguish between the two.

▶ iConnId  (see_also)
See also (iConnId): MARKET_DATA_CONNECTION_ID | DIAMOND_CUTTER_MARKET_DATA_CONNECTION_ID

▶ iSsboe  (prototype)
Prototype: EndQuoteInfo::iSsboe

▶ iSsboe  (text)
Timestamp in seconds since the beginning of epoch (Jan. 1, 1970). If the callback type is MD_UPDATE_CB, this timestamp indicates the receipt time of this message from the market data provider.
If the image type is MD_IMAGE_CB, the timestamp indicates that the data is current "as of" that time.

▶ iType  (text)

▶ iType  (see_also)

Possible values for this variable are :

▶ iUpdateType  (see_also)

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
RApi::EquityOptionStrategyListInfo Class Reference
#include <[RApiPlus.h](RApiPlus_8h_source.html)>
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

── Public Member Functions ──
RApi::EquityOptionStrategyListInfo — Public Member Functions:
virtual int  clearHandles (int *aiCode)
int  dump (int *aiCode)
EquityOptionStrategyListInfo ()
virtual  ~EquityOptionStrategyListInfo ()

── Public Attributes ──
RApi::EquityOptionStrategyListInfo — Public Attributes:
int  iExchangeArrayLen
int  iExpirationArrayLen
int  iRpCode
int  iStrategyTypeArrayLen
int  iTickerArrayLen
int  iUnderlyingArrayLen
void *  pContext
tsNCharcb *  pExchangeArray
tsNCharcb *  pExpirationArray
tsNCharcb *  pStrategyTypeArray
tsNCharcb *  pTickerArray
tsNCharcb *  pUnderlyingArray

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::EquityOptionStrategyListInfo: An instance of this class is the response to a call to REngine::getEquityOptionStrategyList() .
An EquityOptionStrategyListInfo object is used to pass :
• a list of exchanges,
• a list of underlying instruments within an exchange,
• a list of strategy types within an underlying (within an exchange),
• a list of expiration dates within a strategy type (within an underlying/exchange), or
• a list of tickers representing strategies within an expiration date
What is returned depends upon what was initially passed into REngine::getEquityOptionStrategyList() .

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ EquityOptionStrategyListInfo  (prototype)
Prototype: EquityOptionStrategyListInfo::EquityOptionStrategyListInfo()

▶ EquityOptionStrategyListInfo  (text)
This constructor initializes all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to MNM_EMPTY_DATA_ITEM and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to MNM_EMPTY_DATA_ITEM_LEN. Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Boolean members are set to false. Pointers (and pointers to arrays) are set to NULL.

▶ ~EquityOptionStrategyListInfo  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: EquityOptionStrategyListInfo::~EquityOptionStrategyListInfo() [virtual]

▶ ~EquityOptionStrategyListInfo  [virtual]  (text)
This destructor will not deallocate memory associated with internal pointers such as the pData of any tsNCharcbs.

── Member Function Documentation ──

▶ pExpirationArray  (text)
