▶ pStrategyTypeArray  (prototype)
Prototype: EquityOptionStrategyListInfo::pStrategyTypeArray

▶ pStrategyTypeArray  (text)
This is an array of tsNCharcbs indicating a list of strategy types or a single strategy type for which the array of expirations ( EquityOptionStrategyListInfo::pExpirationArray ) applies. The array length is specified by EquityOptionStrategyListInfo::iStrategyTypeArrayLen .

▶ pTickerArray  (prototype)
Prototype: EquityOptionStrategyListInfo::pTickerArray

▶ pTickerArray  (text)
This is an array of tickers. The array length is specified by EquityOptionStrategyListInfo::iTickerArrayLen .

▶ pUnderlyingArray  (prototype)
Prototype: EquityOptionStrategyListInfo::pUnderlyingArray

▶ pUnderlyingArray  (text)
This is an array of tsNCharcbs indicating a list of underlying instruments or a single underlying instrument for which the array of strategy types ( EquityOptionStrategyListInfo::pStrategyTypeArray ) applies. The array length is specified by EquityOptionStrategyListInfo::iUnderlyingArrayLen .

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::ExecutionReplayInfo: An instance of this class represents a collection of fills. Each execution will be represented by a OrderFillReport instance.
The ExecutionReplayInfo class is used to pass a list of executions via the RCallbacks::ExecutionReplay callback resulting from a call to REngine::replayExecutions() .

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ ExecutionReplayInfo  (prototype)
Prototype: ExecutionReplayInfo::ExecutionReplayInfo()

▶ ExecutionReplayInfo  (text)
This constructor initializes all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to MNM_EMPTY_DATA_ITEM and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to MNM_EMPTY_DATA_ITEM_LEN. Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Boolean members are set to false. Pointer values are set to NULL.

▶ ~ExecutionReplayInfo  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: ExecutionReplayInfo::~ExecutionReplayInfo() [virtual]

▶ ~ExecutionReplayInfo  [virtual]  (text)
This destructor will not deallocate memory associated with internal pointers such as the pData of any tsNCharcbs.

── Member Function Documentation ──

▶ dump  (prototype)
Prototype: int ExecutionReplayInfo::dump( int * aiCode)

▶ dump  (text)
This routine will print the current values of the object to stdout. The precision of the output may not reflect the actual precision of the value. For example, doubles may only be printed to six decimal places, even if the value of the double has a greater significant precision.

── Member Data Documentation ──

▶ asFillReportArray  (prototype)
Prototype: ExecutionReplayInfo::asFillReportArray

▶ asFillReportArray  (text)
An array of OrderFillReport objects representing the set of fills. If a position was carried over from a prior trading session, it will appear as a fill report with a type/report id of "sod". The size and price of the sod fill reflect the position carried over and the effective price at which the position was carried over or "filled".

▶ asFillReportArray  (see_also)
See also (asFillReportArray): SodReport

▶ iArrayLen  (prototype)
Prototype: ExecutionReplayInfo::iArrayLen

▶ iArrayLen  (text)
The number of elements in the array ExecutionReplayInfo::asFillReportArray .

▶ iRpCode  (prototype)
Prototype: ExecutionReplayInfo::iRpCode

▶ iRpCode  (text)
The response code associated with the response to REngine::replayExecutions() .

▶ oAccount  (prototype)
Prototype: ExecutionReplayInfo::oAccount

▶ oAccount  (text)
The Account used when calling REngine::replayExecutions() .

▶ sRpCode  (prototype)
Prototype: ExecutionReplayInfo::sRpCode

▶ sRpCode  (text)
The string equivalent of the response code ExecutionReplayInfo::iRpCode . If ExecutionReplayInfo::iRpCode equals API_OK, this string may be empty.

tsNCharcb  sTicker

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::HighBidPriceInfo: This class represents high bid price information.
The HighBidPriceInfo class is used to pass the current trading session's highest bid price. One can subscribe to highest bid price data by calling REngine::subscribe() with the MD_HIGH_BID_LOW_ASK flag enabled. Instances of this class will be passed via RCallbacks::HighBidPrice() .

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ HighBidPriceInfo  (prototype)
Prototype: HighBidPriceInfo::HighBidPriceInfo()

▶ HighBidPriceInfo  (text)
This constructor initializes all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to NULL and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to zero. Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Boolean members are set to false.

▶ ~HighBidPriceInfo  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: HighBidPriceInfo::~HighBidPriceInfo() [virtual]

▶ ~HighBidPriceInfo  [virtual]  (text)
This destructor will not deallocate memory associated with internal pointers such as the pData of any tsNCharcbs.

── Member Function Documentation ──

▶ dump  (prototype)
Prototype: int HighBidPriceInfo::dump( int * aiCode)

▶ dump  (text)
This routine will print the current values of the object to stdout.

── Member Data Documentation ──

▶ bPriceFlag  (prototype)
Prototype: HighBidPriceInfo::bPriceFlag

▶ bPriceFlag  (text)
This member variable specifies if HighBidPriceInfo::dPrice is defined or not. If the value is true, then the price is defined. If the value is false, the price is undefined. A price may be undefined if the trading session is closed, or if the exchange does not publish this information.

▶ dPrice  (prototype)
Prototype: HighBidPriceInfo::dPrice

▶ dPrice  (text)
Highest bid price.

▶ iSsboe  (prototype)
Prototype: HighBidPriceInfo::iSsboe

▶ iSsboe  (text)
Timestamp in seconds since the beginning of epoch (Jan. 1, 1970). If the callback type is MD_UPDATE_CB, this timestamp indicates the receipt time of this message from the market data provider.
If the image type is MD_IMAGE_CB, the timestamp indicates that the data is current "as of" that time.

▶ iType  (prototype)
Prototype: HighBidPriceInfo::iType

▶ iType  (text)
This member variable indicates if the high is from a cache or the real-time data stream.
Possible values for this variable are

▶ iType  (see_also)
See also (iType): MD_IMAGE_CB | MD_UPDATE_CB

▶ iUsecs  (prototype)
Prototype: HighBidPriceInfo::iUsecs

▶ iUsecs  (text)
Microsecond component of the iSsboe timestamp

▶ sExchange  (prototype)
Prototype: HighBidPriceInfo::sExchange

▶ sExchange  (text)
Exchange of the instrument corresponding to this high bid price.

▶ sTicker  (prototype)
Prototype: HighBidPriceInfo::sTicker

▶ sTicker  (text)
Symbol of the instrument corresponding to this high bid price.

tsNCharcb  sRpCode

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::InstrumentByUnderlyingInfo: An instance of this class is the response to a call to REngine::getInstrumentByUnderlying() .
An InstrumentByUnderlyingInfo object is used to pass :
• a list of underlyings,
• a list of expiration dates within an underlying, or
• a list of RefDataInfo objects representing options with an expiration date within an underlying
What is returned depends upon what was initially passed into REngine::getInstrumentByUnderlying() .

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ InstrumentByUnderlyingInfo  (prototype)
Prototype: InstrumentByUnderlyingInfo::InstrumentByUnderlyingInfo()

▶ InstrumentByUnderlyingInfo  (text)
This constructor initializes all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to MNM_EMPTY_DATA_ITEM and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to MNM_EMPTY_DATA_ITEM_LEN. Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Boolean members are set to false. Pointers (and pointers to arrays) are set to NULL.

▶ ~InstrumentByUnderlyingInfo  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: InstrumentByUnderlyingInfo::~InstrumentByUnderlyingInfo() [virtual]

▶ ~InstrumentByUnderlyingInfo  [virtual]  (text)
This destructor will not deallocate memory associated with internal pointers such as the pData of any tsNCharcbs.

── Member Function Documentation ──

Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Boolean members are set to false. Pointers (and pointers to arrays) are set to NULL.

── Member Data Documentation ──

▶ asRefDataInfoArray  (prototype)
Prototype: InstrumentByUnderlyingInfo::asRefDataInfoArray

▶ asRefDataInfoArray  (text)
If non-empty values for pUnderlying, pExchange and pExpiration were passed to REngine::getInstrumentByUnderlying() , InstrumentByUnderlyingInfo::asRefDataInfoArray will return a list of RefDataInfo objects for supported instruments having the specified underlying on the specified exchange with the specified expiration.
Otherwise, the array will be empty.

▶ iArrayLen  (prototype)
Prototype: InstrumentByUnderlyingInfo::iArrayLen

▶ iArrayLen  (text)
This variable specifies the array length.

▶ iExchangeArrayLen  (prototype)
Prototype: InstrumentByUnderlyingInfo::iExchangeArrayLen

▶ iExchangeArrayLen  (text)
The length of InstrumentByUnderlyingInfo::pExchangeArray .

▶ iExpirationArrayLen  (prototype)
Prototype: InstrumentByUnderlyingInfo::iExpirationArrayLen

▶ iExpirationArrayLen  (text)
The length of InstrumentByUnderlyingInfo::pExpirationArray .

▶ iRpCode  (prototype)
Prototype: InstrumentByUnderlyingInfo::iRpCode

▶ iRpCode  (text)
The response code, if available, associated with the request REngine::getInstrumentByUnderlying() . A non-zero response code can be interpreted as an error.

▶ iUnderlyingArrayLen  (prototype)
Prototype: InstrumentByUnderlyingInfo::iUnderlyingArrayLen

▶ iUnderlyingArrayLen  (text)
The length of InstrumentByUnderlyingInfo::pUnderlyingArray .

▶ pContext  (prototype)
Prototype: InstrumentByUnderlyingInfo::pContext

▶ pContext  (text)
The context initially sent with REngine::getOptionList() .

▶ pExchangeArray  (prototype)
Prototype: InstrumentByUnderlyingInfo::pExchangeArray

▶ pExchangeArray  (text)
If a non-empty value for pUnderlying and empty values for pExchange and pExpiration were passed in REngine::getInstrumentByUnderlying() , InstrumentByUnderlyingInfo::pExchangeArray will return a list of supported exchanges for the underlying specified when calling REngine::getInstrumentByUnderlying() .
If non-empty values for pUnderlying and pExchange were passed to REngine::getInstrumentByUnderlying() , InstrumentByUnderlyingInfo::pExchangeArray will return the value passed in initially.
The array length is specified by InstrumentByUnderlyingInfo::iExchangeArrayLen .

▶ pExpirationArray  (prototype)
Prototype: InstrumentByUnderlyingInfo::pExpirationArray

▶ pExpirationArray  (text)
If a non-empty value for pUnderlying and pExchange and an empty value for pExpiration was passed to REngine::getInstrumentByUnderlying() , InstrumentByUnderlyingInfo::pExpirationArray will return a list of supported expirations for the underlying and exchange specified when calling REngine::getInstrumentByUnderlying() .
If non-empty values for Underlying, pExchange and pExpiration were passed to REngine::getInstrumentByUnderlying() , InstrumentByUnderlyingInfo::pExpirationArray will return the value passed in initially.
The array length is specified by InstrumentByUnderlyingInfo::iExpirationArrayLen .

▶ pUnderlyingArray  (prototype)
Prototype: InstrumentByUnderlyingInfo::pUnderlyingArray

▶ pUnderlyingArray  (text)
InstrumentByUnderlyingInfo::pUnderlyingArray is an array of supported underlyings if empty values for pUnderlying, pExchange, pExpiration were passed to REngine::getInstrumentByUnderlying() .
If a non-empty value for pUnderlying and empty values for pExchange and pExpiration were passed in REngine::getInstrumentByUnderlying() , only the initial value of pUnderlying will be returned in this array.
The array length is specified by InstrumentByUnderlyingInfo::iUnderlyingArrayLen .

▶ sRpCode  (prototype)
Prototype: InstrumentByUnderlyingInfo::sRpCode

▶ sRpCode  (text)
The is a string equivalent of the response code. It may not be populated if the value of iRpCode is zero (API_OK).

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::InstrumentSearchInfo: An instance of this class marks the conclusion of the results returned from a call to REngine::searchInstrument() .
REngine::searchInstrument() returns zero to many instruments, with each returned instrument being conveyed via RCallbacks::RefData() . When the results are complete, RCallbacks::InstrumentSearch() will be invoked, with this class conveying the initial parameters.
See also: REngine::searchInstrument() | RCallbacks::RefData() | RCallbacks::InstrumentSearch()

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

Prototype: InstrumentSearchInfo::InstrumentSearchInfo()

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::LimitOrderBookInfo: This class represents a limit order book.
This class is used to pass order books received as a result of calls to REngine::rebuildBook() or REngine::subscribe() . The callback for both of these routines is RCallbacks::LimitOrderBook() .

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ LimitOrderBookInfo  (prototype)
Prototype: LimitOrderBookInfo::LimitOrderBookInfo()

▶ LimitOrderBookInfo  (text)
This constructor initializes all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to NULL and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to zero. Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Boolean members are set to false. Pointers (and pointers to arrays) are set to NULL.

▶ ~LimitOrderBookInfo  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: LimitOrderBookInfo::~LimitOrderBookInfo() [virtual]

▶ ~LimitOrderBookInfo  [virtual]  (text)
This destructor will not deallocate memory associated with internal pointers such as the pData of any tsNCharcbs or array elements.

── Member Function Documentation ──

▶ clearHandles  (prototype)
Prototype: int LimitOrderBookInfo::clearHandles( int * aiCode)

▶ clearHandles  (text)
This routine initializes all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to NULL and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to zero. Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Boolean members are set to false. Pointer values are set to NULL. (Internally used by the constructor.)

▶ dump  (prototype)
Prototype: int LimitOrderBookInfo::dump( int * aiCode)

▶ dump  (text)
This routine prints each row of the order book (bid and ask sides) to stdout. If a tsNCharcb is empty, the string "<empty>" will be printed.
If the flag associated with a price or size is true, the value of the price or size will be followed by "(valid)". If the value of the flag is false, the suffix will be "(invalid)".

── Member Data Documentation ──

▶ adAskPriceArray  (prototype)
Prototype: LimitOrderBookInfo::adAskPriceArray

▶ adAskPriceArray  (text)
This array represents ask prices of the order book. The first element of the array is the top of the order book. The value may be null if this side of the order book is empty.

▶ adBidPriceArray  (prototype)
Prototype: LimitOrderBookInfo::adBidPriceArray

▶ adBidPriceArray  (text)
This array represents bid prices of the order book. The first element of the array is the top of the order book. The value may be null if this side of the order book is empty.

▶ aiAskNumOrdersArray  (prototype)
Prototype: LimitOrderBookInfo::aiAskNumOrdersArray

▶ aiAskNumOrdersArray  (text)

▶ aiAskSsboeArray  (prototype)
Prototype: LimitOrderBookInfo::aiAskSsboeArray

▶ aiAskSsboeArray  (text)
This array contains the seconds portion of the timestamp of when each price level was most recently updated, expressed as seconds elapsed since Jan. 1, 1970 UTC. A value of zero indicates the timestamp is not available.

▶ aiAskUsecsArray  (prototype)

It may be zero, indicating that this side of the order book is empty.

▶ iConnId  (prototype)
Prototype: LimitOrderBookInfo::iConnId

▶ iConnId  (text)
Identifies the connection on which this data was received. It is possible when using the R | Diamond Cutter API, that certain data can be received twice, once from the regular infrastructure, and once from the R | Diamond Cutter infrastructure. This indicator provides a mechanism to distinguish between the two.

▶ iConnId  (see_also)
See also (iConnId): MARKET_DATA_CONNECTION_ID | DIAMOND_CUTTER_MARKET_DATA_CONNECTION_ID

▶ iRpCode  (prototype)

▶ iRpCode  (text)
The response code, if available, associated with a call to either REngine::subscribe() or REngine::rebuildBook() . A non-zero response code can be interpreted as an error.

▶ iSsboe  (prototype)
Prototype: LimitOrderBookInfo::iSsboe

▶ iSsboe  (text)
Timestamp in seconds since the beginning of epoch (Jan. 1, 1970). This timestamp indicates that the data is current "as of" this time.

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::LimitOrderParams: This class encapsulates information needed to submit a limit order.

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ LimitOrderParams  (prototype)
Prototype: LimitOrderParams::LimitOrderParams()

▶ LimitOrderParams  (text)
This constructor initializes all of the members using LimitOrderParams::clearHandles() .

▶ ~LimitOrderParams  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: LimitOrderParams::~LimitOrderParams() [virtual]

▶ ~LimitOrderParams  [virtual]  (text)
This destructor will not deallocate memory associated with internal pointers such as the pData of any tsNCharcbs.

── Member Function Documentation ──

▶ clearHandles  (prototype)
Prototype: int LimitOrderParams::clearHandles( int * aiCode)

▶ clearHandles  (text)
This routine clears all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to NULL and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to zero. Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Boolean members are set to false. Pointer values are set to NULL. This method is used internally by the constructor.

── Member Data Documentation ──

▶ dPrice  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::LimitOrderParams::dPrice

▶ dPrice  (text)
This member variable indicates the price of the order to be placed.

▶ llQty  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::LimitOrderParams::llQty

▶ llQty  (text)
This member variable indicates the quantity of the order to be placed.

▶ pAccount  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::LimitOrderParams::pAccount

▶ pAccount  (text)
This member variable specifies the account on which to place the order. When placing orders AccountInfo::RmsInfo can be NULL.

▶ pContext  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::LimitOrderParams::pContext

▶ pContext  (text)
This member variable specifies a user-defined context that will be passed with each order callback pertaining to this order. This pointer is specific to each instance of REngine .

▶ sBuySellType  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::LimitOrderParams::sBuySellType

▶ sBuySellType  (text)
This member variable specifies whether the order to be placed is a buy or sell. Not all values are supported by all exchanges, and new values may be introduced which are not part of this list.

▶ sBuySellType  (see_also)
See also (sBuySellType): RApi::sBUY_SELL_TYPE_BUY | RApi::sBUY_SELL_TYPE_SELL | RApi::sBUY_SELL_TYPE_SELL_SHORT | RApi::sBUY_SELL_TYPE_SELL_SHORT_EXEMPT

▶ sDuration  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::LimitOrderParams::sDuration

▶ sDuration  (text)
This member variable indicates the duration of the order to be placed. Not all values are supported by all exchanges, and new values may be introduced which are not part of this list.

▶ sDuration  (see_also)
See also (sDuration): RApi::sORDER_DURATION_DAY | RApi::sORDER_DURATION_FOK | RApi::sORDER_DURATION_IOC | RApi::sORDER_DURATION_GTC

▶ sEntryType  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::LimitOrderParams::sEntryType

▶ sEntryType  (text)
This member variable indicates whether this method is being called manually or automatically.

▶ sEntryType  (see_also)
See also (sEntryType): RApi::sORDER_ENTRY_TYPE_AUTO | RApi::sORDER_ENTRY_TYPE_MANUAL

▶ sExchange  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::LimitOrderParams::sExchange

▶ sExchange  (text)
This member variable specifies the exchange of the instrument on which to place the order.

▶ sRoutingInstructions  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::LimitOrderParams::sRoutingInstructions

▶ sRoutingInstructions  (text)
This member variable specifies the routing instructions.

▶ sTag  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::LimitOrderParams::sTag

▶ sTag  (text)
This optional value specifies a tag that is persisted with the resultant order by the infrastructure. If specified, this value will be present on subsequent order updates published by the infrastructure.
If this value is empty ( tsNCharcb::pData is NULL and tsNCharcb::iDataLen is zero), no tag will be set.
This value differs from OrderParams::sUserMsg in that it cannot be updated with subsequent order operations.
This value differs from OrderParams::sUserTag in that this value is used by R | Trader.

▶ sTag  (see_also)
See also (sTag): LineInfo::sTag | OrderReport::sTag | OrderParams::sTag | MarketOrderParams::sTag

▶ sTicker  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::LimitOrderParams::sTicker

▶ sTicker  (text)
This member variable specifies the symbol of the instrument on which to place the order.

▶ sTradeRoute  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::LimitOrderParams::sTradeRoute

▶ sTradeRoute  (text)
Specifies the trade route. Beginning with version 6.1.0.0 this parameter is required.
What are trade routes and how do I use them?

▶ sTradingAlgorithm  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::LimitOrderParams::sTradingAlgorithm

▶ sTradingAlgorithm  (text)
Some exchanges may require identification of which algorithm is submitting a particular order/cancel/modify request. This property provides a field for that purpose. In other words, it is an informational field for the exchange.

▶ sTradingAlgorithm  (see_also)
See also (sTradingAlgorithm): OrderParams::sTradingAlgorithm | LimitOrderParams::sTradingAlgorithm | MarketOrderParams::sTradingAlgorithm | ModifyOrderParams::sTradingAlgorithm | ModifyLimitOrderParams::sTradingAlgorithm | ModifyStopMarketOrderParams::sTradingAlgorithm | ModifyStopLimitOrderParams::sTradingAlgorithm | REngine::cancelOrder | REngine::cancelOrderList | LineInfo::sTradingAlgorithm

▶ sUserMsg  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::LimitOrderParams::sUserMsg

▶ sUserMsg  (text)
This optional variable specifies a user message that is persisted with the order request in the infrastructure. Unlike LimitOrderParams::pContext , this context string is not specific to an instance of the REngine . If this value is empty (sUserMsg.pData is NULL and sUserMsg.iDataLen is zero), then any existing user message ( MarketOrderParams::sUserMsg or ModifyOrderParams::sUserMsg ) will remain unmodified.

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::LineInfo: This class represents a cumulative view of orders.
The LineInfo class is used to pass order information (including various states of the order) via RCallbacks::LineUpdate , RCallbacks::OrderReplay or RCallbacks::OpenOrderReplay .
See also: REngine::cancelOrder | REngine::cancelAllOrders | REngine::modifyOrder | REngine::replayOpenOrders | REngine::sendOrder | REngine::sendOrderList

▶ dump  (prototype)
Prototype: int LineInfo::dump( int * aiCode)

▶ dump  (text)
This routine will print the current values of the object to stdout. The precision of the output may not reflect the actual precision of the value. For example, doubles may only be printed to six decimal places, even if the value of the double has a greater significant precision.

▶ iCancelSsboe  (prototype)
Prototype: int LineInfo::iCancelSsboe

▶ iCancelSsboe  (text)

▶ iCancelUsecs  (prototype)
Prototype: int LineInfo::iCancelUsecs

▶ iCancelUsecs  (text)
The microsecond portion of time at which the order is configured to be canceled. This value corresponds to OrderParams::iCancelUsecs .

▶ iGatewayBufferBytesBehind  (prototype)
Prototype: int LineInfo::iGatewayBufferBytesBehind

▶ iGatewayBufferBytesBehind  (text)
The size (in bytes) of the gateway's output buffer to the client, taken at the time this message left the infrastructure.
A non-zero value implies that the gateway is trying to send data to the client, but the client is not consuming the data quickly enough. This situation might be caused by insufficient network bandwidth between the gateway and the R | API client, or insufficient resources to process the data stream within the client process.
A value of zero means there is no output buffered, or the data is unavailable.
It will not be populated for historical/replayed data.
NOTE : This data is experimental and may not be available in future releases.

▶ iGatewaySsboe  (prototype)
Prototype: int LineInfo::iGatewaySsboe

▶ iGatewaySsboe  (text)
If not zero, the time at the gateway as this message leaves the infrastructure. This value is in seconds since the beginning of epoch (Jan. 1, 1970). It will not be populated for historical/replayed data.
NOTE : This data is experimental and may not be available in future releases.

▶ iGatewayUsecs  (prototype)
Prototype: int LineInfo::iGatewayUsecs

▶ iGatewayUsecs  (text)
The microsecond portion of the gateway timestamp. It will not be populated for historical/replayed data.
NOTE : This data is experimental and may not be available in future releases.

▶ iReleaseSsboe  (prototype)
Prototype: int LineInfo::iReleaseSsboe

▶ iReleaseSsboe  (text)
The seconds portion of the time at which the order is configured to be released, expressed in seconds since the beginning of the epoch. This value corresponds to OrderParams::iReleaseSsboe .

▶ iReleaseUsecs  (prototype)
Prototype: int LineInfo::iReleaseUsecs

▶ iReleaseUsecs  (text)
The millisecond portion of time at which the order was configured to be released. This value corresponds to OrderParams::iReleaseUsecs .

▶ iRpCode  (prototype)
Prototype: int LineInfo::iRpCode

▶ iRpCode  (text)
Response code, if applicable, from the REngine::replayOpenOrders response.
An iRpCode with a value of API_STATE_ERROR (31) indicates that the RApi could not process the price values. Under normal circumstances this should never occur.

▶ iSsboe  (prototype)
Prototype: int LineInfo::iSsboe

▶ iSsboe  (text)
Timestamp in seconds since the beginning of epoch (Jan. 1, 1970)

▶ iToMarketAfterPartialFillInSeconds  (text)

▶ llQuantityToFill  (text)
Confirmed quantity of this order.

▶ llReleasePendingSize  (prototype)
Prototype: LineInfo::llReleasePendingSize

▶ llReleasePendingSize  (text)
Quantity of an order that is unconfirmed by the exchange.
Typically the value will be the quantity of a new order prior to confirmation of that order by an exchange.
Afterwards, this will go to zero and the LineInfo::llQuantityToFill will hold the (now confirmed) value.
If the order is a 'synthetic' stop order (a stop order whose trigger functionality is not implemented by the exchange) that has not been triggered yet, then the quantity of the stop order will be contained in this value. In this case, the exchange has not yet received the order, and consequently has not confirmed the order quantity.

▶ llUnfilled  (prototype)
Prototype: LineInfo::llUnfilled

▶ llUnfilled  (text)
Quantity of this order that has not been filled or cancelled.

▶ oAccount  (prototype)
Prototype: AccountInfo LineInfo::oAccount

▶ oAccount  (text)
Account associated with this order.

▶ pContext  (prototype)
Prototype: int LineInfo::pContext

▶ pContext  (text)
A pointer that was passed when sending or updating this order. This context is order specific.

▶ pContext  (see_also)
See also (pContext): REngine::getOrderContext | REngine::setOrderContext | REngine::sendOrder | REngine::modifyOrder | LimitOrderParams::pContext | MarketOrderParams::pContext | ModifyOrderParams::pContext

▶ sBracketType  (prototype)

▶ sCompletionReason  (see_also)

It is possible that this value is used by the exchange in conjuction with, or in place of, the OrderReport::ExchOrdId, to identify the order.

It is possible that an exchange will need this value, and perhaps other values (such as LineInfo::sCurrentSequenceNumber ) to identify an order.

RApi::LoginParams Class Reference
#include <[RApiPlus.h](RApiPlus_8h_source.html)>
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

── Public Member Functions ──
RApi::LoginParams — Public Member Functions:
LoginParams ()
virtual  ~LoginParams ()

── Public Attributes ──
RApi::LoginParams — Public Attributes:
RCallbacks *  pCallbacks
tsNCharcb  sIhCnnctPt
tsNCharcb  sIhEnvKey
tsNCharcb  sIhPassword

tsNCharcb  sTsCnnctPt
tsNCharcb  sTsEnvKey
tsNCharcb  sTsPassword
tsNCharcb  sTsUser

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::LoginParams: This class encapsulates information needed to login. The information includes authentication credentials, connect points for accessing services, as well as callbacks for receiving notifications from the login session.
Connect point values are typically provided by the Rithmic operations team in 'connection_params.txt' files downloaded from your Rithmic download directory.
See also: REngine::login

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ LoginParams  (prototype)
Prototype: LoginParams::LoginParams()

▶ LoginParams  (text)
This constructor initializes all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to NULL and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to zero. Integers and doubles are initialized to zero. Boolean values are set to false. Pointer values are set to NULL. Pointers to arrays are set to NULL and their lengths are set to zero.

▶ ~LoginParams  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: LoginParams::~LoginParams() [virtual]

▶ ~LoginParams  [virtual]  (text)
This destructor will not deallocate memory associated with internal pointers such as the pData of any tsNCharcbs.

── Member Data Documentation ──

▶ pCallbacks  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::LoginParams::pCallbacks

▶ pCallbacks  (text)
This points to an instance of a user-defined subclass of RApi::RCallbacks . This instance defines all of the callback routines used for this login session.

▶ sIhCnnctPt  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::LoginParams::sIhCnnctPt

▶ sIhCnnctPt  (text)
Optional. Name of the history plant connect point. Specify this value for to access non-real-time data such as bars/candlestick data and intra-day tick data.

▶ sIhCnnctPt  (see_also)
See also (sIhCnnctPt): REngine::replayBars() | REngine::replayTrades() | REngine::subscribeBar() | REngine::unsubscribeBar()

▶ sIhEnvKey  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::LoginParams::sIhEnvKey

▶ sIhEnvKey  (text)
Optional. Environment key used when establishing connections to the history plant/historical data connection. This parameter is used only when the history plant connect point is specified. If this parameter is empty, the default environment will be used. Using the default environment results in the same behavior as prior versions where this parameter was not available.

▶ sIhEnvKey  (see_also)
See also (sIhEnvKey): Connecting to Multiple Rithmic Systems Simultaneously Using Environments

▶ sIhPassword  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::LoginParams::sIhPassword

▶ sIhPassword  (text)
Password used for authentication with the history plant/historical data connection. This parameter is required when specifying the LoginParams::sIhCnnctPt .

▶ sIhUser  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::LoginParams::sIhUser

▶ sIhUser  (text)
User id used for authentication with the history plant/historical data connection. This parameter is required when specifying the LoginParams::sIhCnnctPt .

▶ sMdCnnctPt  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::LoginParams::sMdCnnctPt

▶ sMdCnnctPt  (text)
Optional. Logical name of the ticker plant/market data connection. This parameter is required to retrieve streaming market data.

▶ sMdEnvKey  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::LoginParams::sMdEnvKey

▶ sMdEnvKey  (text)
Optional. Environment key used when establishing connections to the ticker plant/market data connection. This parameter is used only when the market data connect point is specified. If this parameter is empty, the default environment will be used. Using the default environment results in the same behavior as prior versions where this parameter was not available.

▶ sMdEnvKey  (see_also)
See also (sMdEnvKey): Connecting to Multiple Rithmic Systems Simultaneously Using Environments

▶ sMdPassword  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::LoginParams::sMdPassword

▶ sMdPassword  (text)
Optional. Password used for authentication with the ticker plant/market data connection. This parameter is required if the market data connect point is specified.

▶ sMdUser  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::LoginParams::sMdUser

▶ sMdUser  (text)
Optional. User id used for authentication with the ticker plant/market data connection. This parameter is required if the market data connect point is specified.

▶ sPnlCnnctPt  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::LoginParams::sPnlCnnctPt

▶ sPnlCnnctPt  (text)
Optional. Name of the profit-and-loss system connect point. Specify this value for pnl services such as pnl/postion status and updates. This connect point depends on the trading system connect point, so if parameter is specified, LoginParams::sTsCnnctPt must also be specified, otherwise REngine::login() will return with an error code of API_BAD_INPUT (6). This connection also uses the LoginParams::sTsEnvKey as the environment key and LoginParams::sTsUser and LoginParams::sTsPassword for authentication credentials.

▶ sPnlCnnctPt  (see_also)
See also (sPnlCnnctPt): REngine::subscribePnl() | REngine::unsubscribePnl() | REngine::replayPnl()

▶ sTsCnnctPt  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::LoginParams::sTsCnnctPt

▶ sTsCnnctPt  (text)
Optional. Name of the order plant/trading system connection. Specify this value to connect to the trading system for order services such as placing, modifying and cancelling orders, and getting order updates.

▶ sTsEnvKey  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::LoginParams::sTsEnvKey

▶ sTsEnvKey  (text)
Optional. Environment key used when establishing connections to the order plant/trading system connection as well as the profit/loss connect point. This parameter is used when the order plant connect point is specified and also if the profit/loss connect point is also specified. If this parameter is empty, the default environment will be used. Using the default environment results in the same behavior as prior versions where this parameter was not available.

▶ sTsEnvKey  (see_also)
See also (sTsEnvKey): Connecting to Multiple Rithmic Systems Simultaneously Using Environments

▶ sTsPassword  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::LoginParams::sTsPassword

▶ sTsPassword  (text)
Optional. Password used for authentication with the order plant/trading system connection (sTsCnnctPt) and the pnl connect point (sPnlCnnctPt). This value is required when specifying either of those connect points.

▶ sTsUser  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::LoginParams::sTsUser

▶ sTsUser  (text)
Optional. User id used for authentication with the order plant/trading system connection (sTsCnnctPt) and the pnl connect point (sPnlCnnctPt). This value is required when specifying either of those connect points.

── Public Attributes ──
RApi::LowAskPriceInfo — Public Attributes:
bool  bPriceFlag
double  dPrice
int  iSsboe
int  iType
int  iUsecs
tsNCharcb  sExchange
tsNCharcb  sTicker

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::LowAskPriceInfo: This class represents low ask price information.
The LowAskPriceInfo class is used to pass the current trading session's lowest asking price. One can subscribe to lowest asking price data by calling REngine::subscribe() with the MD_HIGH_BID_LOW_ASK flag enabled.
Instances of this class will be passed via the RCallbacks::LowAskPrice() callback.

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ LowAskPriceInfo  (prototype)
Prototype: LowAskPriceInfo::LowAskPriceInfo()

▶ LowAskPriceInfo  (text)
This constructor initializes all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to NULL and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to zero. Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Boolean members are set to false.

▶ ~LowAskPriceInfo  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: LowAskPriceInfo::~LowAskPriceInfo() [virtual]

▶ ~LowAskPriceInfo  [virtual]  (text)
This destructor will not deallocate memory associated with internal pointers such as the pData of any tsNCharcbs.

── Member Function Documentation ──

▶ dump  (prototype)
Prototype: int LowAskPriceInfo::dump( int * aiCode)

▶ dump  (text)
This routine will print the current values of the object to stdout.

── Member Data Documentation ──

▶ bPriceFlag  (prototype)
Prototype: LowAskPriceInfo::bPriceFlag

▶ bPriceFlag  (text)
This member variable specifies if LowAskPriceInfo::dPrice is defined or not. If the value is true, then the price is defined. If the value is false, the price is undefined. A low ask price may be undefined if the trading session is closed, or if the exchange does not publish this information.

▶ dPrice  (prototype)
Prototype: LowAskPriceInfo::dPrice

▶ dPrice  (text)
This member variable specifies the low ask price.

▶ iSsboe  (prototype)
Prototype: LowAskPriceInfo::iSsboe

▶ iSsboe  (text)
Timestamp in seconds since the beginning of epoch (Jan. 1, 1970). If the callback type is MD_UPDATE_CB, this timestamp indicates the receipt time of this message from the market data provider.
If the image type is MD_IMAGE_CB, the timestamp indicates that the data is current "as of" that time.

▶ iType  (prototype)
Prototype: LowAskPriceInfo::iType

▶ iType  (text)
This member variable indicates if the low is from a cache or the real-time data stream.
Possible values for this variable are

▶ iType  (see_also)
See also (iType): MD_IMAGE_CB | MD_UPDATE_CB

▶ iUsecs  (prototype)
Prototype: LowAskPriceInfo::iUsecs

▶ iUsecs  (text)
Microsecond component of the iSsboe timestamp

▶ sExchange  (prototype)
Prototype: LowAskPriceInfo::sExchange

▶ sExchange  (text)
This member variable contains the exchange corresponding to the low price.

▶ sTicker  (prototype)
Prototype: LowAskPriceInfo::sTicker

▶ sTicker  (text)
This member variable contains the symbol corresponding to the low price.

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::LowPriceInfo: This class represents low information.
The LowPriceInfo class is used to pass the daily low price received as a result of calls to REngine::subscribe() requests via the RCallbacks::LowPrice() callback.

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ LowPriceInfo  (prototype)
Prototype: LowPriceInfo::LowPriceInfo()

▶ LowPriceInfo  (text)
This constructor initializes all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to MNM_EMPTY_DATA_ITEM and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to MNM_EMPTY_DATA_ITEM_LEN. Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Boolean members are set to false.

▶ ~LowPriceInfo  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: LowPriceInfo::~LowPriceInfo() [virtual]

▶ ~LowPriceInfo  [virtual]  (text)
This destructor will not deallocate memory associated with internal pointers such as the pData of any tsNCharcbs.

── Member Function Documentation ──

▶ dump  (prototype)
Prototype: int LowPriceInfo::dump( int * aiCode)

▶ dump  (text)
This routine will print the current values of the object to stdout.

── Member Data Documentation ──

▶ bPriceFlag  (prototype)
Prototype: LowPriceInfo::bPriceFlag

▶ bPriceFlag  (text)
This member variable specifies if LowPriceInfo::dPrice is defined or not. If the value is true, then the price is defined. If the value is false, the price is undefined. A low price may be undefined if the trading session is closed, or if the exchange does not publish this information.

▶ dPrice  (prototype)
Prototype: LowPriceInfo::dPrice

▶ dPrice  (text)
This member variable specifies the low price.

▶ iSsboe  (prototype)
Prototype: LowPriceInfo::iSsboe

▶ iSsboe  (text)
Timestamp in seconds since the beginning of epoch (Jan. 1, 1970). If the callback type is MD_UPDATE_CB, this timestamp indicates the receipt time of this message from the market data provider.
If the image type is MD_IMAGE_CB, the timestamp indicates that the data is current "as of" that time.

▶ iType  (prototype)
Prototype: LowPriceInfo::iType

▶ iType  (text)
This member variable indicates if the low is from a cache or the real-time data stream.
Possible values for this variable are

▶ iType  (see_also)
See also (iType): MD_IMAGE_CB | MD_UPDATE_CB

▶ iUsecs  (prototype)
Prototype: LowPriceInfo::iUsecs

▶ iUsecs  (text)
Microsecond component of the iSsboe timestamp

▶ sExchange  (prototype)
Prototype: LowPriceInfo::sExchange

▶ sExchange  (text)
This member variable contains the exchange corresponding to the low price.

▶ sTicker  (prototype)
Prototype: LowPriceInfo::sTicker

▶ sTicker  (text)
This member variable contains the symbol corresponding to the low price.

▶ iJopNsecs  (prototype)
Prototype: MarketModeInfo::iJopNsecs

▶ iJopNsecs  (text)
If available, the nanosecond component of the jump-off-point timestamp as presented by the exchange/market data source.
If the callback type (iType) is MD_IMAGE_CB, this value may be zero.

▶ iJopSsboe  (prototype)
Prototype: MarketModeInfo::iJopSsboe

▶ iJopSsboe  (text)
If available, the jump-off-point timestamp in seconds since the beginning of epoch (Jan. 1, 1970). This usually represents the transmit timestamp as presented by the exchange/market data source.
If the callback type (iType) is MD_IMAGE_CB, the timestamp indicates that the data is current "as of" that time.

▶ iSourceNsecs  (prototype)
Prototype: MarketModeInfo::iSourceNsecs

▶ iSourceNsecs  (text)
If available, the nanosecond component of the source timestamp as presented by the exchange/market data source.
If the callback type (iType) is MD_IMAGE_CB, this value may be zero.

▶ iSourceSsboe  (prototype)
Prototype: MarketModeInfo::iSourceSsboe

▶ iSourceSsboe  (text)
If available, the timestamp in seconds since the beginning of epoch (Jan. 1, 1970) as presented by the exchange/market data source. Generally this timestamp correlates to the event time, rather than a transmission (JOP) or receipt timestamp.
If the callback type (iType) is MD_IMAGE_CB, the timestamp indicates that the data is current "as of" that time.

▶ iSourceUsecs  (prototype)
Prototype: MarketModeInfo::iSourceUsecs

▶ iSourceUsecs  (text)
If available, the microsecond component of the source timestamp as presented by the exchange/market data source.
If the callback type (iType) is MD_IMAGE_CB, this value may be zero.

▶ iSsboe  (prototype)
Prototype: MarketModeInfo::iSsboe

▶ iSsboe  (text)
Timestamp in seconds since the beginning of epoch (Jan. 1, 1970). If the callback type is MD_UPDATE_CB, this timestamp indicates the receipt time of this message from the market data provider.
If the callback type (iType) is MD_IMAGE_CB, the timestamp indicates that the data is current "as of" that time.

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::MarketOrderParams: This class encapsulates information needed to submit a market order.
The only optional value is MarketOrderParams::pContext .

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ MarketOrderParams  (prototype)

▶ MarketOrderParams  (text)
This constructor initializes all of the members using MarketOrderParams::clearHandles() .

▶ ~MarketOrderParams  [virtual]  (prototype)

▶ ~MarketOrderParams  [virtual]  (text)
This destructor will not deallocate memory associated with internal pointers such as the pData of any tsNCharcbs.

── Member Function Documentation ──

▶ clearHandles  (prototype)
Prototype: int MarketOrderParams::clearHandles( int * aiCode)

▶ clearHandles  (text)
This routine clears all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to NULL and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to zero. Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Boolean members are set to false. Pointer values are set to NULL. This method is used internally by the constructor.

── Member Data Documentation ──

▶ llQty  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::MarketOrderParams::llQty

▶ llQty  (text)
This member variable indicates the quantity of the order to be placed.

▶ pAccount  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::MarketOrderParams::pAccount

▶ pAccount  (text)
This member variable specifies the account on which to place the order. When placing orders AccountInfo::RmsInfo can be NULL.

▶ pContext  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::MarketOrderParams::pContext

▶ pContext  (text)
This member variable specifies a user-defined context that will be passed with each order callback pertaining to this order. This pointer is specific to each instance of REngine .

▶ sBuySellType  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::MarketOrderParams::sBuySellType

▶ sBuySellType  (text)
This member variable specifies whether the order to be placed is a buy or sell. Not all values are supported by all exchanges, and new values may be introduced which are not part of this list.

▶ sBuySellType  (see_also)
See also (sBuySellType): RApi::sBUY_SELL_TYPE_BUY | RApi::sBUY_SELL_TYPE_SELL | RApi::sBUY_SELL_TYPE_SELL_SHORT | RApi::sBUY_SELL_TYPE_SELL_SHORT_EXEMPT

▶ sDuration  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::MarketOrderParams::sDuration

▶ sDuration  (text)
This member variable indicates the duration of the order to be placed. Not all values are supported by all exchanges, and new values may be introduced which are not part of this list.
For EUREX market orders, immediate or cancel is not supported, and specifying the sORDER_DURATION_IOC will return an OrderRejectReport .

▶ sDuration  (see_also)
See also (sDuration): RApi::sORDER_DURATION_DAY | RApi::sORDER_DURATION_FOK | RApi::sORDER_DURATION_IOC | RApi::sORDER_DURATION_GTC

▶ sEntryType  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::MarketOrderParams::sEntryType

▶ sEntryType  (text)
This member variable indicates whether this method is being called manually or automatically.

▶ sEntryType  (see_also)
See also (sEntryType): RApi::sORDER_ENTRY_TYPE_AUTO | RApi::sORDER_ENTRY_TYPE_MANUAL

▶ sExchange  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::MarketOrderParams::sExchange

▶ sExchange  (text)
This member variable specifies the exchange of the instrument on which to place the order.

▶ sRoutingInstructions  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::MarketOrderParams::sRoutingInstructions

▶ sRoutingInstructions  (text)
This member variable specifies the routing instructions.

▶ sTag  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::MarketOrderParams::sTag

▶ sTag  (text)
This optional value specifies a tag that is persisted with the resultant order by the infrastructure. If specified, this value will be present on subsequent order updates published by the infrastructure.
If this value is empty ( tsNCharcb::pData is NULL and tsNCharcb::iDataLen is zero), no tag will be set.
This value differs from OrderParams::sUserMsg in that it cannot be updated with subsequent order operations.
This value differs from OrderParams::sUserTag in that this value is used by R | Trader.

▶ sTag  (see_also)
See also (sTag): LineInfo::sTag | OrderReport::sTag | OrderParams::sTag | LimitOrderParams::sTag

▶ sTicker  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::MarketOrderParams::sTicker

▶ sTicker  (text)
This member variable specifies the symbol of the instrument on which to place the order.

▶ sTradeRoute  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::MarketOrderParams::sTradeRoute

▶ sTradeRoute  (text)
Specifies the trade route. Beginning with version 6.1.0.0 this parameter is required.
What are trade routes and how do I use them?

▶ sTradingAlgorithm  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::MarketOrderParams::sTradingAlgorithm

▶ sTradingAlgorithm  (text)
Some exchanges may require identification of which algorithm is submitting a particular order/cancel/modify request. This property provides a field for that purpose. In other words, it is an informational field for the exchange.

▶ sTradingAlgorithm  (see_also)
See also (sTradingAlgorithm): OrderParams::sTradingAlgorithm | LimitOrderParams::sTradingAlgorithm | MarketOrderParams::sTradingAlgorithm | ModifyOrderParams::sTradingAlgorithm | ModifyLimitOrderParams::sTradingAlgorithm | ModifyStopMarketOrderParams::sTradingAlgorithm | ModifyStopLimitOrderParams::sTradingAlgorithm | REngine::cancelOrder | REngine::cancelOrderList | LineInfo::sTradingAlgorithm

▶ sUserMsg  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::MarketOrderParams::sUserMsg

▶ sUserMsg  (text)
This optional variable specifies a user message that is persisted with the order request in the infrastructure. Unlike MarketOrderParams::pContext , this context string is not specific to an instance of the REngine . If this value is empty (sUserMsg.pData is NULL and sUserMsg.iDataLen is zero), then any existing user message ( LimitOrderParams::sUserMsg or ModifyOrderParams::sUserMsg ) will remain unmodified.

RApi::MidPriceInfo Class Reference
#include <[RApiPlus.h](RApiPlus_8h_source.html)>
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

── Public Member Functions ──

double  dPercentChange
int  iSsboe
int  iType
int  iUsecs
tsNCharcb  sExchange
tsNCharcb  sTicker

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::MidPriceInfo: This class represents mid price information.
If trade market data is unavailable, mid price information may be published in its stead. The mid price is the average of the best bid and best ask, i.e. (best bid price + best ask)/2. Some additional statistics on the mid price are calculated, such as the open, high, low and close mid prices.
To express interest in mid price data, one can specify the MD_MID_PRICE subscription flag when subscribing. The MidPriceInfo class is used to pass mid price info via RCallbacks::MidPrice() and the CloseMidPriceInfo class is used to convey close mid prices via RCallbacks::CloseMidPrice() .
See also: REngine::subscribe | RApi::MD_MID_PRICE | MidPriceInfo | RCallbacks::MidPrice | CloseMidPriceInfo | RCallbacks::CloseMidPrice

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ MidPriceInfo  (prototype)
Prototype: MidPriceInfo::MidPriceInfo()

▶ MidPriceInfo  (text)
This constructor initializes all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to NULL and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to zero. Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Boolean members are set to false.

Prototype: MidPriceInfo::iSsboe

▶ iSsboe  (text)
Timestamp in seconds since the beginning of epoch (Jan. 1, 1970). If the callback type is MD_UPDATE_CB, this timestamp indicates the receipt time of this message from the market data provider.
If the image type is MD_IMAGE_CB, the timestamp indicates that the data is current "as of" that time.

▶ iType  (prototype)
Prototype: MidPriceInfo::iType

▶ iType  (text)
Indicates if the trade is from a cache, the real-time data stream or from the historical database.
Possible values for this variable are

▶ iType  (see_also)
See also (iType): MD_IMAGE_CB | MD_UPDATE_CB | MD_HISTORY_CB

▶ iUsecs  (prototype)
Prototype: MidPriceInfo::iUsecs

▶ iUsecs  (text)
Microsecond component of the iSsboe timestamp

▶ sExchange  (prototype)
Prototype: MidPriceInfo::sExchange

▶ sExchange  (text)
Exchange of this instrument.

▶ sTicker  (prototype)
Prototype: MidPriceInfo::sTicker

▶ sTicker  (text)
Ticker symbol of this instrument.

Class RApi::ModifyLimitOrderParams: This class encapsulates information needed to modify a limit order.

▶ ~ModifyLimitOrderParams  [virtual]  (text)
This destructor will not deallocate memory associated with internal pointers such as the pData of any tsNCharcbs.

── Member Function Documentation ──

▶ clearHandles  (prototype)
Prototype: int ModifyLimitOrderParams::clearHandles( int * aiCode)

▶ clearHandles  (text)
This routine clears all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to NULL and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to zero. Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Boolean members are set to false. Pointer values are set to NULL. This method is used internally by the constructor.

── Member Data Documentation ──

▶ dPrice  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::ModifyLimitOrderParams::dPrice

▶ dPrice  (text)
This member variable indicates the desired price of the order to be modified. Not all order types require a price, such as variations of market orders.

▶ dPrice  (see_also)
See also (dPrice): RApi::sORDER_TYPE_MARKET | RApi::sORDER_TYPE_STOP_MARKET | RApi::sORDER_TYPE_MKT_IF_TOUCHED

▶ llQty  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::ModifyLimitOrderParams::llQty

▶ llQty  (text)
This member variable specifies the desired total quantity for the order, including fills that have already occurred for this order.
If the quantity specified is less than or equal to the current quantity filled, then the remaining quantity of the order is cancelled. The completion reason for the order will be Partially Filled, Balance Canceled (PFBC). If the quantity specified is greater than the quantity already filled, then the difference between the filled quantity and the quantity specified by ModifyLimitOrderParams::llQty will be the quantity working at the exchange.

▶ llQty  (see_also)
See also (llQty): RApi::sCOMPLETION_REASON_PFBC

▶ pAccount  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::ModifyLimitOrderParams::pAccount

▶ pAccount  (text)
This member variable specifies the account used to modify the order.

▶ pContext  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::ModifyLimitOrderParams::pContext

▶ pContext  (text)
This member variable specifies a user-defined context that will be passed with each order callback pertaining to this order. If this is not specified, any existing context associated with this order, if any, will be kept.

▶ sEntryType  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::ModifyLimitOrderParams::sEntryType

▶ sEntryType  (text)
This member variable indicates whether this method is being called manually or automatically.

▶ sEntryType  (see_also)
See also (sEntryType): RApi::sORDER_ENTRY_TYPE_AUTO | RApi::sORDER_ENTRY_TYPE_MANUAL

▶ sExchange  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::ModifyLimitOrderParams::sExchange

▶ sExchange  (text)
This member variable specifies the exchange of the original order to modify.

▶ sOrderNum  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::ModifyLimitOrderParams::sOrderNum

▶ sOrderNum  (text)
This member variable specifies the order number of the order to be modified.

▶ sTicker  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::ModifyLimitOrderParams::sTicker

▶ sTicker  (text)
This member variable specifies the symbol of the original order to modify.

▶ sTradingAlgorithm  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::ModifyLimitOrderParams::sTradingAlgorithm

▶ sTradingAlgorithm  (text)
Some exchanges may require identification of which algorithm is submitting a particular order/cancel/modify request. This property provides a field for that purpose. In other words, it is an informational field for the exchange.

▶ sTradingAlgorithm  (see_also)
See also (sTradingAlgorithm): OrderParams::sTradingAlgorithm | LimitOrderParams::sTradingAlgorithm | MarketOrderParams::sTradingAlgorithm | ModifyOrderParams::sTradingAlgorithm | ModifyLimitOrderParams::sTradingAlgorithm | ModifyStopMarketOrderParams::sTradingAlgorithm | ModifyStopLimitOrderParams::sTradingAlgorithm | REngine::cancelOrder | REngine::cancelOrderList | LineInfo::sTradingAlgorithm

▶ sUserMsg  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::ModifyLimitOrderParams::sUserMsg

▶ sUserMsg  (text)
This optional variable specifies a user message that is persisted with the order request in the infrastructure. Unlike MarketOrderParams::pContext , this context string is not specific to an instance of the REngine . If this value is empty (sUserMsg.pData is NULL and sUserMsg.iDataLen is zero), then any existing user message ( MarketOrderParams::sUserMsg or
LimitOrderParams::sUserMsg , or ModifyLimitOrderParams::sUserMsg ) will remain unmodified.

tsNCharcb  sTriggerTicker
tsNCharcb  sUserMsg
tsNCharcb  sWindowName

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::ModifyOrderParams: This class encapsulates information needed to modify an order.
If a trigger price is specified and the order type is MIT/LIT, then the trigger exchange and trigger ticker must be specified as well. The trigger price will be assumed to be a price for the trigger exch/ticker.
If a trigger price is specified and the order type is not MIT/LIT, then the trigger price is assumed to be a price for the exchange and ticker.
Changing other parameters of MIT/LIT orders is not supported. In these cases, the recommendation is to cancel and replace the MIT/LIT order.

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ bMinQty  (prototype)
Prototype: bool RApi::ModifyOrderParams::bMinQty

▶ bPrice  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::ModifyOrderParams::bPrice

▶ bPrice  (text)
This flag should be set to true when specifying a value for RApi::ModifyOrderParams::dPrice . If this flag is false, the value of RApi::ModifyOrderParams::dPrice will be ignored.

▶ bQty  (prototype)
Prototype: bool RApi::ModifyOrderParams::bQty

▶ bTrailByTicks  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::ModifyOrderParams::bTrailByTicks

▶ bTrailByTicks  (text)
If this is a stop or stop limit order, it is possible to have the stop price 'trail' the market. This variable specifies that the number of ticks to trail by should be changed. A stop order for which trailing stops is disabled cannot have the trailing functionality enabled by setting this flag to true.

▶ bTrailByTicks  (see_also)
See also (bTrailByTicks): RApi::ModifyOrderParams::iTrailByTicks

▶ bTriggerPrice  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::ModifyOrderParams::bTriggerPrice

▶ bTriggerPrice  (text)
This flag should be set to true when specifying a value for RApi::ModifyOrderParams::dTriggerPrice . If this flag is false, the value of RApi::ModifyOrderParams::dTriggerPrice will be ignored.

▶ dTriggerPrice  (prototype)

▶ dTriggerPrice  (text)

▶ dTriggerPrice  (see_also)

▶ iTrailByTicks  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::ModifyOrderParams::iTrailByTicks

▶ iTrailByTicks  (text)
If this is a stop or stop limit order, it is possible to have the stop price 'trail' the market. This variable specifies the number of ticks to trail by. This value is ignored if the ModifyOrderParams::bTrailByTicks is false.

▶ iTrailByTicks  (see_also)

Some exchanges may require identification of which algorithm is submitting a particular order/cancel/modify request. This property provides a field for that purpose. In other words, it is an informational field for the exchange.

▶ sTradingAlgorithm  (see_also)
See also (sTradingAlgorithm): OrderParams::sTradingAlgorithm | LimitOrderParams::sTradingAlgorithm | MarketOrderParams::sTradingAlgorithm | ModifyOrderParams::sTradingAlgorithm | ModifyLimitOrderParams::sTradingAlgorithm | ModifyStopMarketOrderParams::sTradingAlgorithm | ModifyStopLimitOrderParams::sTradingAlgorithm | REngine::cancelOrder | REngine::cancelOrderList | LineInfo::sTradingAlgorithm

▶ sUserMsg  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::ModifyStopLimitOrderParams::sUserMsg

▶ sUserMsg  (text)
This optional variable specifies a user message that is persisted with the order request in the infrastructure. Unlike MarketOrderParams::pContext , this context string is not specific to an instance of the REngine . If this value is empty (sUserMsg.pData is NULL and sUserMsg.iDataLen is zero), then any existing user message ( MarketOrderParams::sUserMsg or
LimitOrderParams::sUserMsg , or ModifyStopLimitOrderParams::sUserMsg ) will remain unmodified.

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::ModifyStopMarketOrderParams: This class encapsulates information needed to modify a stop limit order.

▶ ~ModifyStopMarketOrderParams  [virtual]  (text)
This destructor will not deallocate memory associated with internal pointers such as the pData of any tsNCharcbs.

── Member Function Documentation ──

▶ clearHandles  (prototype)
Prototype: int ModifyStopMarketOrderParams::clearHandles( int * aiCode)

▶ clearHandles  (text)
This routine clears all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to NULL and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to zero. Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Boolean members are set to false. Pointer values are set to NULL. This method is used internally by the constructor.

── Member Data Documentation ──

▶ dTriggerPrice  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::ModifyStopMarketOrderParams::dTriggerPrice

▶ dTriggerPrice  (text)
This member variable indicates the desired trigger price of the order to be modifid.

▶ llQty  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::ModifyStopMarketOrderParams::llQty

▶ llQty  (text)
This member variable specifies the desired total quantity for the order, including fills that have already occurred for this order.
If the quantity specified is less than or equal to the current quantity filled, then the remaining quantity of the order is cancelled. The completion reason for the order will be Partially Filled, Balance Canceled (PFBC). If the quantity specified is greater than the quantity already filled, then the difference between the filled quantity and the quantity specified by ModifyStopMarketOrderParams::llQty will be the quantity working at the exchange.

▶ llQty  (see_also)
See also (llQty): RApi::sCOMPLETION_REASON_PFBC

▶ pAccount  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::ModifyStopMarketOrderParams::pAccount

▶ pAccount  (text)
This member variable specifies the account used to modify the order.

▶ pContext  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::ModifyStopMarketOrderParams::pContext

▶ pContext  (text)
This member variable specifies a user-defined context that will be passed with each order callback pertaining to this order. If this is not specified, any existing context associated with this order, if any, will be kept.

▶ sEntryType  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::ModifyStopMarketOrderParams::sEntryType

▶ sEntryType  (text)
This member variable indicates whether this method is being called manually or automatically.

▶ sEntryType  (see_also)
See also (sEntryType): RApi::sORDER_ENTRY_TYPE_AUTO | RApi::sORDER_ENTRY_TYPE_MANUAL

▶ sExchange  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::ModifyStopMarketOrderParams::sExchange

▶ sExchange  (text)
This member variable specifies the exchange of the original order to modify.

▶ sOrderNum  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::ModifyStopMarketOrderParams::sOrderNum

▶ sOrderNum  (text)
This member variable specifies the order number of the order to be modifid.

▶ sTicker  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::ModifyStopMarketOrderParams::sTicker

▶ sTicker  (text)
This member variable specifies the symbol of the original order to modify.

▶ sTradingAlgorithm  (prototype)

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::OpeningIndicatorInfo: This class represents opening indicators.
The OpeningIndicatorInfo class is used to pass opening indicator price information received as a result of calls to REngine::subscribe() requests via the RCallbacks::OpeningIndicator() callback. Not all exchanges or instruments make use of opening indicators.

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

Prototype: OpeningIndicatorInfo::iSsboe

▶ iSsboe  (text)
Timestamp in seconds since the beginning of epoch (Jan. 1, 1970). If the callback type is MD_UPDATE_CB, this timestamp indicates the receipt time of this message from the market data provider.
If the image type is MD_IMAGE_CB, the timestamp indicates that the data is current "as of" that time.

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::OpenPriceInfo: This class represents open price information as published by an exchange.
The OpenPriceInfo class is used to present open price information. The method REngine::subscribe() with MD_OPEN in the subscription flags is used to express interest in receiving open prices. The callback used to pass that information is RCallbacks::OpenPrice() .

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ OpenPriceInfo  (prototype)
Prototype: OpenPriceInfo::OpenPriceInfo()

▶ OpenPriceInfo  (text)
This constructor initializes all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to MNM_EMPTY_DATA_ITEM and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to MNM_EMPTY_DATA_ITEM_LEN. Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Boolean members are set to false.

▶ ~OpenPriceInfo  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: OpenPriceInfo::~OpenPriceInfo() [virtual]

▶ ~OpenPriceInfo  [virtual]  (text)
This destructor will not deallocate memory associated with internal pointers such as the pData of any tsNCharcbs.

── Member Data Documentation ──

▶ bPriceFlag  (prototype)

▶ bPriceFlag  (text)
This member variable specifies if OpenPriceInfo::dPrice is defined or not. If the value is true, then the price is defined. If the value is false, the price is undefined. It is possible that a price is undefined if the exchange does not publish this information.

▶ dPrice  (prototype)
Prototype: OpenPriceInfo::dPrice

▶ dPrice  (text)

If the image type is MD_IMAGE_CB, the timestamp indicates that the data is current "as of" that time.

▶ iType  (prototype)
Prototype: OpenPriceInfo::iType

▶ iType  (text)
This member variable indicates if the open price is from a cache or the real-time data stream.
It is possible for exchanges to publish open prices intraday. Because of this, it is possible to receive a open price intraday, with an iType value of MD_UPDATE_CB.
Possible values for this variable are

▶ iType  (see_also)

── Public Attributes ──

tsNCharcb *  pExpirationCCYYMMArray
tsNCharcb *  pProductCodeArray
tsNCharcb  sRpCode

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::OptionListInfo: An instance of this class is the response to a call to REngine::getOptionList() .
An OptionListInfo object is used to pass :
• a list of exchanges,
• a list of product codes within an exchange,
• a list of expiration dates within a product code (within an exchange), or
• a list of RefDataInfo objects representing options within an expiration date
What is returned depends upon what was initially passed into REngine::getOptionList() .

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ OptionListInfo  (prototype)
Prototype: OptionListInfo::OptionListInfo()

▶ OptionListInfo  (text)
This constructor initializes all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to MNM_EMPTY_DATA_ITEM and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to MNM_EMPTY_DATA_ITEM_LEN. Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Boolean members are set to false. Pointers (and pointers to arrays) are set to NULL.

▶ ~OptionListInfo  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: OptionListInfo::~OptionListInfo() [virtual]

▶ ~OptionListInfo  [virtual]  (text)
This destructor will not deallocate memory associated with internal pointers such as the pData of any tsNCharcbs.

▶ pProductCodeArray  (prototype)
Prototype: OptionListInfo::pProductCodeArray

▶ pProductCodeArray  (text)

▶ sRpCode  (prototype)
Prototype: OptionListInfo::sRpCode

▶ sRpCode  (text)
The is a string equivalent of the response code. It may not be populated if the value of iRpCode is zero (API_OK).

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::OrderBustReport: The OrderBustReport represents a trade which has been busted and is passed in RCallbacks::BustReport() .
The OrderBustReport class conveys information about a trade bust.
See also: REngine::modifyOrder | REngine::sendOrder | REngine::sendOrderList

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ OrderBustReport  (prototype)
Prototype: OrderBustReport::OrderBustReport()

▶ OrderBustReport  (text)
This constructor initializes all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to NULL and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to zero. Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Boolean members are set to false. Pointer values are set to NULL.

▶ ~OrderBustReport  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: OrderBustReport::~OrderBustReport() [virtual]

▶ ~OrderBustReport  [virtual]  (text)
This destructor will not deallocate memory associated with internal pointers such as the pData of any tsNCharcbs.

── Member Function Documentation ──

▶ dump  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: int OrderBustReport::dump( int * aiCode) [virtual]

▶ dump  [virtual]  (text)
This routine will print the current values of the object to stdout. The precision of the output may not reflect the actual precision of the value. For example, doubles may only be printed to six decimal places, even if the value of the double has a greater significant precision.
It also calls the OrderReport::dump routine.
Reimplemented from RApi::OrderReport .

── Member Data Documentation ──

▶ sFillType  (prototype)
Prototype: tsNCharcb OrderBustReport::sFillType

▶ sFillType  (text)
If available, contains the fill type of this report. It is typically populated when the busted fill represents one leg of a spread. If this value is empty, this report pertains to a "regular" fill.

▶ sSettlementCurrency  (prototype)
Prototype: tsNCharcb OrderBustReport::sSettlementCurrency

▶ sSettlementCurrency  (text)
If available, contains the settlement currency associated with this bust.

▶ sSettlementDate  (prototype)
Prototype: tsNCharcb OrderBustReport::sSettlementDate

▶ sSettlementDate  (text)
If available, contains the settlement date associated with this bust.

▶ sTradeDate  (prototype)
Prototype: tsNCharcb OrderBustReport::sTradeDate

▶ sTradeDate  (text)
If available, the trade date associated with this report.

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::OrderCancelReport: The OrderCancelReport represents a cancellation of an order and is passed in RCallbacks::CancelReport() .
The OrderCancelReport class conveys information about a cancellation of a full or partial order, depending on what portion of the order could be cancelled.
See also: REngine::cancelOrder | REngine::cancelAllOrders

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ ~OrderCancelReport  [virtual]  (text)

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::OrderFailureReport: The OrderFailureReport represents a failure of an order operation to be placed at the exchange and is passed in RCallbacks::FailureReport() .
The OrderFailureReport callback is invoked when an order operation cannot be placed with the exchange. These operations include the placement of new orders and the cancellation or modification of existing orders.
See also: RCallbacks::FailureReport | REngine::cancelAllOrders | REngine::cancelOrder | REngine::modifyOrder | REngine::sendOrder | REngine::sendOrderList

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ OrderFailureReport  (text)
This constructor initializes all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to NULL and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to zero. Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Boolean members are set to false. Pointer values are set to NULL.

▶ ~OrderFailureReport  [virtual]  (prototype)

▶ ~OrderFailureReport  [virtual]  (text)
This destructor will not deallocate memory associated with internal pointers such as the pData of any tsNCharcbs.

── Member Function Documentation ──

It also calls the OrderReport::dump routine.

tsNCharcb  sTriggerTicker

tsNCharcb  sUserTag
tsNCharcb  sWindowName

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::OrderFillReport: The OrderFillReport represents a fill for an order and is passed in RCallbacks::FillReport() .
The OrderFillReport class conveys non-cumulative information about a full or partial fill for an order.
A fill report for a strategy (an instrument with multiple components like a calendar spread) will be followed by fill reports for each of the legs. The aggregate of these leg fill reports is equivalent to the fill report of the strategy. If price reference data is missing for any of these leg instruments, it is possible that the fill report callback for that leg instrument will not be invoked.
See also: REngine::modifyOrder | REngine::sendOrder | REngine::sendOrderList

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ OrderFillReport  (prototype)
Prototype: OrderFillReport::OrderFillReport()

▶ OrderFillReport  (text)
This constructor initializes all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to NULL and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to zero. Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Boolean members are set to false. Pointer values are set to NULL.

▶ ~OrderFillReport  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: OrderFillReport::~OrderFillReport() [virtual]

▶ ~OrderFillReport  [virtual]  (text)
This destructor will not deallocate memory associated with internal pointers such as the pData of any tsNCharcbs.

── Member Function Documentation ──

▶ clearHandles  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: int OrderFillReport::clearHandles( int * aiCode) [virtual]

▶ clearHandles  [virtual]  (text)
This routine will clear the current values of the object.
Reimplemented from RApi::OrderReport .

▶ dump  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: int OrderFillReport::dump( int * aiCode) [virtual]

▶ dump  [virtual]  (text)
This routine will print the current values of the object to stdout. The precision of the output may not reflect the actual precision of the value. For example, doubles may only be printed to six decimal places, even if the value of the double has a greater significant precision.
It also calls the OrderReport::dump routine.
Reimplemented from RApi::OrderReport .

── Member Data Documentation ──

▶ iRpCode  (prototype)
Prototype: int OrderFillReport::iRpCode

▶ iRpCode  (text)
Response code, if applicable, from the REngine::replayExecutions response.

▶ sFillType  (prototype)
Prototype: tsNCharcb OrderFillReport::sFillType

▶ sFillType  (text)
If available, the fill type of this fill. It is typically populated when the fill represents one leg of a spread. If this value is empty, this report pertains to a "regular" fill.

▶ sLiquidityIndicator  (prototype)
Prototype: tsNCharcb OrderFillReport::sLiquidityIndicator

▶ sLiquidityIndicator  (text)
If available, an indicator of whether this fill added or removed liquidity, or was routed.

▶ sQuoteId  (prototype)
Prototype: tsNCharcb OrderFillReport::sQuoteId

▶ sQuoteId  (text)
If available, the quote id associated with this fill. If/when quoting, fills can occur on the quotes. When replaying fills, those fills from quotes will be returned in the form of an OrderFillReport .

▶ sQuoteId  (see_also)
See also (sQuoteId): REngine::replayQuotes() | REngine::replayExecutions() | RCallbacks::executionReplay() | ExecutionReplayInfo

▶ sQuoteMsgId  (prototype)
Prototype: tsNCharcb OrderFillReport::sQuoteMsgId

▶ sQuoteMsgId  (text)
If available, the quote msg id associated with this fill. If/when quoting, fills can occur on the quotes. When replaying fills, those fills from quotes will be returned in the fmorm of an OrderFillReport .

▶ sQuoteMsgId  (see_also)
See also (sQuoteMsgId): REngine::replayQuotes

▶ sRpCode  (prototype)

▶ sRpCode  (text)

▶ sSettlementCurrency  (text)

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::OrderHistoryDatesInfo: An instance of this class represents the dates in which order history is available.
An OrderHistoryDatesInfo object is used to pass an array of tsNCharcbs via RCallbacks::OrderHistoryDates() .

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ OrderHistoryDatesInfo  (prototype)
Prototype: OrderHistoryDatesInfo::OrderHistoryDatesInfo()

▶ OrderHistoryDatesInfo  (text)
This constructor initializes all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to NULL and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to zero. Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Boolean members are set to false. Pointers (and pointers to arrays) are set to NULL.

▶ ~OrderHistoryDatesInfo  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: OrderHistoryDatesInfo::~OrderHistoryDatesInfo() [virtual]

▶ ~OrderHistoryDatesInfo  [virtual]  (text)
This destructor will not deallocate memory associated with internal pointers such as the pData of any tsNCharcbs or array elements.

── Member Function Documentation ──

▶ dump  (prototype)
Prototype: int OrderHistoryDatesInfo::dump( int * aiCode)

▶ dump  (text)
This routine prints the values of the OrderHistoryDatesInfo object to stdout. If a tsNCharcb has an iDataLen value of zero, then the string "<empty>" will be printed.

── Member Data Documentation ──

▶ asDateArray  (prototype)
Prototype: OrderHistoryDatesInfo::asDateArray

▶ asDateArray  (text)
This is an array of tsNCharcb strings, each representing a date. The array length is specified by OrderHistoryDatesInfo::iArrayLen .

▶ iArrayLen  (prototype)
Prototype: OrderHistoryDatesInfo::iArrayLen

▶ iArrayLen  (text)
This variable specifies the array length.

▶ iRpCode  (prototype)
Prototype: OrderHistoryDatesInfo::iRpCode

▶ iRpCode  (text)
The response code, if available, associated with the request REngine::listOrderHistoryDates() . A non-zero response code can be interpreted as an error.

▶ pContext  (prototype)
Prototype: OrderHistoryDatesInfo::pContext

▶ pContext  (text)
The context initially sent with REngine::listOrderHistoryDates() .

▶ sRpCode  (prototype)
Prototype: OrderHistoryDatesInfo::sRpCode

▶ sRpCode  (text)
The is a string equivalent of the response code. It may not be populated if the value of iRpCode is zero (API_OK).

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::OrderModifyReport: The OrderModifyReport represents the successful modification of an existing order and is passed in RCallbacks::ModifyReport() .
The OrderModifyReport class conveys information about an order as it stands after a successful modification.
See also: REngine::modifyOrder | REngine::sendOrder | REngine::sendOrderList

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

── Member Function Documentation ──

▶ dump  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: int OrderModifyReport::dump( int * aiCode) [virtual]

▶ dump  [virtual]  (text)
This routine will print the current values of the object to stdout. The precision of the output may not reflect the actual precision of the value. For example, doubles may only be printed to six decimal places, even if the value of the double has a greater significant precision.
It also calls the OrderReport::dump routine.

── Member Data Documentation ──

▶ llNewSize  (prototype)
Prototype: OrderModifyReport::llNewSize

▶ llNewSize  (text)
This quantity is the value as provided by the exchange upon a successful modification of an order. Because exchange conventions may differ regarding quantities already filled against this order (remaining working quantity vs. total quantity of the order), it may be useful to inspect OrderReport::llTotalFilled and OrderReport::llTotalUnfilled .

▶ llNewSize  (see_also)
See also (llNewSize): OrderReport::llTotalFilled | OrderReport::llTotalUnfilled
