── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::OrderNotModifiedReport: The OrderNotModifiedReport represents the failure of a request to modify an existing order and is passed in RCallbacks::NotModifiedReport() .
The OrderNotModifiedReport class conveys information about the failure to modify an order.
See also: REngine::modifyOrder

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ OrderNotModifiedReport  (prototype)
Prototype: OrderNotModifiedReport::OrderNotModifiedReport()

▶ OrderNotModifiedReport  (text)
This constructor initializes all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to NULL and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to zero. Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Boolean members are set to false. Pointer values are set to NULL.

▶ ~OrderNotModifiedReport  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: OrderNotModifiedReport::~OrderNotModifiedReport() [virtual]

▶ ~OrderNotModifiedReport  [virtual]  (text)
This destructor will not deallocate memory associated with internal pointers such as the pData of any tsNCharcbs.

── Member Function Documentation ──

▶ dump  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: int OrderNotModifiedReport::dump( int * aiCode) [virtual]

▶ dump  [virtual]  (text)
This routine will print the current values of the object to stdout. The precision of the output may not reflect the actual precision of the value. For example, doubles may only be printed to six decimal places, even if the value of the double has a greater significant precision.
It also calls the OrderReport::dump routine.
Reimplemented from RApi::OrderReport .

── Member Data Documentation ──

▶ llNotModifiedSize  (prototype)
Prototype: OrderNotModifiedReport::llNotModifiedSize

▶ llNotModifiedSize  (text)
Quantity that could not be modified.

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::OrderParams: This class encapsulates information needed to submit an order.

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

── Member Function Documentation ──

▶ clearHandles  (prototype)
Prototype: int OrderParams::clearHandles( int * aiCode)

▶ clearHandles  (text)
This routine clears all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to NULL and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to zero. Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Boolean members are set to false. Pointer values are set to NULL. This method is used internally by the constructor.

── Member Data Documentation ──

▶ bCancelAfter  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::OrderParams::bCancelAfter

▶ bCancelAfter  (text)
This member variable specifies whether OrderParams::iCancelAfter should be specified when placing an order.

▶ bCancelSsboe  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::OrderParams::bCancelSsboe

▶ bCancelSsboe  (text)
Indicates whether the seconds portion of a 'cancel at' timestamp (in seconds since the beginning of epoch) is specified when placing an order.

▶ bReleaseCondition  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::OrderParams::bReleaseCondition

▶ bReleaseCondition  (text)
Setting this flag to true enables the server-side release condition mechanism for this order. When the specified market condition is met, the order is released. The market condition has a price component as well as a size component.

▶ bReleaseCondition  (see_also)
See also (bReleaseCondition): RApi::OrderParams::iReleaseConditionPriceId | RApi::OrderParams::iReleaseConditionPriceCmpOperator | RApi::OrderParams::dReleaseConditionPrice | RApi::OrderParams::iReleaseConditionSizeId | RApi::OrderParams::iReleaseConditionSizeCmpOperator | RApi::OrderParams::llReleaseConditionSize

▶ bReleaseSsboe  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::OrderParams::bReleaseSsboe

▶ bReleaseSsboe  (text)
This member variable specifies whether a release timestamp (in seconds since the beginning of epoch) should be specified when placing an order.

▶ bReleaseUsecs  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::OrderParams::bReleaseUsecs

▶ bReleaseUsecs  (text)
This member variable specifies whether the microseconds portion of a release timestamp should be specified when placing an order.

▶ bToMarketAfterPartialFillInSeconds  (prototype)
Prototype: bool OrderParams::bToMarketAfterPartialFillInSeconds

▶ bToMarketAfterPartialFillInSeconds  (text)
Flag indicating whether OrderParams::iToMarketAfterPartialFillInSeconds should be used. If this value is false, the value in OrderParams::iToMarketAfterPartialFillInSeconds will be ignored.

▶ bToMarketAfterPartialFillInSeconds  (see_also)
See also (bToMarketAfterPartialFillInSeconds): OrderParams::iToMarketAfterPartialFillInSeconds | LineInfo::bToMarketAfterPartialFillInSeconds | LineInfo::iToMarketAfterPartialFillInSeconds

▶ bToMarketAt  (prototype)
Prototype: bool OrderParams::bToMarketAt

▶ bToMarketAt  (text)
Flag indicating whether OrderParams::iToMarketAtSsboe and OrderParams::iToMarketAtUsecs should be used. If this value is false, OrderParams::iToMarketAtSsboe and OrderParams::iToMarketAtUsecs will be ignored.
Using this setting configures this order to be converted to a market order at the specified time. Market-if-touched, limit-if-touched, and market orders cannot make use of this feature and will be regarded as 'bad input'.

▶ bToMarketAt  (see_also)
See also (bToMarketAt): OrderParams::sOrderType | OrderParams::bToMarketAt | OrderParams::iToMarketAtSsboe | OrderParams::iToMarketAtUsecs | LineInfo::bToMarketAt | LineInfo::iToMarketAtSsboe | LineInfo::iToMarketAtUsecs

▶ bTrailingStop  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::OrderParams::bTrailingStop

▶ bTrailingStop  (text)
If this is a stop or stop limit order, it is possible to have the stop price 'trail' the market. This variable specifies whether to enable trailing stops for the order. It is ignored for other order types.

▶ bTrailingStop  (see_also)
See also (bTrailingStop): RApi::BEST_ASK_PRICE | RApi::BEST_BID_PRICE | RApi::TRADE_PRICE | RApi::OrderParams::iTrailByPriceId | RApi::OrderParams::iTrailByTicks | RApi::OrderParams::sOrderType | RApi::sORDER_TYPE_STOP_LIMIT | RApi::sORDER_TYPE_STOP_MARKET

▶ dPrice  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::OrderParams::dPrice

▶ dPrice  (text)
This member variable indicates the limit price of the order to be placed. It is ignored for market, stop market and market if touched orders.

▶ iCancelAfter  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::OrderParams::iCancelAfter

▶ iCancelAfter  (text)
This member variable specifies the whole number of seconds when the order is to be canceled after the order is open. This value is only used if OrderParams::bCancelAfter is true. Otherwise it is ignored.

▶ iReleaseSsboe  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::OrderParams::iReleaseSsboe

▶ iReleaseSsboe  (text)
This member variable specifies the release timestamp that is submited if OrderParams::bReleaseSsboe is true. Otherwise this value is ignored. If this value is specified, the order manager within the infrastructure will wait until the specified time before sending the order to the exchange. This parameter cannot be used in conjunction with OrderParams::sReleaseCommand .

▶ iReleaseUsecs  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::OrderParams::iReleaseUsecs

▶ iReleaseUsecs  (text)
This member variable specifies the microseconds portion of the release timestamp that is submited if OrderParams::bReleaseUsecs is true.
Otherwise this value is ignored.

▶ iToMarketAfterPartialFillInSeconds  (prototype)

▶ iToMarketAfterPartialFillInSeconds  (text)
Number of seconds after a partial fill of this order in which this order is configured to be converted to a market order.

▶ iToMarketAfterPartialFillInSeconds  (see_also)

▶ iToMarketAtSsboe  (prototype)

▶ iTrailByPriceId  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::OrderParams::iTrailByPriceId

▶ iTrailByPriceId  (text)
If this is a stop or stop limit order, it is possible to have the stop price 'trail' the market. If trailing stops are enabled, this variable specifies which price to trail (last trade, best bid or best ask). It is ignored for other order types.

▶ iTrailByPriceId  (see_also)
See also (iTrailByPriceId): RApi::BEST_ASK_PRICE | RApi::BEST_BID_PRICE | RApi::TRADE_PRICE | RApi::OrderParams::bTrailingStop | RApi::OrderParams::iTrailByTicks | RApi::OrderParams::sOrderType | RApi::sORDER_TYPE_STOP_LIMIT | RApi::sORDER_TYPE_STOP_MARKET

▶ iTrailByTicks  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::OrderParams::iTrailByTicks

▶ iTrailByTicks  (text)
If this is a stop or stop limit order, it is possible to have the stop price 'trail' the market. If trailing stops are enabled, this variable specifies the number of ticks to trail by. It is ignored for other order types.

▶ iTrailByTicks  (see_also)
See also (iTrailByTicks): RApi::OrderParams::bTrailingStop | RApi::OrderParams::iTrailByPriceId | RApi::OrderParams::sOrderType | RApi::sORDER_TYPE_STOP_LIMIT | RApi::sORDER_TYPE_STOP_MARKET

▶ iTriggerCmpOperator  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::OrderParams::iTriggerCmpOperator

▶ iTriggerCmpOperator  (text)
This value indicates the operator to be used when evaluating the the trigger condition of a market if touched or limit if touched order. It is only used for market if touched and limit if touched orders.

▶ iTriggerCmpOperator  (see_also)
See also (iTriggerCmpOperator): RApi::OP_NONE | RApi::OP_EQUAL_TO | RApi::OP_LESS_THAN | RApi::OP_GREATER_THAN | RApi::OP_LESS_THAN_OR_EQUAL_TO | RApi::OP_GREATER_THAN_OR_EQUAL_TO | RApi::OP_NOT_EQUAL_TO | RApi::OP_NOT_GREATER_THAN | RApi::OP_NOT_LESS_THAN | RApi::sORDER_TYPE_LMT_IF_TOUCHED | RApi::sORDER_TYPE_MKT_IF_TOUCHED

▶ iTriggerPriceId  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::OrderParams::iTriggerPriceId

▶ iTriggerPriceId  (text)
This value indicates the type of price to be used when evaluating the the trigger condition. It is only used for market if touched and limit if touched orders.

▶ iTriggerPriceId  (see_also)
See also (iTriggerPriceId): RApi::BEST_ASK_PRICE | RApi::BEST_BID_PRICE | RApi::TRADE_PRICE | RApi::sORDER_TYPE_LMT_IF_TOUCHED | RApi::sORDER_TYPE_MKT_IF_TOUCHED

▶ llMaxShowQty  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::OrderParams::llMaxShowQty

▶ llMaxShowQty  (text)
This member variable specifies the max show quantity that is submited if OrderParams::bMaxShowQty is true. Otherwise this value is ignored.

▶ llMinQty  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::OrderParams::llMinQty

▶ llMinQty  (text)
This member variable indicates the minimum quantity of the order to be placed. Different exchanges may treat this value differently.
For CME orders, the minimum quantity is only considered for orders using a duration of RApi::sORDER_DURATION_IOC . In this case, setting the minimum quantity equal to the order quantity simulates a FAK-style order.
For EUREX orders, the minimum quantity is not considered.

▶ llQty  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::OrderParams::llQty

▶ llQty  (text)
This member variable indicates the quantity of the order to be placed.

▶ llReleaseConditionSize  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::OrderParams::llReleaseConditionSize

▶ llReleaseConditionSize  (text)
Specifies the size to compare, if the release condition is enabled.

▶ llReleaseConditionSize  (see_also)
See also (llReleaseConditionSize): RApi::OrderParams::bReleaseCondition

▶ pAccount  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::OrderParams::pAccount

▶ pAccount  (text)
This member variable specifies the account on which to place the order. When placing orders AccountInfo::RmsInfo can be NULL.
NOTE : If there are multiple orders being sent via REngine::sendOrderList() , the value of OrderParams::pAccount of the first element in the array will be used for all orders.

▶ pContext  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::OrderParams::pContext

▶ pContext  (text)
This member variable specifies a user-defined context that will be passed with each order callback pertaining to this order. This pointer is specific to each instance of REngine .

▶ sBuySellType  (prototype)

▶ sRoutingInstructions  (text)

▶ sTag  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::OrderParams::sTag

▶ sTag  (text)
This optional value specifies a tag that is persisted with the resultant order by the infrastructure. If specified, this value will be present on subsequent order updates published by the infrastructure.
If this value is empty ( tsNCharcb::pData is NULL and tsNCharcb::iDataLen is zero), no tag will be set.
This value differs from OrderParams::sUserMsg in that it cannot be updated with subsequent order operations.
This value differs from OrderParams::sUserTag in that this value is used by R | Trader.

▶ sTag  (see_also)
See also (sTag): LineInfo::sTag | OrderReport::sTag | LimitOrderParams::sTag | MarketOrderParams::sTag

▶ sTicker  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::OrderParams::sTicker

▶ sTicker  (text)
This member variable specifies the symbol of the instrument on which to place the order.

▶ sToMarketOnReject  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::OrderParams::sToMarketOnReject

▶ sToMarketOnReject  (text)
Indicates that this order should be converted to a market order if rejected by the exchange.
A value of "yes" enables this feature; otherwise leave empty.
If an order fails due to something other than rejection by the exchange (i.e. risk validation failure), the conversion will not happen.

▶ sToMarketOnReject  (see_also)
See also (sToMarketOnReject): LineInfo::sToMarketOnReject | OrderRejectReport::bReplacementOrderToFollow

▶ sToMarketWhenLimitPriceTouched  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::OrderParams::sToMarketWhenLimitPriceTouched

▶ sToMarketWhenLimitPriceTouched  (text)
Indicates that this order should be converted to a market order when the instrument's trade price touches this order's limit price.
A value of "yes" enables this feature; otherwise leave empty.

▶ sToMarketWhenLimitPriceTouched  (see_also)
See also (sToMarketWhenLimitPriceTouched): LineInfo::sToMarketWhenLimitPriceTouched

▶ sTradeRoute  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::OrderParams::sTradeRoute

▶ sTradeRoute  (text)
Specifies the trade route. Beginning with version 6.1.0.0 this parameter is required.
What are trade routes and how do I use them?

▶ sTradingAlgorithm  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::OrderParams::sTradingAlgorithm

▶ sTradingAlgorithm  (text)
Some exchanges may require identification of which algorithm is submitting a particular order/cancel/modify request. This property provides a field for that purpose. In other words, it is an informational field for the exchange.

▶ sTradingAlgorithm  (see_also)
See also (sTradingAlgorithm): OrderParams::sTradingAlgorithm | LimitOrderParams::sTradingAlgorithm | MarketOrderParams::sTradingAlgorithm | ModifyOrderParams::sTradingAlgorithm | ModifyLimitOrderParams::sTradingAlgorithm | ModifyStopMarketOrderParams::sTradingAlgorithm | ModifyStopLimitOrderParams::sTradingAlgorithm | REngine::cancelOrder | REngine::cancelOrderList | LineInfo::sTradingAlgorithm

▶ sTriggerExchange  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::OrderParams::sTriggerExchange

▶ sTriggerExchange  (text)
This member variable specifies the exchange of associated with the trigger condition. It is only used for market if touched and limit if touched orders.

▶ sTriggerExchange  (see_also)
See also (sTriggerExchange): RApi::sORDER_TYPE_LMT_IF_TOUCHED | RApi::sORDER_TYPE_MKT_IF_TOUCHED

▶ sTriggerTicker  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::OrderParams::sTriggerTicker

▶ sTriggerTicker  (text)
This member variable specifies the symbol of associated with the trigger condition. It is only used for market if touched and limit if touched orders.

▶ sTriggerTicker  (see_also)
See also (sTriggerTicker): RApi::sORDER_TYPE_LMT_IF_TOUCHED | RApi::sORDER_TYPE_MKT_IF_TOUCHED

▶ sUserMsg  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::OrderParams::sUserMsg

▶ sUserMsg  (text)
This optional value specifies a user message that is persisted with the resultant order by the infrastructure. If specified, this value will be present on subsequent order updates published by the infrastructure.
If this value is empty ( tsNCharcb::pData is NULL and tsNCharcb::iDataLen is zero), no user msg will be set.
Note : The value can be updated with any subsequent order operation including other instances of R | API and R | Trader.

▶ sUserMsg  (see_also)
See also (sUserMsg): LineInfo::sUserMsg | OrderReport::sUserMsg | LimitOrderParams::sUserMsg | MarketOrderParams::sUserMsg | ModifyOrderParams::sUserMsg | REngine::cancelOrder() | REngine::cancelOrderList()

▶ sUserTag  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::OrderParams::sUserTag

▶ sUserTag  (text)
This optional value specifies a user tag that is persisted with the resultant order by the infrastructure. If specified, this value will be present on subsequent order updates published by the infrastructure.
If this value is empty ( tsNCharcb::pData is NULL and tsNCharcb::iDataLen is zero), no user tag will be set.
This value differs from OrderParams::sUserMsg in that it cannot be updated with subsequent order operations. It can, however, be updated by calling REngine::modifyOrderRefData() .
This value differs from OrderParams::sTag in that it is not used by R | Trader. The intent of OrderParams::sUserTag is to allow an end user of R | Trader (or perhaps an application written on top of R | API) to specify a tag that is visible to other clients of the trading platform.

▶ sUserTag  (see_also)
See also (sUserTag): LineInfo::sUserTag | OrderReport::sUserTag | REngine::modifyOrderRefData

▶ sWindowName  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::OrderParams::sWindowName

▶ sWindowName  (text)
This optional value specifies the window or widget name from which this new order request originated. If specified, this value can appear on subsequent order updates in either the WindowName or OriginatorWindowName field. When placing a list of orders, only the first value of WindowName will be used for all orders in the list.

▶ sWindowName  (see_also)
See also (sWindowName): LineInfo::sOriginatorWindowName | LineInfo::sWindowName | OrderReport::sOriginatorWindowName | OrderReport::sWindowName

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::OrderRejectReport: The OrderRejectReport represents the rejection of a request to place an order and is passed in RCallbacks::RejectReport() .
The OrderRejectReport class conveys information about the rejection.
See also: REngine::modifyOrder | REngine::sendOrder | REngine::sendOrderList

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ OrderRejectReport  (prototype)
Prototype: OrderRejectReport::OrderRejectReport()

▶ OrderRejectReport  (text)
This constructor initializes all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to NULL and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to zero. Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Boolean members are set to false. Pointer values are set to NULL.

▶ ~OrderRejectReport  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: OrderRejectReport::~OrderRejectReport() [virtual]

▶ ~OrderRejectReport  [virtual]  (text)
This destructor will not deallocate memory associated with internal pointers such as the pData of any tsNCharcbs.

── Member Function Documentation ──

▶ dump  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: int OrderRejectReport::dump( int * aiCode) [virtual]

▶ dump  [virtual]  (text)
This routine will print the current values of the object to stdout. The precision of the output may not reflect the actual precision of the value. For example, doubles may only be printed to six decimal places, even if the value of the double has a greater significant precision.
It also calls the OrderReport::dump routine.
Reimplemented from RApi::OrderReport .

── Member Data Documentation ──

▶ bReplacementOrderToFollow  (prototype)

▶ bReplacementOrderToFollow  (text)
Indicates if there is a replacement order to follow this order's rejection.

▶ bReplacementOrderToFollow  (see_also)
See also (bReplacementOrderToFollow): OrderParams::sToMarketOnReject | LineInfo::sToMarketOnReject

tsNCharcb  sDate
tsNCharcb  sRpCode

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::OrderReplayInfo: An instance of this class represents a collection of orders. Each order will be represented by an LineInfo instance.
The OrderReplayInfo class is used to pass a collection of orders via RCallbacks::OrderReplay() or RCallbacks::OpenOrderReplay() .
See also: REngine::replayAllOrders() | REngine::replayHistoricalOrders() | REngine::replayOpenOrders() | RCallbacks::OpenOrderReplay() | RCallbacks::OrderReplay()

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ OrderReplayInfo  (prototype)
Prototype: OrderReplayInfo::OrderReplayInfo()

▶ OrderReplayInfo  (text)
This constructor initializes all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to NULL and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to zero. Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Boolean members are set to false. Pointer values are set to NULL.

▶ ~OrderReplayInfo  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: OrderReplayInfo::~OrderReplayInfo() [virtual]

▶ ~OrderReplayInfo  [virtual]  (text)
This destructor will not deallocate memory associated with internal pointers such as the pData of any tsNCharcbs.

── Member Function Documentation ──

▶ dump  (prototype)

▶ dump  (text)
This routine will print the current values of the object to stdout. The precision of the output may not reflect the actual precision of the value. For example, doubles may only be printed to six decimal places, even if the value of the double has a greater significant precision.

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::OrderReport: The OrderReport represents basic information about an order.
The OrderReport class is the base class for other Order*Report classes, It is passed in RCallbacks::OrderOtherReport().

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ OrderReport  (prototype)
Prototype: OrderReport::OrderReport()

▶ OrderReport  (text)
This constructor initializes all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to NULL and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to zero. Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Boolean members are set to false. Pointer values are set to NULL.

▶ ~OrderReport  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: OrderReport::~OrderReport() [virtual]

▶ ~OrderReport  [virtual]  (text)
This destructor will not deallocate memory associated with internal pointers such as the pData of any tsNCharcbs.

── Member Function Documentation ──

▶ clearHandles  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: int OrderReport::clearHandles( int * aiCode) [virtual]

▶ clearHandles  [virtual]  (text)
This routine will clear the current values of the object.
Reimplemented in RApi::OrderFillReport .

▶ dump  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: int OrderReport::dump( int * aiCode) [virtual]

▶ dump  [virtual]  (text)
This routine will print the current values of the object to stdout. The precision of the output may not reflect the actual precision of the value. For example, doubles may only be printed to six decimal places, even if the value of the double has a greater significant precision.
Reimplemented in RApi::OrderBustReport , RApi::OrderCancelReport , RApi::OrderFailureReport , RApi::OrderFillReport , RApi::OrderModifyReport , RApi::OrderNotCancelledReport , RApi::OrderNotModifiedReport , RApi::OrderRejectReport , RApi::OrderStatusReport , RApi::OrderTradeCorrectReport , RApi::OrderTriggerPulledReport , and RApi::OrderTriggerReport .

── Member Data Documentation ──

▶ bAvgFillPriceFlag  (prototype)
Prototype: bool OrderReport::bAvgFillPriceFlag

▶ bAvgFillPriceFlag  (text)
Flag indicating whether OrderReport::dAvgFillPrice contains a valid value. This flag should be checked for a value of true before using OrderReport::dAvgFillPrice .

▶ bFillPriceFlag  (prototype)
Prototype: bool OrderReport::bFillPriceFlag

▶ bFillPriceFlag  (text)
Flag indicating whether OrderReport::dFillPrice contains a valid value. This flag should be checked for a value of true before using the value of OrderReport::dFillPrice .

▶ bPriceToFillFlag  (prototype)
Prototype: bool OrderReport::bPriceToFillFlag

▶ bPriceToFillFlag  (text)
Flag indicating whether OrderReport::dPriceToFill contains a valid value. This flag should be checked for a value of true before using the value of OrderReport::dPriceToFill .

▶ bTriggerPriceFlag  (prototype)
Prototype: bool OrderReport::bTriggerPriceFlag

▶ bTriggerPriceFlag  (text)
Flag indicating whether OrderReport::dTriggerPrice contains a valid value. This flag should be checked for a value of true before using the value of OrderReport::dTriggerPrice .

▶ dAvgFillPrice  (prototype)
Prototype: double OrderReport::dAvgFillPrice

▶ dAvgFillPrice  (text)
Average price at which fills have occurred for this order.

▶ dFillPrice  (prototype)
Prototype: double OrderReport::dFillPrice

▶ dFillPrice  (text)
Fill price associated with this order report. The order report may be for a fill ( OrderFillReport ), but it may not.

▶ dPriceToFill  (prototype)
Prototype: double OrderReport::dPriceToFill

▶ dPriceToFill  (text)
Price associated with the order that has been placed.

▶ dTriggerPrice  (prototype)
Prototype: double OrderReport::dTriggerPrice

▶ dTriggerPrice  (text)
Trigger price associated with the order that has been placed.

▶ iConnId  (prototype)
Prototype: int OrderReport::iConnId

▶ iConnId  (text)
This identifies the connection on which this report was received. It is possible when using the R | Diamond Cutter connect point, certain order report callbacks will be invoked twice, once on the trading system connect point, and once on the R | Diamond Cutter connect point. This indicator provides a mechanism to distinguish between the two.

▶ iGatewayBufferBytesBehind  (prototype)
Prototype: int OrderReport::iGatewayBufferBytesBehind

▶ iGatewayBufferBytesBehind  (text)
The size (in bytes) of the gateway's output buffer to the client, taken at the time this message left the infrastructure.
A non-zero value implies that the gateway is trying to send data to the client, but the client is not consuming the data quickly enough. This situation might be caused by insufficient network bandwidth between the gateway and the R | API client, or insufficient resources to process the data stream within the client process.
A value of zero means there is no output buffered, or the data is unavailable.
It will not be populated for historical/replayed data.
NOTE : This data is experimental and may not be available in future releases.

▶ iGatewaySsboe  (prototype)
Prototype: int OrderReport::iGatewaySsboe

▶ iGatewaySsboe  (text)
If not zero, the time at the gateway as the OrderReport leaves the infrastructure. This value is in seconds since the beginning of epoch (Jan. 1, 1970). It will not be populated for historical/replayed data.
NOTE : This data is experimental and may not be available in future releases.

▶ iGatewayUsecs  (prototype)
Prototype: int OrderReport::iGatewayUsecs

▶ iGatewayUsecs  (text)
If not zero, the microsecond portion of the gateway timestamp. It will not be populated for historical/replayed data.
NOTE : This data is experimental and may not be available in future releases.

▶ iSourceNsecs  (prototype)
Prototype: int OrderReport::iSourceNsecs

▶ iSourceNsecs  (text)
Nanosecond portion of the source timestamp

▶ iSourceNsecs  (see_also)
See also (iSourceNsecs): OrderReport::iSourceSsboe

▶ iSourceSsboe  (prototype)
Prototype: int OrderReport::iSourceSsboe

▶ iSourceSsboe  (text)
Timestamp, as provided by the exchange, in seconds since the beginning of epoch (Jan. 1, 1970)

▶ llTotalFilled  (prototype)
Prototype: OrderReport::llTotalFilled

▶ llTotalFilled  (text)
The total quantity filled for this order as of the timestamp. This value reflects the Rithmic order manager's state of this order.

▶ llTotalUnfilled  (prototype)
Prototype: OrderReport::llTotalUnfilled

▶ llTotalUnfilled  (text)
The total quantity still unfilled for this order as of the timestamp. This value reflects the Rithmic order manager's state of this order.

Account associated with this order.

A pointer that was passed when sending or updating this order. This context is order specific.

▶ sBuySellType  (prototype)
Prototype: tsNCharcb OrderReport::sBuySellType

▶ sBuySellType  (text)
String indicating whether the order is a buy or a sell, including some variations.

▶ sBuySellType  (see_also)
See also (sBuySellType): sBUY_SELL_TYPE_BUY | sBUY_SELL_TYPE_SELL | sBUY_SELL_TYPE_SELL_SHORT | sBUY_SELL_TYPE_SELL_SHORT_EXEMPT

▶ sCurrentSequenceNumber  (prototype)
Prototype: tsNCharcb OrderReport::sCurrentSequenceNumber

▶ sCurrentSequenceNumber  (text)
This is the sequence number assigned to this order by the infrastructure.
This value may change over the life of the order. Also, it is possible that this value is used in conjuction with, or in place of the OrderReport::sExchOrdId , by the exchange to identify the order.

▶ sEntryType  (prototype)
Prototype: tsNCharcb OrderReport::sEntryType

▶ sEntryType  (text)
The entry type indicates whether the most recent order operation was initiated automatically or manually.

▶ sEntryType  (see_also)
See also (sEntryType): sORDER_ENTRY_TYPE_AUTO | sORDER_ENTRY_TYPE_MANUAL

▶ sExchange  (prototype)
Prototype: tsNCharcb OrderReport::sExchange

▶ sExchange  (text)
The exchange of the instrument associated with this order.

▶ sExchOrdId  (prototype)
Prototype: tsNCharcb OrderReport::sExchOrdId

▶ sExchOrdId  (text)
This is the order id assigned by the exchange for this order.

▶ sInitialSequenceNumber  (prototype)
Prototype: tsNCharcb OrderReport::sInitialSequenceNumber

▶ sInitialSequenceNumber  (text)
This is the (correlation) sequence number assigned to this order by the infrastructure when the order is placed. It typically will not change over the life of the order. It is possible that an exchange will need this value, and perhaps other values (such as OrderReport::sCurrentSequenceNumber or OrderReport::sExchOrdId ) to identify an order.

▶ sLinkedOrderNums  (prototype)
Prototype: tsNCharcb OrderReport::sLinkedOrderNums

▶ sLinkedOrderNums  (text)
Contains a comma-delimited list of order numbers representing the set of linked orders of which this order is a member. Linked orders are often profit/stop pairs for exiting a position.

▶ sLinkedOrderNums  (see_also)
See also (sLinkedOrderNums): REngine::linkOrders() | REngine::sendOcaList() | REngine::sendBracketOrder() | LineInfo::sLinkedOrderNums

▶ sOmnibusAccount  (prototype)
Prototype: tsNCharcb OrderReport::sOmnibusAccount

▶ sOmnibusAccount  (text)
If available, this value contains the exchange account pertaining to the order.

▶ sOrderDuration  (prototype)
Prototype: tsNCharcb OrderReport::sOrderDuration

▶ sOrderDuration  (text)
String indicating the order duration of this order.

▶ sOrderDuration  (see_also)
See also (sOrderDuration): sORDER_DURATION_IOC | sORDER_DURATION_FOK | sORDER_DURATION_DAY | sORDER_DURATION_GTC

▶ sOrderNum  (prototype)
Prototype: tsNCharcb OrderReport::sOrderNum

▶ sOrderNum  (text)
The unique number assigned to this order by the infrastructure. Note that this value is not the same as the one used by the exchange to uniquely identify the order.

▶ sOrderType  (prototype)
Prototype: tsNCharcb OrderReport::sOrderType

▶ sOrderType  (text)
String indicating whether the order is a limit order or a market order.

▶ sOrderType  (see_also)
See also (sOrderType): sORDER_TYPE_LIMIT | sORDER_TYPE_MARKET

▶ sOriginalOrderNum  (prototype)
Prototype: int OrderReport::sOriginalOrderNum

▶ sOriginalOrderNum  (text)
The order number of the order causing this order to be placed. This value can be used to correlate a bracket exit order with the entry order.

▶ sOriginalOrderType  (prototype)
Prototype: tsNCharcb OrderReport::sOriginalOrderType

▶ sOriginalOrderType  (text)
Original order type of this order. Some exchanges modify the order type based on how it changes over time (e.g. a stop limit order converting to a limit order after it is triggered). This value will contain the order type when the order was initially placed.

▶ sOriginalOrderType  (see_also)
See also (sOriginalOrderType): LineInfo::sOriginalOrderType

▶ sOriginatorWindowName  (prototype)
Prototype: tsNCharcb OrderReport::sOriginatorWindowName

▶ sOriginatorWindowName  (text)
If a window name was specified when this order was created, that value will be retained in this field.

▶ sOriginatorWindowName  (see_also)
See also (sOriginatorWindowName): OrderReport::sWindowName | LineInfo::sOriginatorWindowName | LineInfo::sWindowName

▶ sPositionDisposition  (prototype)
Prototype: tsNCharcb OrderReport::sPositionDisposition

▶ sPositionDisposition  (text)
If available, this value contains the position disposition of the order. The position disposition is the net position on this instrument, for this account taking into consideration this order. It typically has values of opening, closing, or closing then opening.

▶ sReportId  (prototype)
Prototype: tsNCharcb OrderReport::sReportId

▶ sReportId  (text)
String indicating the report identifier. It is possible for this value to be empty. If this report is an sod report, the value will be "sod".

▶ sReportType  (prototype)
Prototype: tsNCharcb OrderReport::sReportType

▶ sReportType  (text)
String indicating the report type. If this report is an sod report, the value will be "sod".

▶ sTag  (prototype)
Prototype: tsNCharcb OrderReport::sTag

▶ sTag  (text)
If a value for the tag string was passed when placing the order, it will appear in here. It can be regarded as a static user-assigned value associated with the order.

▶ sTag  (see_also)
See also (sTag): OrderParams::sTag | LimitOrderParams::sTag | MarketOrderParams::sTag

▶ sText  (prototype)
Prototype: tsNCharcb OrderReport::sText

▶ sText  (text)
Informational text that may be provided with this order report.

▶ sTicker  (prototype)
Prototype: tsNCharcb OrderReport::sTicker

▶ sTicker  (text)
The symbol of the instrument associated with this order.

▶ sTickerPlantExchOrdId  (prototype)
Prototype: tsNCharcb OrderReport::sTickerPlantExchOrdId

▶ sTickerPlantExchOrdId  (text)
When available/applicable, this contains the exchange order id as it appears in the ticker plant (or market data stream).

▶ sTickerPlantExchOrdId  (see_also)
See also (sTickerPlantExchOrdId): DboInfo::sExchOrdId | LineInfo::sTickerPlantExchOrdId

▶ sTriggerExchange  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::OrderReport::sTriggerExchange

This member variable specifies the exchange of associated with the trigger condition.

▶ sTriggerTicker  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::OrderReport::sTriggerTicker

▶ sTriggerTicker  (text)
This member variable specifies the symbol of associated with the trigger condition.

▶ sUser  (prototype)
Prototype: tsNCharcb OrderReport::sUser

▶ sUser  (text)
If available, the user who most recently operated on this order.

▶ sUserMsg  (prototype)
Prototype: tsNCharcb OrderReport::sUserMsg

▶ sUserMsg  (text)
If a value for the context string was passed when placing or operating on the order, it will appear in here.

▶ sUserMsg  (see_also)

Prototype: tsNCharcb OrderReport::sUserTag

▶ sUserTag  (see_also)

▶ sWindowName  (prototype)
Prototype: tsNCharcb OrderReport::sWindowName

▶ sWindowName  (text)
Name of window associated with this order or order request. Some applications will populate this field when submitting order requests to identify which window/widget requested the order operation.

▶ sWindowName  (see_also)
See also (sWindowName): LineInfo::sWindowName | LineInfo::sOriginatorWindowName

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::OrderStatusReport: The OrderStatusReport represents the successful placement of an order and is passed in RCallbacks::StatusReport() .
The OrderStatusReport class conveys information about the new order.
See also: REngine::modifyOrder | REngine::sendOrder | REngine::sendOrderList

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ OrderStatusReport  (prototype)
Prototype: OrderStatusReport::OrderStatusReport()

▶ OrderStatusReport  (text)
This constructor initializes all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to NULL and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to zero. Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Boolean members are set to false. Pointer values are set to NULL.

▶ ~OrderStatusReport  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: OrderStatusReport::~OrderStatusReport() [virtual]

▶ ~OrderStatusReport  [virtual]  (text)
This destructor will not deallocate memory associated with internal pointers such as the pData of any tsNCharcbs.

── Member Function Documentation ──

▶ dump  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: int OrderStatusReport::dump( int * aiCode) [virtual]

▶ dump  [virtual]  (text)
This routine will print the current values of the object to stdout. The precision of the output may not reflect the actual precision of the value. For example, doubles may only be printed to six decimal places, even if the value of the double has a greater significant precision.
It also calls the OrderReport::dump routine.
Reimplemented from RApi::OrderReport .

── Member Data Documentation ──

▶ llConfirmedSize  (prototype)
Prototype: OrderStatusReport::llConfirmedSize

▶ llConfirmedSize  (text)
Quantity that was successfully placed.

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::OrderTradeCorrectReport: The OrderTradeCorrectReport represents a trade which is under investigation and is passed in RCallbacks::TradeCorrectReport() .
The OrderTradeCorrectReport class conveys information about a possible trade correction.
See also: REngine::modifyOrder | REngine::sendOrder | REngine::sendOrderList

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ OrderTradeCorrectReport  (prototype)
Prototype: OrderTradeCorrectReport::OrderTradeCorrectReport()

▶ OrderTradeCorrectReport  (text)
This constructor initializes all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to NULL and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to zero. Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Boolean members are set to false. Pointer values are set to NULL.

▶ ~OrderTradeCorrectReport  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: OrderTradeCorrectReport::~OrderTradeCorrectReport() [virtual]

▶ ~OrderTradeCorrectReport  [virtual]  (text)
This destructor will not deallocate memory associated with internal pointers such as the pData of any tsNCharcbs.

── Member Function Documentation ──

▶ dump  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: int OrderTradeCorrectReport::dump( int * aiCode) [virtual]

▶ dump  [virtual]  (text)
This routine will print the current values of the object to stdout. The precision of the output may not reflect the actual precision of the value. For example, doubles may only be printed to six decimal places, even if the value of the double has a greater significant precision.
It also calls the OrderReport::dump routine.
Reimplemented from RApi::OrderReport .

── Member Data Documentation ──

▶ sFillType  (prototype)
Prototype: tsNCharcb OrderTradeCorrectReport::sFillType

▶ sFillType  (text)
If available, contains the fill type of this report. It is typically populated when the correction represents one leg of a spread. If this value is empty, this report pertains to a "regular" fill.

▶ sSettlementCurrency  (prototype)
Prototype: tsNCharcb OrderTradeCorrectReport::sSettlementCurrency

▶ sSettlementCurrency  (text)
If available, contains the settlement currency associated with this trade correction.

▶ sSettlementDate  (prototype)
Prototype: tsNCharcb OrderTradeCorrectReport::sSettlementDate

▶ sSettlementDate  (text)
If available, contains the settlement date associated with this trade correction.

▶ sTradeDate  (prototype)
Prototype: tsNCharcb OrderTradeCorrectReport::sTradeDate

▶ sTradeDate  (text)
If available, the trade date associated with this report.

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::OrderTriggerPulledReport: The OrderTriggerPulledReport represents the successful placement of an order by the order release manager and is passed in RCallbacks::TriggerPulledReport() .
The OrderTriggerPulledReport class conveys information about the order being placed by the order release manager. Typically, this report is only used when certain 'trigger' conditions are met, causing the placement of the order at the exchange.

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ OrderTriggerPulledReport  (prototype)
Prototype: OrderTriggerPulledReport::OrderTriggerPulledReport()

▶ OrderTriggerPulledReport  (text)
This constructor initializes all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to MNM_EMPTY_DATA_ITEM and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to MNM_EMPTY_DATA_ITEM_LEN. Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Boolean members are set to false. Pointer values are set to NULL.

▶ ~OrderTriggerPulledReport  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: OrderTriggerPulledReport::~OrderTriggerPulledReport() [virtual]

▶ ~OrderTriggerPulledReport  [virtual]  (text)
This destructor will not deallocate memory associated with internal pointers such as the pData of any tsNCharcbs.

── Member Function Documentation ──

▶ dump  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: int OrderTriggerPulledReport::dump( int * aiCode) [virtual]

▶ dump  [virtual]  (text)
This routine will print the current values of the object to stdout. The precision of the output may not reflect the actual precision of the value. For example, doubles may only be printed to six decimal places, even if the value of the double has a greater significant precision.
It also calls the OrderReport::dump routine.
Reimplemented from RApi::OrderReport .

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::PnlInfo: An instance of this class represents the pnl of an Account at the Account level or for an instrument.

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ PnlInfo  (prototype)
Prototype: PnlInfo::PnlInfo()

▶ PnlInfo  (text)
This constructor initializes all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to NULL and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to zero. Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Boolean members are set to false. Pointers (and pointers to arrays) are set to NULL.

▶ ~PnlInfo  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: PnlInfo::~PnlInfo() [virtual]

▶ ~PnlInfo  [virtual]  (text)
This destructor will not deallocate memory associated with internal pointers such as the pData of any tsNCharcbs or array elements.

── Member Function Documentation ──

▶ clearHandles  (prototype)
Prototype: int PnlInfo::clearHandles( int * aiCode)

▶ clearHandles  (text)
This routine initializes all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to NULL and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to zero. Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Boolean members are set to false. Pointer values are set to NULL.

▶ dump  (prototype)
Prototype: int PnlInfo::dump( int * aiCode)

▶ dump  (text)
This routine prints the pnl information to stdout. If a tsNCharcb has an iDataLen value of MNM_EMPTY_DATA_ITEM_LEN, then the string "<empty>" will be printed.

── Member Data Documentation ──

▶ bPossOutOfOrder  (prototype)
Prototype: bool PnlInfo::bPossOutOfOrder

▶ bPossOutOfOrder  (text)
When true, this value indicates that this PnlInfo instance may be occurring out of order. This value will be true if the PnlInfo instance has been cached, either because this is historical PnlInfo (from PnlReplayInfo ) or if the instance was cached locally while additional asynchronous processing required for the PnlInfo was performed.

▶ dAccountBalance  (prototype)
Prototype: PnlInfo::dAccountBalance

▶ dAccountBalance  (text)
This member variable represents the account balance of the account. This value is not always set, so one must check PnlInfo::eAccountBalance first.

▶ dAvailableBuyingPower  (prototype)
Prototype: double RApi::PnlInfo::dAvailableBuyingPower

▶ dAvgOpenFillPrice  (prototype)
Prototype: PnlInfo::dAvgOpenFillPrice

▶ dAvgOpenFillPrice  (text)
The average open fill price is the average fill price for an open position on an instrument. The oldest fills on the buy side are matched with the oldest fills on the sell side. If there are unmatched fills, the average fill price for these fills are put in this variable.

▶ dCashOnHand  (prototype)
Prototype: PnlInfo::dCashOnHand

▶ dCashOnHand  (text)
This member variable represents the cash on hand associated with this account.

▶ dClosedPnl  (prototype)
Prototype: PnlInfo::dClosedPnl

▶ dClosedPnl  (text)
This member variable represents the closed p/l for this instrument. If the ticker and symbol are not specified, this value represents the closed p/l for the Account.

▶ dImpliedMarginReserved  (prototype)
Prototype: PnlInfo::dImpliedMarginReserved

▶ dImpliedMarginReserved  (text)
If applicable and available, the implied margin reserved.

▶ dMarginBalance  (prototype)
Prototype: PnlInfo::dMarginBalance

▶ dMarginBalance  (text)
This member variable represents the margin balance on the account. This value is not always set, so one must check PnlInfo::eMarginBalance first.

▶ dOpenPnl  (prototype)
Prototype: PnlInfo::dOpenPnl

▶ dOpenPnl  (text)
This member variable represents the open p/l for this instrument. If the ticker and symbol are not specified, this value represents the open p/l for the Account.

▶ iSsboe  (text)

▶ iUsecs  (prototype)
Prototype: int PnlInfo::iUsecs

▶ iUsecs  (text)
Microsecond portion of the timestamp

▶ llBuyQty  (prototype)
Prototype: PnlInfo::llBuyQty

▶ llBuyQty  (text)
If PnlInfo::eBuyQty is true, this member variable contains the quantity bought contributing to the PnlInfo::llPosition . If PnlInfo::sTicker and PnlInfo::sExchange are empty, this value will reflect the quantity over all instruments.

▶ llBuyWorkingQty  (prototype)
Prototype: PnlInfo::llBuyWorkingQty

▶ llBuyWorkingQty  (text)
Sum of the quantities of working buy orders. This sum will include unfilled quantities of partially filled orders.

▶ llImpliedNetQty  (prototype)
Prototype: PnlInfo::llImpliedNetQty

▶ llImpliedNetQty  (text)
If available, the implied net quantity of the instrument. Sometimes combining outright positions can result in an implied position, such as when holding positions in a calendar spread's legs, there might be an implied position in the calendar spread itself.

▶ llLongExposure  (prototype)
Prototype: PnlInfo::llLongExposure

▶ llLongExposure  (text)
Long exposure based on buy fills, sell fills and buy working quantity.

▶ llPosition  (prototype)
Prototype: PnlInfo::llPosition

▶ llPosition  (text)
If PnlInfo::ePosition is true, this member variable contains the position. If PnlInfo::sTicker and PnlInfo::sExchange are empty, this value will reflect the position over all instruments.

▶ llSellQty  (prototype)
Prototype: PnlInfo::llSellQty

▶ llSellQty  (text)
If PnlInfo::eSellQty is true, this member variable contains the quantity sold contributing to the PnlInfo::llPosition . If PnlInfo::sTicker and PnlInfo::sExchange are empty, this value will reflect the quantity over all instruments.

▶ llSellWorkingQty  (prototype)
Prototype: PnlInfo::llSellWorkingQty

▶ llSellWorkingQty  (text)
Sum of the quantities of working sell orders. This sum will include unfilled quantities of partially filled orders.

▶ llShortExposure  (prototype)
Prototype: PnlInfo::llShortExposure

▶ llShortExposure  (text)
Short exposure based on buy fills, sell fills and sell working quantity.

▶ oAccount  (prototype)
Prototype: PnlInfo::oAccount

▶ oAccount  (text)
This member variable respresents the account associated with this pnl.

▶ sExchange  (prototype)
Prototype: PnlInfo::sExchange

▶ sExchange  (text)
This member variable contains the exchange corresponding to the pnl information. If PnlInfo::sTicker.pData is NULL and PnlInfo::sExchange.pData is also NULL, then the p/l information applies at the Account level.

▶ sTicker  (prototype)
Prototype: PnlInfo::sTicker

▶ sTicker  (text)
This member variable contains the symbol corresponding to the pnl information. If PnlInfo::sTicker.pData is NULL and PnlInfo::sExchange.pData is also NULL, then the p/l information applies at the Account level.

#include <[RApiPlus.h](RApiPlus_8h_source.html)>

── Public Attributes ──
RApi::PnlReplayInfo — Public Attributes:
PnlInfo *  asPnlInfoArray
int  iArrayLen
int  iRpCode
AccountInfo  oAccount
tsNCharcb  sRpCode

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::PnlReplayInfo: An instance of this class represents the p/l of an Account.
A PnlReplayInfo object is used to pass an array of PnlInfo instances returned via RCallbacks::PnlReplay() resulting from a call to REngine::replayPnls().

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ PnlReplayInfo  (prototype)
Prototype: PnlReplayInfo::PnlReplayInfo()

▶ PnlReplayInfo  (text)
This constructor initializes all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to MNM_EMPTY_DATA_ITEM and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to MNM_EMPTY_DATA_ITEM_LEN. Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Boolean members are set to false. Pointers (and pointers to arrays) are set to NULL.

▶ ~PnlReplayInfo  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: PnlReplayInfo::~PnlReplayInfo() [virtual]

▶ ~PnlReplayInfo  [virtual]  (text)
This destructor will not deallocate memory associated with internal pointers such as the pData of any tsNCharcbs or array elements.

── Member Function Documentation ──

▶ dump  (prototype)
Prototype: int PnlReplayInfo::dump( int * aiCode)

▶ dump  (text)
This routine prints the values of the PnlReplayInfo object to stdout. If a tsNCharcb has an iDataLen value of MNM_EMPTY_DATA_ITEM_LEN, then the string "<empty>" will be printed.

── Member Data Documentation ──

▶ asPnlInfoArray  (prototype)
Prototype: PnlReplayInfo::asPnlInfoArray

▶ asPnlInfoArray  (text)
This is an array of PnlInfo objects. The array length is specified by PnlReplayInfo::iArrayLen .

▶ iArrayLen  (prototype)
Prototype: PnlReplayInfo::iArrayLen

▶ iArrayLen  (text)
This variable specifies the array length.

▶ iRpCode  (prototype)
Prototype: PnlReplayInfo::iRpCode

▶ iRpCode  (text)
The response code, if available, associated with the request REngine::replayPnls(). A non-zero response code can be interpreted as an error.

▶ oAccount  (prototype)
Prototype: PnlReplayInfo::oAccount

▶ oAccount  (text)
This member variable respresents the account used when requesting the pnl replay.

▶ sRpCode  (prototype)
Prototype: PnlReplayInfo::sRpCode

▶ sRpCode  (text)
The is a string equivalent of the response code. It may not be populated if the value of iRpCode is zero (API_OK).

tsNCharcb  sTag
tsNCharcb  sTicker
tsNCharcb  sTradingAlgorithm
tsNCharcb  sUserMsg
tsNCharcb  sWindowName

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::PositionExitInfo: An instance of this class returns the result returned by the infrastructure of a call to REngine::exitPosition() .
The PositionExitInfo object will have the parameters passed into REngine::exitPosition() as well as the response code for the exit position request from the infrastructure.
A non-zero value of the response code iRpCode indicates some error condition.
See also: REngine::exitPosition()

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ PositionExitInfo  (prototype)
Prototype: PositionExitInfo::PositionExitInfo()

▶ PositionExitInfo  (text)
This constructor initializes all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to NULL and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to zero. Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Boolean members are set to false. Pointers (and pointers to arrays) are set to NULL.

▶ ~PositionExitInfo  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: PositionExitInfo::~PositionExitInfo() [virtual]

▶ ~PositionExitInfo  [virtual]  (text)
This destructor will not deallocate memory associated with internal pointers such as the pData of any tsNCharcbs or array elements.

── Member Function Documentation ──

── Member Data Documentation ──

▶ iRpCode  (prototype)

▶ iRpCode  (text)

tsNCharcb  sRpCode
tsNCharcb  sTicker

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::PriceIncrInfo: An instance of this class represents the minimum price increment of an instrument.
It is possible that the minimum price increment changes based on the current price of the instrument. A PriceIncrInfo object is used to pass an array of PriceIncrRow instances. Each row indicates a minimum price increment for a range of prices. If the minimum price increment is constant, there will only be one row in the array. The PriceIncrInfo for an instrument can be requested by calling REngine::getPriceIncrInfo() ;

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ PriceIncrInfo  (prototype)
Prototype: PriceIncrInfo::PriceIncrInfo()

▶ PriceIncrInfo  (text)
This constructor initializes all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to MNM_EMPTY_DATA_ITEM and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to MNM_EMPTY_DATA_ITEM_LEN. Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Boolean members are set to false. Pointers (and pointers to arrays) are set to NULL.

▶ ~PriceIncrInfo  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: PriceIncrInfo::~PriceIncrInfo() [virtual]

▶ ~PriceIncrInfo  [virtual]  (text)
This destructor will deallocate the array of PriceIncrRows.

RApi::PriceIncrRow Class Reference
#include <[RApiPlus.h](RApiPlus_8h_source.html)>
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

── Public Member Functions ──
RApi::PriceIncrRow — Public Member Functions:

PriceIncrRow ()

── Public Attributes ──
RApi::PriceIncrRow — Public Attributes:
double  dFirstPrice
double  dLastPrice
double  dPriceIncr
int  iFirstOperator
int  iLastOperator
int  iPrecision

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::PriceIncrRow: An instance of this class represents the minimum price increment for a price range.
A PriceIncrRow object is used to indicate a minimum price increment for a range of prices. The range is defined by an upper and/or lower bound on the price. A value of OPER_NONE for an operator means that there is no constraint. When the operator is OPER_NONE, the corresponding price is not valid and should not be examined.
Therefore, if both operators are OPER_NONE, there are no constraints on the price range, and the minimum price increment is constant for all prices. For example, if the minimum price increment is 0.25 for all prices, the instance will have the following values :
• PriceIncrRow::dPriceIncr = 0.25
• PriceIncrRow::iPrecision = 2
• PriceIncrRow::iFirstOperator = RApi::OP_NONE
• PriceIncrRow::iLastOperator = RApi::OP_NONE
If one operator is OPER_NONE and the other is not, there is a single constraint. For example, if the minimum price increment is 0.10 when the price of the instrument falls below -3.00, then the instance will have the followin gvalues :
• PriceIncrRow::dPriceIncr = 0.10
• PriceIncrRow::iPrecision = 2
• PriceIncrRow::iFirstOperator = RApi::OP_LESS_THAN
• PriceIncrRow::dFirstPrice = -3.00
• PriceIncrRow::iLastOperator = RApi::OP_NONE
Another example is if the minimum price increment is 0.10 when the price of the instrument is above 3.00, the instance may have the following values :
• PriceIncrRow::dPriceIncr = 0.10
• PriceIncrRow::iPrecision = 2
• PriceIncrRow::iFirstOperator = RApi::OP_NONE
• PriceIncrRow::iLastOperator = RApi::OP_GREATER_THAN
• PriceIncrRow::dLastPrice = 3.0
Finally, if both operators are not OPER_NONE, then there are two constraints that apply to the current price increment. For example, if the minimum price increment is 0.05 when the price is greater or equal to -3.00 and less than or equal to 3.00, the instance may have the following values :
• PriceIncrRow::dPriceIncr = 0.05
• PriceIncrRow::iPrecision = 2
• PriceIncrRow::iFirstOperator = RApi::OP_GREATER_THAN_OR_EQUAL
• PriceIncrRow::dFirstPrice = -3.00
• PriceIncrRow::iLastOperator = RApi::OP_LESS_THAN_OR_EQUAL
• PriceIncrRow::dLastPrice = 3.00

── Public Attributes ──

bool  bPriceFlag
double  dPrice
int  iSsboe
int  iType
int  iUsecs
tsNCharcb  sExchange
tsNCharcb  sTicker

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::ProjectedSettlementPriceInfo: This class represents projected settlement price information.
The projected settlement price is what the expected settlment price might be for an instrument. The calculation of this price is based on the settlement procedure as documented by an exchange.
For example, some exchanges might base the settlement price on the VWAP for some time period (settlement period) leading up to the trading session close.
This value is not available for all instruments.
To subsribe for this data, the RApi::MD_PROJECTED_SETTLEMENT flag must be enabled when calling REngine::subscribe() or REngine::subscribeByUnderlying() .
The callback used to convey projected settlement prices is RCallbacks::ProjectedSettlementPrice() .

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ ProjectedSettlementPriceInfo  (prototype)
Prototype: ProjectedSettlementPriceInfo::ProjectedSettlementPriceInfo()

▶ ProjectedSettlementPriceInfo  (text)
This constructor initializes all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to NULL and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to zero. Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Boolean members are set to false.

▶ iSsboe  (prototype)
Prototype: ProjectedSettlementPriceInfo::iSsboe

▶ iSsboe  (text)
Timestamp in seconds since the beginning of epoch (Jan. 1, 1970). If the callback type is MD_UPDATE_CB, this timestamp indicates the receipt time of this message from the market data provider.
If the image type is MD_IMAGE_CB, the timestamp indicates that the data is current "as of" that time.

Optionally specifies a new user message to associate with this quote. If this value is empty (pUserMsg -> pData is NULL and pUserMsg -> iDataLen is zero), then any existing user message ( QuoteParams::sUserMsg ) will remain associated with this quote.

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::QuoteInfo: This class represents information about quotes.
The QuoteInfo class is used to pass quote information (including various states of the quote) via RCallbacks::Quote .
See also: REngine::submitQuoteList | REngine::cancelQuoteList | RCallbacks::Quote

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ QuoteInfo  (prototype)
Prototype: QuoteInfo::QuoteInfo()

▶ QuoteInfo  (text)
This constructor initializes all of the members using QuoteInfo::clearHandles() .

▶ ~QuoteInfo  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: QuoteInfo::~QuoteInfo() [virtual]

▶ ~QuoteInfo  [virtual]  (text)
This destructor will not deallocate memory associated with internal pointers such as the pData of any tsNCharcbs.

── Member Function Documentation ──

▶ clearHandles  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: int QuoteInfo::clearHandles( int * aiCode) [virtual]

▶ clearHandles  [virtual]  (text)
This routine clears all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to NULL and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to zero. Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Boolean members are set to false. Pointer values are set to NULL. This method is used internally by the constructor.

▶ dump  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: int QuoteInfo::dump( int * aiCode) [virtual]

▶ dump  [virtual]  (text)
This routine will print the current values of the object to stdout. The precision of the output may not reflect the actual precision of the value. For example, doubles may only be printed to six decimal places, even if the value of the double has a greater significant precision.

── Member Data Documentation ──

▶ bAsk  (prototype)
Prototype: bool QuoteInfo::bAsk

▶ bAsk  (text)
Indicates whether the dAskPriceToFill and llAskQuantityToFill contain values. It may be possible to receive updates on a quote which do not specify (i.e. reiterate) the bid/ask prices and sizes. In these cases, this flag may be false even though the quote is open.

▶ bBid  (prototype)
Prototype: bool QuoteInfo::bBid

▶ bBid  (text)
Indicates whether the dBidPriceToFill and llBidQuantityToFill contain values. It may be possible to receive updates on a quote which do not specify (i.e. reiterate) the bid/ask prices and sizes. In these cases, this flag may be false even though the quote is open.

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::QuoteParams: This class encapsulates information needed to submit a quote for a single instrument.
Quotes must be placed on the ask/offer side, and can optionally be placed on the bid side.
See also: REngine::submitQuoteList

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ ~QuoteParams  [virtual]  (prototype)

▶ ~QuoteParams  [virtual]  (text)
This destructor will not deallocate memory associated with internal pointers such as the pData of any tsNCharcbs.

── Member Function Documentation ──

Boolean members are set to false. Pointer values are set to NULL. (Internally used by the constructor.)

── Member Data Documentation ──

▶ bBid  (prototype)

▶ bBid  (text)
Indicates whether this quote has a bid component.

▶ dAskPrice  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::QuoteParams::dAskPrice

▶ llBidQty  (prototype)

▶ pAccount  (prototype)

▶ pAccount  (text)
Account on which to place the quote.

▶ sExchange  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::QuoteParams::sExchange

▶ sExchange  (text)
Exchange of the instrument on which to place the quote.

▶ sTicker  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::QuoteParams::sTicker

▶ sTicker  (text)
Symbol of the instrument on which to place the quote.

▶ sUserMsg  (prototype)
Prototype: RApi::QuoteParams::sUserMsg

▶ sUserMsg  (text)
This optional value specifies a user message that is persisted with the resultant quote by the infrastructure. If specified, this value will be present on subsequent quote updates published by the infrastructure.
If this value is empty ( tsNCharcb::pData is NULL and tsNCharcb::iDataLen is zero), no user msg will be set.
Note : The value can be updated with any subsequent operation on the quote, including other instances of R | API and R | Trader.

▶ sUserMsg  (see_also)
See also (sUserMsg): QuoteInfo::sUserMsg | QuoteReport::sUserMsg | QuoteCancelParams::sUserMsg | REngine::submitQuoteList() | REngine::cancelQuoteList()

── Public Attributes ──
RApi::QuoteReplayInfo — Public Attributes:
int  iRpCode
AccountInfo  oAccount
void *  pContext
tsNCharcb  sRpCode

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::QuoteReplayInfo: This class demarcates the end of a stream of historical quote data.
The QuoteReplayInfo class is used to convey the conclusion and/or response code associated with a call to REngine::replayQuotes() via RCallbacks::QuoteReplay() .
Information about the actual quotes will be conveyed via RCallbacks::Quote() , with the QuoteInfo instance's callback type set to indicate historical data.

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ QuoteReplayInfo  (prototype)
Prototype: QuoteReplayInfo::QuoteReplayInfo()

▶ QuoteReplayInfo  (text)
This constructor initializes all of the members. The pData of each tsNCharcb is set to NULL and the iDataLen of each tsNCharcb is set to zero. Integer members and double members are initialized to zero. Boolean members are set to false. Pointer values are set to NULL.

▶ ~QuoteReplayInfo  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: QuoteReplayInfo::~QuoteReplayInfo() [virtual]

▶ ~QuoteReplayInfo  [virtual]  (text)
This destructor will not deallocate memory associated with internal pointers such as the pData of any tsNCharcbs.

── Member Function Documentation ──

▶ dump  (prototype)
Prototype: int QuoteReplayInfo::dump( int * aiCode)

▶ dump  (text)
This routine will print the current values of the object to stdout. The precision of the output may not reflect the actual precision of the value. For example, doubles may only be printed to six decimal places, even if the value of the double has a greater significant precision.

── Member Data Documentation ──

▶ iRpCode  (prototype)
Prototype: QuoteReplayInfo::iRpCode

▶ iRpCode  (text)
Response code associated with the quote replay request. A non-zero response code can be interpreted as an error.

▶ oAccount  (prototype)
Prototype: QuoteReplayInfo::oAccount

▶ oAccount  (text)
Account used when requesting the quote replay.

▶ pContext  (prototype)
Prototype: QuoteReplayInfo::pContext

▶ pContext  (text)
Context pointer passed in when calling REngine::replayQuotes() .

▶ sRpCode  (prototype)
Prototype: QuoteReplayInfo::sRpCode

▶ sRpCode  (text)
The string equivalent of the response code QuoteReplayInfo::iRpCode . If QuoteReplayInfo::iRpCode equals API_OK, this string may be empty.

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::QuoteReport: The QuoteReport represents an update to a quote related request.
To receive quote reports, one must subscribe to quotes. Upon receipt of an update, the quote report callback will be invoked, passing an instance of QuoteReport , conveying the update.
See also: REngine::subscribeOrder() | REngine::submitQuoteList() | REngine::cancelQuoteList() | RCallbacks::Quote()

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ sUser  (prototype)
Prototype: tsNCharcb QuoteReport::sUser

▶ sUser  (text)
If available, the user who most recently operated on this quote.

▶ sUserMsg  (prototype)
Prototype: tsNCharcb QuoteReport::sUserMsg

▶ sUserMsg  (text)
If a value for the context string was passed when placing or operating on the quote, it will appear in here.

▶ sUserMsg  (see_also)
See also (sUserMsg): QuoteParams::sUserMsg | QuoteCancelParams::sUserMsg

── Detailed Description ──
Class RApi::RCallbacks: This class contains all the user-defined callbacks for RApi .
A subclass of RCallbacks must be defined, instantiated and passed via LoginParams::pCallbacks when logging into the infrastructure. While the session is active, the instance referenced by LoginParams::pCallbacks must not be destroyed.

── Constructor & Destructor Documentation ──

▶ ~RCallbacks  [virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: RCallbacks::~RCallbacks() [virtual]

── Member Function Documentation ──

▶ AccountList  [inline, virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: int RCallbacks::AccountList( AccountListInfo * pInfo , void * pContext , int * aiCode ) [inline, virtual]

▶ AccountList  [inline, virtual]  (text)
This method is invoked when the engine receives a list of accounts associated with the current login id.

▶ AccountList  [inline, virtual]  (params)
Parameters of AccountList:
  • pInfo: the AccountListInfo object that contains an array of AccountInfo objects.
  • pContext: the global context pointer provided by either REngineParams::pContext or set via REngine::setContext . If this value has not been set, its value will be NULL.
  • aiCode: the error code set by the method if it failed.
Returns: OK if the method was successful, NOT_OK otherwise. In the latter case aiCode is populated with the error code.

▶ AccountList  [inline, virtual]  (see_also)
See also (AccountList): AccountInfo

▶ AccountUpdate  [inline, virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: int RCallbacks::AccountUpdate( AccountUpdateInfo * pInfo , void * pContext , int * aiCode ) [inline, virtual]

▶ AccountUpdate  [inline, virtual]  (text)
This method is invoked when the engine receives an update to an account associated with the current login id. A subscription is not necessary, but one must be currently logged in to the Trading System connection.

▶ AccountUpdate  [inline, virtual]  (params)
Parameters of AccountUpdate:
  • pInfo: the AccountUpdateInfo object that contains the update information
  • pContext: the global context pointer provided by either REngineParams::pContext or set via REngine::setContext . If this value has not been set, its value will be NULL.
  • aiCode: the error code set by the method if it failed.
Returns: OK if the method was successful, NOT_OK otherwise. In the latter case aiCode is populated with the error code.

▶ AccountUpdate  [inline, virtual]  (see_also)
See also (AccountUpdate): AccountUpdateInfo | LoginParams::sTsCnnctPt

▶ Aggregator  [inline, virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: int RCallbacks::Aggregator( AggregatorInfo * pInfo , void * pContext , int * aiCode ) [inline, virtual]

▶ Aggregator  [inline, virtual]  (text)
This method is invoked when the engine receives a a response to REngine::isThereAnAggregator() query.

▶ Aggregator  [inline, virtual]  (params)
Parameters of Aggregator:
  • pInfo: AggregatorInfo object conveying the infrastruture's response
  • pContext: the global context pointer provided by either REngineParams::pContext or set via REngine::setContext . If this value has not been set, its value will be NULL.
  • aiCode: the error code set by the method if it failed.
Returns: OK if the method was successful, NOT_OK otherwise. In the latter case aiCode is populated with the error code.

▶ Aggregator  [inline, virtual]  (see_also)
See also (Aggregator): REngine::isThereAnAggregator() | AggregatorInfo

▶ AgreementList  [inline, virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: virtual int RApi::RCallbacks::AgreementList( AgreementListInfo * pInfo , void * pContext , int * aiCode ) [inline, virtual]

▶ Alert  [inline, virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: int RCallbacks::Alert( AlertInfo * pInfo , void * pContext , int * aiCode ) [inline, virtual]

▶ Alert  [inline, virtual]  (text)
This method is invoked when the engine detects an alert condition.

▶ Alert  [inline, virtual]  (params)
Parameters of Alert:
  • pInfo: the AlertInfo object that contains information about the alert.
  • pContext: the global context pointer provided by either REngineParams::pContext or set via REngine::setContext . If this value has not been set, its value will be NULL.
  • aiCode: the error code set by the method if it failed.
Returns: OK if the method was successful, NOT_OK otherwise. In the latter case aiCode is populated with the error code.

▶ Alert  [inline, virtual]  (see_also)
See also (Alert): AlertInfo

▶ AskQuote  [inline, virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: int RCallbacks::AskQuote( AskInfo * pInfo , void * pContext , int * aiCode ) [inline, virtual]

▶ AskQuote  [inline, virtual]  (text)
This method is invoked when the engine receives an ask quote from the market data feed.

▶ AskQuote  [inline, virtual]  (params)
Parameters of AskQuote:
  • pInfo: the AskInfo object representing the quote.
  • pContext: the global context pointer provided by either REngineParams::pContext or set via REngine::setContext . If this value has not been set, its value will be NULL.
  • aiCode: the error code set by the user-defined method if it failed.
Returns: OK if the method was successful, NOT_OK otherwise. In the latter case aiCode is populated with the error code.

▶ AskQuote  [inline, virtual]  (see_also)
See also (AskQuote): AskInfo

▶ AssignedUserList  [inline, virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: int RCallbacks::AssignedUserList( AssignedUserListInfo * pInfo , void * pContext , int * aiCode ) [inline, virtual]

▶ AssignedUserList  [inline, virtual]  (text)
This method is invoked when the engine receives a response from the infrastructure to REngine::listAssignedUsers() .

▶ AssignedUserList  [inline, virtual]  (params)
Parameters of AssignedUserList:
  • pInfo: AssignedUserListInfo object representing the response.
  • pContext: global context pointer provided by either REngineParams::pContext or set via REngine::setContext . If this value has not been set, its value will be NULL.
  • aiCode: error code to be set by the user-defined method in case of failure.
Returns: OK if the method was successful, NOT_OK otherwise. In the latter case aiCode is populated with the error code.

▶ AssignedUserList  [inline, virtual]  (see_also)
See also (AssignedUserList): REngine::listAssignedUsers() | AssignedUserListInfo

▶ AutoLiquidate  [inline, virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: int RCallbacks::AutoLiquidate( AutoLiquidateInfo * pInfo , void * pContext , int * aiCode ) [inline, virtual]

▶ AutoLiquidate  [inline, virtual]  (text)
This method is invoked when the engine receives an update to the auto-liquidate threshold (current) value.

▶ AutoLiquidate  [inline, virtual]  (params)
Parameters of AutoLiquidate:
  • pInfo: AutoLiquidateInfo instance conveying most recent values.
  • pContext: the global context pointer provided by either REngineParams::pContext or set via REngine::setContext . If this value has not been set, its value will be NULL.
  • aiCode: the error code set by the method if it failed.
Returns: OK if the method was successful, NOT_OK otherwise. In the latter case aiCode is populated with the error code.

▶ AutoLiquidate  [inline, virtual]  (see_also)
See also (AutoLiquidate): AutoLiquidateInfo | REngine::subscribeAutoLiquidate() | REngine::unsubscribeAutoLiquidate()

▶ DboBookRebuild  [inline, virtual]  (text)
This method marks the end of the stream of dbo orders resulting from a call to REngine::rebuildDboBook() . The status (response code) of the request will be DboBookRebuildInfo::iRpCode .
The actual dbo data will be returned via RCallbacks::Dbo() .

▶ DboBookRebuild  [inline, virtual]  (params)

▶ FillReport  [inline, virtual]  (text)
This method is invoked when the engine receives a fill report from the infrastructure. The fill report does not provide cumulative fill information for the order, but fill information pertaining to a single fill event. RCallbacks::LineUpdate will be invoked to provide cumulative fill information.

▶ IbList  [inline, virtual]  (text)

▶ IbList  [inline, virtual]  (params)

▶ LimitOrderBook  [inline, virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: int RCallbacks::LimitOrderBook( LimitOrderBookInfo * pInfo , void * pContext , int * aiCode ) [inline, virtual]

▶ LimitOrderBook  [inline, virtual]  (text)
This method is invoked when the engine receives a limit order book. The limit order book may be the result of a subscription to MD_QUOTES or a call to REngine::rebuildBook .
If the limit order book is due to a subscription to MD_QUOTES, the callback represents a snapshot of the order book against which subsequent quotes can be applied. In other words, the RCallbacks::LimitOrderBook method provides an initial order book state, and RCallbacks::AskQuote and RCallbacks::BidQuote provide updates to that initial book.
It is also possible to receive an order book update which clears out one or both sides of the order book.

▶ LimitOrderBook  [inline, virtual]  (params)
Parameters of LimitOrderBook:
  • pInfo: the LimitOrderBookInfo object that contains information about the order book.
  • pContext: the global context pointer provided by either REngineParams::pContext or set via REngine::setContext . If this value has not been set, its value will be NULL.
  • aiCode: the error code set by the method if it failed.
Returns: OK if the method was successful, NOT_OK otherwise. In the latter case aiCode is populated with the error code.

▶ LimitOrderBook  [inline, virtual]  (see_also)
See also (LimitOrderBook): LimitOrderBookInfo | REngine::rebuildBook | REngine::subscribe | RApi::MD_QUOTES

▶ Quote() [1/2]  [inline, virtual]  (text)
This method is invoked when the a quote message is received from the infrastructure.

▶ Quote() [1/2]  [inline, virtual]  (params)
Parameters of Quote() [1/2]:
  • pInfo: the QuoteInfo object that representing the quote

▶ TradeReplay  [inline, virtual]  (prototype)
Prototype: int RCallbacks::TradeReplay( TradeReplayInfo * pInfo , void * pContext , int * aiCode ) [inline, virtual]

▶ TradeReplay  [inline, virtual]  (text)
This method marks the end of the stream of historical trade data. If an error is encountered, or if there were no trades, this will be the first callback invoked as a result of a prior call to REngine::replayTrades() . If no error is encountered, a number of RCallbacks::TradePrint() invocations will occur prior to this callback.

▶ TradeReplay  [inline, virtual]  (params)
Parameters of TradeReplay:

  • pContext: the global context pointer provided by either REngineParams::pContext or set via REngine::setContext . If this value has not been set, its value will be NULL.
  • aiCode: the error code set by the user-defined method if it failed.
Returns: OK if the method was successful, NOT_OK otherwise. In the latter case aiCode is populated with the error code.
